segments.transliteration: wa musakhkhira alssahabi wa mujriya alfulki.

translation.en: And He Who causes the clouds to be obedient (to Him) and He Who causes ships to move,

segments.transliteration: wa ja`ila alshshamsi diya‘an wal-qamari nuran,

translation.en: And He Who appoints the sun a splendor and the moon a light,
And He Who is the Creator of Adam, peace be upon him,

segments.arabic: وَمُنْشِئَ الأنْبِياءِ عَلَيْهِمُ السَّلامُ مِنْ ذُرِّيَّتِهِ،
segments.transliteration: wa munsh’ia alanbiya‘i `alayhimu alssalamu min dhurriyatihi,

translation.en: And the Maker of the Propehts from his offspring—peace be upon them,

segments.arabic: وَمُعَلِّمَ إدْريسَ عَدَدَ النُّجومِ وَالحِسابَ وَالسِّنينَ وَالشُّهورَ وَأوْقاتِ الأزْمانِ،
segments.transliteration: wa mu`allima idrisa `adada alnnujumi wal-hisaba wal-ssinina wal-shshuhura wa awqati al-azmani,

translation.en: And He Who taught Idris to calculate the stars, and taught him the reckoning and the number of years and months and the time;
And He Who spoke to Moses
And caused his stick to be serpent,

segments.arabic: وَمُنْزِلَ التَّوْراةِ في الألْواحِ عَلى موسى عَلَيْهِ السَّلامُ.
segments.transliteration: wa munzila alttawrati fy al-alwahi `ala musa `alayhi alssalamu.

translation.en: And revealed the Torah in the tablets to Moses, peace be upon him;
And He Who caused the ark of Noah to run,
And He Who ransomed Ishmael from slaughtering,
And He Who tried Jacob by the loss of Joseph

segments.arabic: وَرادَّ يوسُفَ عَلَيْهِ بَعْدَ أنِ ابْيَضَّتْ عَيناهُ مِنَ البُكاءِ،
segments.transliteration: wa radda yusufa `alayhi ba`da ani abyaddat `aynahu mina albuka‘i,

translation.en: And He Who restored Joseph to him (i.e. his father) after his eyes had become white, out of heavy weeping.
Hence, his heart got rid of sadness and sorrow;

segments.arabic: وَرازِقَ زَكَرِيّا يَحْيى عَلى الكِبَرِ بَعْدَ الإياسِ
segments.transliteration: wa raziqa zakariyya yahya `ala alkibari ba`da aliyasi

translation.en: And He Who granted Zachariah, in his old age, John as son after he had despaired of so;
And He Who sent forth the she-camel to (Prophet) Salih;
And He Who sent forth the awful cry on those who plotted against (Propeht) Hud,
And He Who relieved Job from his adversity,

segments.transliteration: wa munjy luwtin mina alqawmi alfahishina.

translation.en: And He Who saved (Prophet) Lot from the committers of abomination;
And He Who bestowed upon Luqman with wisdom;

segments.arabic: وَمُلقي رُوحِ القُدُسِ بِكَلِماتِهِ عَلى مَريَمَ عَلَيْها السَّلامُ،
segments.transliteration: wa mulqy ruwhi alqudusi bikalimatihi `ala maryama `alayha alssalamu,

translation.en: And He Who ordered the Holy Spirit to convey His Words to Mary, peace be upon her,

segments.arabic: وَخَلَقْتَ مِنْها عَبْدَكَ عيسى عَلَيْهِ السَّلامُ،

translation.en: And He created from her His servant, Jesus, peace be upon him,

segments.arabic: وَالمُنْتَقِمَ مِنْ قَتَلَةِ يَحْيى بْنِ زَكَرِيّا عَلَيْهِما السَّلامُ،
segments.transliteration: wal-muntaqima min qatalati yahya bni zakariyya `alayhima alssalamu,

translation.en: And He Who punished the slayers of Joan, son of Zachariah, peace be upon both of them;
I beseech You by Your taking Jesus up to Your heavens,

segments.arabic: وَبِإبْقائِكَ لَهُ إلى أنْ تَنْتَقِمَ لَهُ مِنْ أعْدائِكَ.
segments.transliteration: wa b’ibqa’ika lahu ila an tantaqima lahu min a`da’ika.

translation.en: And by Your keeping him alive until You decide to retaliate upon Your enemies for him.

segments.arabic: وَيا مُرْسِلَ مُحَمَّدٍ صَلّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ خاتِمِ أنْبِيائِكَ
segments.transliteration: wa ya mursila muhammadin salla allahu `alayhi wa alihi khatimi anbiya’ika

translation.en: O He Who sent Muhammad—peace of Allah be upon him and his Household—the seal of Your Prophets,
To the most evil of Your slaves, carrying to them Your excellent commandments,
And Your straight religion,
Which is extension of the religion of Your intimate friend, Abraham, peace be upon him,
And (You sent him for) making His straight religion prevail over all religions,
And making His Word be the highest.
O the Lord of majesty and honor;

segments.arabic: يا مَنْ لا تَأخُذُهُ سِنَةٌ وَلا نَوْمٌ،
segments.transliteration: ya man la takhudhuhu sinatun wa la nawmun,

translation.en: O He Whom is not overtaken by sleep or slumber;
O the One and Only; O the Besought of all;

segments.arabic: يا عَزيزُ يا قادِرُ يا قاهِرُ،

translation.en: O the Almighty; O the Able; O the Omnipotent;
O the Owner of power and authority
And supremacy and grandeur;
O the Most High; O the All-powerful;
O the Nigh; O the Responding;
O the Forebearing; O the Reproducer (of creation);
O the Approaching; O the Far-off;
O the All-kind; O the All-merciful;
O the All-generous; O the All-forgiving;
O the Owner of excuse;
O the Aide; O the provider of food;
O the Healer; O the Rescuer;
O the Provider with dress; O the Curer;
O the Alleviator of harms;

segments.arabic: يا عَليمُ يا حَكيمُ يا وَدودُ.

translation.en: O the All-knowing; O the All-wise; O the All-loving;
O the All-forgiver; O the All-merciful:
O the Beneficent of this world and the Hereafter;

segments.arabic: يا ذا الْمَعارِجِ يا ذا القُدْسِ،
segments.transliteration: ya dha alma`ariji ya dha alqudsi,

translation.en: O the Lord of the Ascending Stairways; O the Lord of sanctity;
O the Creator; O the All-knowing;
O the Reliever; O the Turning with mercy;
O the Lord of bounty; O the All-aware;

segments.transliteration: ya man khalaqa wa lam yukhlaq

translation.en: O He Who created, but He was not created;

segments.arabic: يا مَنْ لَمْ يَلِدْ وَلَمْ يُولَدْ،
segments.transliteration: ya man lam yalid wa lam yulad,

translation.en: O He Who never begets nor was he begotten;
O He Who is different from all things;

segments.arabic: وَبانَتِ الأشْياءُ مِنْهُ بِقَهْرِهِ لَها وَخُضوعِها لَهُ،
segments.transliteration: wa banati alashia‘u minhu biqahrihi laha wa khudu`iha lahu,

translation.en: And all things are different from Him out of His prevelance over them and their submission before Him;

segments.arabic: يا مَنْ خَلَقَ البِحارَ وَأجْرى الأنْهارَ
segments.transliteration: ya man khalaqa albihara wa ajra alanhara

translation.en: O He Who created the oceans and caused the rivers to flow;

segments.transliteration: wa anbata alashjara wa akhraja minha alnnara,

translation.en: And He caused the trees to grow and He extracted fire from them

segments.arabic: وَمِنْ يابِسِ الأرَضينَ النَّباتَ وَالأعْنابَ وَسائِرَ الثِّمارِ.
segments.transliteration: wa min yabisi alaradina alnnabata wal-a`naba wa sa’ira alththimari.

translation.en: And extracted plants and rapes and the other fruits from the dry lands.

segments.arabic: يا فالِقَ البَحرِ لِعَبْدِهِ موسى عَلَيْهِ السَّلامُ وَمُكَلِّمَهُ،
segments.transliteration: ya faliqa albahri li`abdihi musa `alayhi alssalamu wa mukallimahu,

translation.en: O He Who set apart the river for His servant, Moses, peace be upon him, and He Who spoke to him,
And He Who drowned Pharaoh and faction
And He Who destroyed Nimrud and his groups

segments.arabic: وَمُلَيِّنَ الحَديدِ لِخَليفَتِهِ داوودَ عَلَيْهِ السَّلامُ،
segments.transliteration: wa mulayyina alhadidi likhalifatihi dawuda `alayhi alssalamu,

translation.en: And He Who made the iron pliant for His vicegerent, David, peace be upon him,

segments.arabic: وَمُسَخِّرَ الجِبالِ مَعَهُ يُسَبِّحْنَ بِالغُدُوِّ وَالآصالِ،
segments.transliteration: wa musakhkhira aljibali ma`ahu yusabbihna bialghuduwwi wal-asali,

translation.en: And He Who subdued the hills to hymn the praises (of their Lord) with him at nightfall and sunrise;

segments.arabic: وَمُسَخِّرَ الطَّيْرِ وَالهَوامِّ وَالرِّياحِ وَالجِنِّ وَالإنْسِ لِعَبْدِكَ سُلَيْمانَ عَلَيْهِ السَّلامُ
segments.transliteration: wa musakhkhira alttayri wal-hawammi wal-rriyahi wal-jinni wal-insi li`abdika sulaymana `alayhi alssalamu

translation.en: And He Who subdued the birds and the verments and the winds and the jinn and the humankind for His servant, Solomon, peace be upon him;

segments.arabic: وأسْألُكَ بِاسْمِكَ الَّذي اهْتَزَّ لَهُ عَرْشُكَ
segments.transliteration: wa as-aluka bismika alladhy ahtazza lahu `arshuka

translation.en: I beseech You by Your Name to which Your Throen shook
And by which Your angels were delighted;
So, there is no god save You;

segments.arabic: خالِقُ النَّسَمَةِ وَبارِئُ النَّوى وَفالِقُ الحَبَّةِ،
segments.transliteration: khaliqu alnnasamati wa bar’iu alnnawa wa faliqu alhabbati,

translation.en: The Creator of souls and the Maker of seeds and the Splitter of grains;

segments.transliteration: wa bismika al`azizi aljalili alkabiri almuta`ali.

translation.en: And by Your Name: the Almighty, the Lofty, the Great, the high Exalted;

segments.arabic: وَبِاسْمِكَ الَّذي يَنْفُخُ بِهِ عَبْدُكَ وَمَلَكُكَ إسْرافيلُ عَلَيْهِ السَّلامُ في الصّورِ،
segments.transliteration: wa bismika alladhy yanfukhu bihi `abduka wa malakuka israfilu `alayhi alssalamu fy alssuri,

translation.en: And by Your Name by which Your servant and angel, Michael, peace be upon him, shall blow the Trumpet,

segments.arabic: فَيَقومُ أهْلُ القُبورِ سِراعاً إلى الْمَحْشَرِ يَنْسِلونَ،
segments.transliteration: fayaqumu ahlu alquburi sira`an ila almahshari yansiluna,

translation.en: And thus the inhabitants of graves shall stand up, hasting to the place of resurrection;

segments.arabic: وَبِاسْمِكَ الَّذي رَفَعْتَ بِهِ السَّماواتِ مِنْ غَيْرِ عِمادٍ
segments.transliteration: wa bismika alladhy rafa`ta bihi alssamawati min ghayri `imadin

translation.en: And by Your Name by which You uplifted the heavens without supports
And by which You made bulwarks for the earth;

segments.arabic: وَبِاسْمِكَ الَّذي سَطَحْتَ بِهِ الأرَضينَ فَوْقَ الماءِ الْمَحْبوسِ،
segments.transliteration: wa bismika alladhy satahta bihi alaradina fawqa alma‘i almahbusi,

translation.en: And by Your Name by which You surfaced the earth on the repressed water;

segments.arabic: وَبِاسْمِكَ الَّذي حَبَسْتَ بِهِ ذلِكَ الماءَ
segments.transliteration: wa bismika alladhy habasta bihi dhlika alma‘a

translation.en: And by Your Name by which You repressed that water;

segments.arabic: وَبِاسْمِكَ الَّذي حَمَلْتَ بِهِ الأرَضينَ مَنِ اخْتَرْتَهُ لِحَمْلِها،
segments.transliteration: wa bismika alladhy hamalta bihi alaradina mani akhtartahu lihamliha,

translation.en: And by Your Name by which You caused him whom You chose for undertaking the earth to undertake it

segments.arabic: وَجَعَلْتَ لَهُ مِنَ القُوَّةِ ما اسْتَعانَ بِهِ عَلى حَمْلِها.
segments.transliteration: wa ja`alta lahu mina alquwwati ma asta`ana bihi `ala hamliha.

translation.en: And You supplied him with the power that helped him undertake it;

segments.arabic: وَبِاسْمِكَ الَّذي تَجْري بِهِ الشَّمْسُ وَالقَمَرُ،
segments.transliteration: wa bismika alladhy tajry bihi alshshamsu wal-qamaru,

translation.en: And by Your Name by which the sun and the moon move;

segments.arabic: وَبِاسْمِكَ الَّذي سَلَخْتَ بِهِ النَّهارَ مِنَ اللَيْلِ،
segments.transliteration: wa bismika alladhy salakhta bihi alnnahara mina allayli,

translation.en: And by Your Name by which You strip the day of the night;

segments.arabic: وَبِاسْمِكَ الَّذي إذا دُعيتَ بِهِ أنْزَلْتَ أرْزاقَ العِبادِ
segments.transliteration: wa bismika alladhy idha du`ita bihi anzalta arzaqa al`ibadi

translation.en: And by Your Name; if You are prayed by it, You bring down the provisions of the servants
And all Your creatures
And Your lands and Your oceans
And the inhabitants of the oceans and the verments
And the jinn and the humankind

segments.transliteration: wa kulli dabbatin anta akhidhun binasiyatiha

translation.en: And every creeping creature that You grasp by the forelock!
You verily have power over all things.

segments.arabic: وَبِاسْمِكَ الَّذي جَعَلْتَ بِهِ لِجَعْفَرَ عَلَيْهِ السَّلامُ جَناحاً يَطيرُ بِهِ مَعَ الْمَلائِكَةِ،
segments.transliteration: wa bismika alladhy ja`alta bihi lija`fara `alayhi alssalamu janahan yatiru bihi ma`a almala’ikati,

translation.en: And by Your Name by which You made a wing for Ja`far, peace be upon him, by which he flies with the angels;

segments.arabic: وَبِاسْمِكَ الَّذي دَعاكَ بِهِ يُونُسُ عَلَيْهِ السَّلامُ في بَطْنِ الحوتِ فَأخْرَجْتَهُ مِنْهُ،
segments.transliteration: wa bismika alladhy da`aka bihi yuwnusu `alayhi alssalamu fy batni alhuti fakhrajtahu minhu,

translation.en: And by Your Name by which Jonah—peace be upon him—prayed You from the interior of the whale; and You thus took him out of there;

segments.arabic: وَبِاسْمِكَ الَّذي أنْبَتَّ بِهِ عَلَيْهِ شَجَرَةً مِنْ يَقْطينٍ،
segments.transliteration: wa bismika alladhy anbatta bihi `alayhi shajaratan min yaqtinin,

translation.en: And by Your Name by which You caused a tree of gourd to grow above him;

segments.arabic: فَاسْتَجَبْتَ لَهُ وَكَشَفْتَ عَنْهُ ما كانَ فيهِ مَنْ ضيقِ بَطْنِ الحوتِ.
segments.transliteration: fastajabta lahu wa kashafta `anhu ma kana fihi man diqi batni alhuti.

translation.en: Hence, You responded to him and relieved from the distress that he had experienced in the interior of the whale;

segments.arabic: أسْألُكَ أنْ تُصَلِّيَ عَلى مُحَمَّدٍ عَبْدِكَ وَرَسولِكَ وَعَلى آلِهِ الطَّيِّبينَ،
segments.transliteration: as-aluka an tusalliya `ala muhammadin `abdika wa rasulika wa `ala alihi alttayyibina,

translation.en: I beseech You to send blessings upon Muhammad, Your servant and Your Messenger, and upon his Household, the Immaculate,

segments.arabic: وَأنْ تُفَرِّجَ عَنّي وَتَكْشِفَ ضُرّي وَتَسْتَنْقِذَني مِنْ وَرطَتي،
segments.transliteration: wa an tufarrija `anny wa takshifa durry wa tastanqidhany min wartati,

translation.en: And to relieve me (from my distress) and to remove away my harm and to save me from my troubles
And to release me from my ordeal
And to help me settle my debts
And to help me fulfill my trust
And to suppress my enemies
And not to make those who envy me rejoice at my misfortune

segments.arabic: وَلا تَبْتَلِيَني بِما لا طاقَةَ لي بِهِ،
segments.transliteration: wa la tabtaliyany bima la taqata ly bihi,

translation.en: And not to try me through that which I cannot stand
And to make me attain my hope
And to make easy for me my ordeal
And to facilitate my determination
And to make me reach my desire
And to join for me the welfare of the two abodes (i.e. this world and the Next World)

segments.transliteration: wa tahrusany wa kulla man yu`niny amruhu,

translation.en: And to safeguard me as well as everybody for whom I care

segments.arabic: بِعَينِكَ الّتي لا تَنامُ في اللَيْلِ وَالنَّهارِ،
segments.transliteration: bi`aynika allty la tanamu fy allayli wal-nnahari,

translation.en: With Your eye that never sleeps at night or in days;

segments.arabic: يا ذا الجَلالِ والإكْرامِ وَالأسْماءِ العِظامِ.
segments.transliteration: ya dha aljalali wal-ikrami wal-asma‘i al`izami.

translation.en: O the Lord of majesty and honor and Grand Names.

segments.arabic: اَللّهُمَّ يا رَبُّ أنا عَبْدُكَ وَابْنُ عَبْدِكَ وَابْنُ أمَتِكَ
segments.transliteration: allahumma ya rabbu ana `abduka wabnu `abdika wabnu amatika

translation.en: O Allah: O my Lord: I am verily Your servant and the son of Your servant and Your bondwoman

segments.arabic: وَمِنْ أوْلِياءِ أهْلِ بَيْتِ نَبِيِّكَ صَلّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَعَلَيْهِمْ،
segments.transliteration: wa min awliya‘i ahli bayti nabiyyika salla allahu `alayhi wa `alayhim,

translation.en: And I am one of the loyalists of Your Prophet’s Household—peace of Allah be upon him and them—
Upon whom You showered blessings
And whom You had mercy and You blessed

segments.arabic: كَما صَلَّيْتَ وَبارَكْتَ عَلى إبْراهيمَ وَآلِ إبراهيمِ
segments.transliteration: kama sallayta wa barakta `ala ibrahima wa ali ibrahimi

translation.en: In the same way as You blessed and sanctified Abraham and the household of Abraham;
Verily, You are worthy of all praise, full of all Glory.
And depending upon Your glory and Your bountifulness,
I beseech You; O my Lord; O my Lord;
O my Lord; O my Lord;
O my Lord; O my Lord;
O my Lord; O my Lord;
O my Lord; O my Lord;
In the name of Muhammad, Your servant and Messenger,
Peace of Allah be upon his and his Household,
And in the name of Your duty toward Yourself
That You may be the enemy of my enemies and those who envy me
And that You may disappoint them and revenge on them for me
And make me prevail on them and do with them on my behalf
And that You may grant me victory over them and guard me against them
And expand my sustenance
And make me attain the ultimate of my hopes;
Verily, You are All-hearing, Responding.

text.en:
The general acts are those advisedly said
or done each day in the month without distinction.

Ninth: The Holy Prophet (s.a.w.a.) is reported as saying: Whoever
repeats the following imploration for forgiveness one hundred times in
Rajab and immediately gives alms after it, Almighty Allah will
conclude his life with mercy and forgiveness.

segments.arabic: أَسْتَغْفِرُ الله الَّذِي لا إلهَ إِلاّ هُوَ وَحَدْهُ لا شَرِيكَ لَهُ وَأَتُوبُ إِلَيْهِ

text.en:
Also, whoever repeats it
four hundred times, will be given the reward of one hundred martyrs.

Tenth: The Holy Prophet (s.a.w.a.) is also reported to have said:
Whoever repeats this litany one thousand times in Rajab:

translation.en: There is no god but Allah.

text.en:
Almighty
Allah will decide for him one hundred thousand rewards and award
him one hundred houses in Paradise.

Eleventh: A Hadith reads that whoever repeats the following
imploration for forgiveness seventy times in the morning and seventy
times in the evening on each day in Rajab:

After this imploration is repeated seventy times, it is recommended
to raise the hands towards the sky and say:

will be admitted by
Almighty Allah if he dies in Rajab and Hellfire will not touch him on
account of the blessings of Rajab.

Twelfth: It is recommended to implore Almighty Allah for
forgiveness in the following form one thousand times in Rajab:

segments.arabic: أَسْتَغْفِرُ الله ذا الجَلالِ وَالاِكْرامِ مِنْ جَمِيعِ الذُّنُوبِ والآثامِ.

text.en:
so that
He will forgive

Thirteenth: In his book entitled Iqbal al-A’mal, Sayyid Ibn Tawus
has mentioned great rewards for those who repeat Surah <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>

ten thousand, one thousand, or one hundred times in Rajab. Likewise,
he has narrated that one who recites this Surah <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
 one
hundred times on Fridays in Rajab will enjoy, on the Resurrection
Day, light that draws him towards Paradise.

Fourteenth: Sayyid Ibn Tawus has also narrated that one who fasts
on a day in Rajab and offers a four-unit prayer in the first unit of
which he repeats <ref=1:2:255>Ayah al-Kursi</ref>
 one hundred times and in the second,
Surah <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
 two hundred times, will not depart life before he
sees (in sleep) his place in Paradise or someone else sees it on behalf
of him.

Fifteenth: Sayyid Ibn Tawus has also reported from the Holy
Prophet (s.a.w.a.) that if one offer a four-unit prayer on Friday in
Rajab between the obligatory Zohr and Asr Prayers in each unit of
which Surah <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
 is recited once, <ref=1:2:255>Ayah al-Kursi</ref>
 repeated seven
times, and Surah <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
 five times; and then the following
imploration for forgiveness repeated ten time:

segments.arabic: أَسْتَغْفِرُ الله الَّذِي لا إِلهَ إِلاّ هُوَ وَأَسْأَلُهُ التَّوْبَةَ

text.en:
if one offers this
prayer, Almighty Allah will decide for him since the day on which he
offered this prayer and up to the day of his death one thousand
rewards a day, a city built with ruby in Paradise for each verse (of the
Holy Qur’an) he recites, a palace built with white pearls in Paradise
for each letter he recites from the Holy Qur’an, Paradisiacal Women,
ultimate pleasure, inclusion with the (true) worshippers, seal his life
with happiness and forgiveness... etc.

Sixteenth: It is recommended to fast on three days in the month of
Rajab: Thursday, Friday, and Saturday. It has been narrated that
whoever fasts on these three days in one of the Sacred Months will be
awarded with the reward of nine hundred year worship.

Seventeenth: It is recommended to offer a sixty-unit prayer in
Rajab. Each night, it is recommended to offer two units of this prayer
in the first unit of which Surah <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
 is recited once, Surah <ref=1:109>Al-Kafiroon</ref>
 repeated three times, and Surah <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
 recited
one time. After the accomplishment of the two prayer units, it is
recommended to raise the hands towards the sky and say the
following:

segments.arabic: لا إِلهَ إِلاّ الله وَحْدَهُ لا شَرِيكَ لَهُ لَهُ المُلْكُ وَلَهُ الحَمْدُ ، يُحْيي وَيُمِيتُ وَهُوَ حَيُّ لا يَمُوتُ ، بِيَدِهِ الخَيْرُ وَهُوَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيٍْ قَدِيرٌ وَإِلَيْهِ المَصِيرُ ، وَلا حَوْلَ وَلا قُوَّةَ إِلاّ بِالله العَلِيِّ العَظِيمِ ، اللّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلى مُحَمَّدٍ النَبِيِّ الاُمِّيِّ وَآلِهِ.

text.en:
It is also recommended to pass one’s hand over the face while
uttering the last statement. The Holy Prophet (s.a.w.a.) is reported to
have said that if one offers this prayer and says this supplicatory
prayer, Almighty Allah will respond to his needs and award him the
reward of sixth times of Hajj and ‘Umrah (ritual pilgrimage).

Eighteenth: It has been also narrated that the Holy Prophet
(s.a.w.a.) said, “One who recites Surah <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
 one hundred times
at one night in Rajab will be decided to have fasted for one hundred
years for the sake of Almighty Allah Who will also decide for him one
hundred palaces (in Paradise) in the vicinity of one of the Prophets.

Nineteenth: The Holy Prophet (s.a.w.a.) is also reported as saying
that Almighty Allah will forgive all the sins of those who offer a ten
unit prayer, at one night in Rajab, reciting in each unit Surah <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
 and <ref=1:109>Al-Kafiroon</ref>
 once and repeating Surah <ref=1:112>الإخلاص</ref>
 three times.

Twentieth: In Zad al-Ma’ad, ‘Allamah al-Majlisi has mentioned
that Imam AliAmeer al-Momineen(a.s.) reported the following from
the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.a.): If one repeats the following Quranic
chapters, litanies, and supplicatory prayers three times each day and
each night in the months of Rajab, Sha’ban, and Ramadan Surah al-Faatehah,  Ayah al-Kursi,  Surah al-Kafirun, Surah al-Tawheed, Surah al-Falaq, Surah al-Nas and say:

segments.arabic: سُبْحانَ الله وَالحَمْدُ للهِ وَلا إِلهَ إِلاّ الله وَالله أَكْبَرُ ، وَلا حَوْلَ وَلا قُوَّةَ إِلاّ بِالله العَلِيّ العَظِيمِ

text.en: and say three times:

segments.arabic: اللّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلى مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ

text.en: and say three times:

and say four hundred
times:

he will
have all his sins forgiven even if they are as many as and as much as
raindrops, tree leaves, sea foam... etc.

Allamah al-Majlisi has also mentioned that it is traditional to
repeat the following litany one thousand times each night in Rajab:

translation.en: There is no god but Allah.

text.en: Daywise Salats in the Month of Rajab
As reported in Iqbaal ul-A'maal

2nd Night of Rajab – It has been reported from the Holy Prophet (peace and blessing be upon his family): Whosoever recites on the second night of Rajab, a 10 (5x2) Raka’ prayer, reciting in each raka’ Sura <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
 once and Sura <ref=1:109>Al-Kafiroon</ref>
 once, Allah will forgive all his sins, big or small, and will write his name among the musalleen until the next year.

3rd Night of Rajab – It has been reported from the Holy Prophet (peace and blessing be upon his family): Whoever recites on the third night of Rajab, a 10 (5x2) Raka’ prayer, reciting in each Raka’ Sura <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
 once and Sura <ref=1:110>Al-Nasr</ref> 
 5 times, Allah will build him a palace in Jannah, the length and width of which will be 7 times the size of the world.

3rd Day of Rajab - It has been reported from the Holy Prophet (peace and blessing be upon his family): Whoever recites on the third day of Rajab, a 4 (2x2) Raka’ prayer, reciting in each Raka’ after Sura <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
, Sura <ref=1:2:163-165>Baqarah 163-165-</ref>
, Allah will give him a reward that cannot be described.

4th Night of Rajab – It has been reported from the Holy Prophet (peace and blessing be upon his family): Whoever recites on the fourth night of Rajab, a 100 (50x2) raka’ Salaah, in each first Raka' after Sura <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
 reciting Sura <ref=1:113>Al-Falaq</ref>
; in each second Raka' after Sura <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
 reciting Sura <ref=1:114>Al-Nas</ref>
, an angel would descend from each heaven to record the reward for him up until the Resurrection Day when his face shall be as bright as the moon, and he would be given his Record of Deeds to the right hand and he would benefit from an easy reckoning.

5th Night of Rajab - It has been reported from the Holy Prophet (peace and blessing be upon his family): Whoever recites on the fifth night of Rajab, a 6 (3x2) Raka’ prayer, reciting in each Raka’ after Sura <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
, 25 times Sura <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
, Allah will give him the reward of 40 prophets, 40 truthful ones, 40 martyrs, and will cause him to pass on the Serat like lightning as if he is riding on a horse made of light.

6th Night of Rajab - It has been reported from the Holy Prophet (peace and blessing be upon his family): Whoever recites on the sixth night of Rajab, a 2 Raka' prayer, and in each unit, he recites Sura <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
 once, and <ref=1:2:255>Ayah al-Kursi</ref>
 seven times, a caller from Heaven would call, “O servant of God! You are truly a friend of God indeed. For each letter that you recited in this prayer, there is intercession from the Muslims, you will have seventy-thousand good deeds and each of the good deeds near God are greater than the mountains in this world.”

7th Night of Rajab The Holy prophet (saws) says that if a person recites 4 unit namaaz on the night of 7th rajab, (such that in each unit after Surah <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
 recites Surat <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
 thrice, Surah <ref=1:113>Al-Falaq</ref>
 once, Surah an <ref=1:114>Al-Nas</ref>
 once. After finishing namaaz recites Salwaat &10 times Tasbeehate Arba’). Allah will offer him shelter under the Arsh, & give him reward of a person fasting in the month of ramdhan. Till the time he finishes the prayers, the angels pray for his forgiveness. Allah will ease the pangs of death on him & save him from the squeeze in the grave. He will not die unless he sees his place in Paradise, & will be safe from the hardships of Qiyamat.

8th Night of Rajab - It has been reported from the Holy Prophet (peace and blessing be upon his family): Whoever recites on the eighth night of Rajab, a 20 (10x2) Raka’ Prayer, in each raka’ after Sura <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
, reciting Surah’s <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
, <ref=1:109>Al-Kafiroon</ref>
, <ref=1:113>Al-Falaq</ref>
 and <ref=1:114>Al-Nas</ref>
 3 times each; Allah would reward him the reward of the thankful and the people who persevere and would raise his name amongst the truthful ones and for each letter that he says, Allah would grant him the reward of all the truthful ones and martyrs. It would be as if he recited the whole Quran during the month of Ramadhan. He will be welcomed by seventy angels once he leaves the grave. These angels will give him the glad tidings of Paradise and guide him to it.

9th Night of Rajab - It has been reported from the Holy Prophet (peace and blessing be upon his family): Whoever recites a 2 Raka' of prayer on the ninth night of Rajab and in each unit he recites the <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
once, the <ref=1:102>Al-Takathur</ref> 
 Chapter five times, Allah would forgive him, grant him the reward of one-hundred Hajj and Umrah pilgrimages, send down a million forms of mercy for him, secure him from the Fire before he moves from his place. Should he die within eighty days, he would be considered a martyr.

10th Night of Rajab - It has been reported from the Holy Prophet (peace and blessing be upon his family): Whoever recites on the tenth night of Rajab, a 12 (6x2) Raka' prayer, and in each unit reciting Sura <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
 once and Sura <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
 three times, Allah would raise a palace for him over a column made of red rubies...In this column there are seven-hundred rooms that are each larger than this world. All the rooms in it are made of gold, silver, rubies and aquamarine. There are as many houses in this palace as there are stars in the sky with things there that man cannot describe.

11th Night of Rajab - It has been reported from the Holy Prophet (peace and blessing be upon his family): Whoever recites on the eleventh night of Rajab, a 12 (6x2) Raka' prayer, and in each unit reciting Sura <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
 once, and <ref=1:2:255>Ayah al-Kursi</ref>
 twelve times, Allah will give him the reward of one who has recited the Torah, the Bible, the Psalms, and the Criterion (Quran), and any of the Books which God – the Sublime has revealed to His Prophets. A Caller from the Throne will call, Restart your deeds as indeed God has forgiven you.

12th Night of Rajab - It has been reported from the Holy Prophet (peace and blessing be upon his family): Whoever recites on the twelfth night of Rajab, a 2 Raka' prayer, reciting in each unit Sura <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
 once and Sura <ref=1:2:285-286>Baqara:285-286</ref>
 ten times, Allah would grant him the reward of those who enjoin to do good and forbid from evil, and the reward of freeing seventy slaves from the progeny of Ishmael, and Allah would grant him seventy mercies.

13th Night of Rajab - It has been reported from the Holy Prophet (peace and blessing be upon his family): Whoever recites on the thirteenth night of Rajab, a 10 (5x2) Raka' prayer, reciting in the first Raka' Sura <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
 and Sura <ref=1:100>Al-Adiyaat</ref> 
 once, and in the other nine units reciting Sura <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
 and Sura <ref=1:102>Takathur</ref> 
 once each,

Allah would forgive him even if he has been damned by his parents, neither Munkar nor Nakir would not approach or frighten him and he would cross the Bridge like lightning and his Record of Deeds would be handed to his right hand and his Record of Deeds would be made heavier in his favor and he would be granted one-thousand towns in Paradise.

14th Night of Rajab - It has been reported from the Holy Prophet (peace and blessing be upon his family): Whoever recites on the fourteenth night of Rajab, a 30 (15x2) Raka' prayer, reciting in each unit Sura <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
, Sura <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
, <ref=1:18:110>Aya 110 of Sura Kahf</ref>
once each,

I swear by Him who controls my life that he would finish his prayer perfectly purified even if his sins are more than the stars in the sky and it would be as if he has recited the whole Book that the Sublime God has sent down.
<ref=3:1011>15th Night of Rajab</ref>

16th Night of Rajab - It has been reported from the Holy Prophet (peace and blessing be upon his family): Whoever recites on the sixteenth night of Rajab, a 30 (15x2) Raka' prayer, reciting in each unit Sura <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
 and Sura <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
 ten times,

Allah would grant him the reward of seventy martyrs before he finishes his prayer and on the Resurrection Day his brightness would shine and look like the space between Mecca and Medina, and Allah rewards him immunity from the Fire, protection from hypocrisy and frees him from the torture of the grave.

17th Night of Rajab - It has been reported from the Holy Prophet (peace and blessing be upon his family): Whoever recites on the seventeenth night of Rajab, a 30 (15x2) Raka' prayer, reciting in each unit Sura <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
 and Sura <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
 ten times,
Allah would grant him the reward of seventy martyrs before he finishes his prayer.

18th Night of Rajab - It has been reported from the Holy Prophet (peace and blessing be upon his family): Whoever recites on the eighteenth night of Rajab, a 2 Raka' prayer, reciting in each unit Sura <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
 once and Sura <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
 and Sura <ref=1:113>Al-Falaq</ref>
 and <ref=1:114>Al-Nas</ref>
 ten times each,

upon finishing his prayer Allah would tell the angels, I forgive his sins for saying this prayer even if his sins are more than those of a cheating businessman and Allah would place six ditches between him and the Fire the distance between each ditch would be as much as the distance between the sky and the earth.

19th Night of Rajab - It has been reported from the Holy Prophet (peace and blessing be upon his family): Whoever recites on the nineteenth night of the month of Rajab, a 4 (2x2) Raka' prayer, reciting in each unit Sura <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
 once, and 15 times <ref=1:2:255>Ayah al-Kursi</ref>
 and 15 times Sura <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>

Allah grants him a reward similar to what He granted Moses. For each letter, he would be granted the reward of a martyr. Allah would resurrect him with angels who give him three glad tidings. First, he would not experience problems at the stopping point in the Hereafter, second there would be no reckoning for him and third he would enter Paradise without any reckoning. And once he stops in front of Allah – the Sublime Allah would greet him and tell him, O my servant! Have no fear or grief since I am pleased with you and Paradise is allowed for you.

20th Night of Rajab - It has been reported from the Holy Prophet (peace and blessing be upon his family): Whoever recites on the twentieth night of the month of Rajab, a 2 Raka' prayer, reciting in each unit Sura <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
 once and Sura <ref=1:97>Al-Qadr</ref> 
 five times,
shall be rewarded the reward of Abraham, Moses, John and Jesus by Allah. Whoever says this prayer shall not be harmed by the genies or men. Allah would look upon him with Mercy.

21st Night of Rajab - It has been reported from the Holy Prophet (peace and blessing be upon his family): Whoever recites on the twenty-first night of the month of Rajab, a 6 (3x2) Raka' prayer, reciting in each unit Sura <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
 once, Sura <ref=1:108>Al-Kauthar</ref>
 ten times, Sura <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
 ten times,

Allah orders the angels – the Honorable Recorders (Kiraman Katibin) not to record any bad deeds for him for one year and only record his good deeds. I swear by Him who rightfully appointed me to the Prophethood that anyone who loves me and loves Allah and says this prayer – and if he cannot stand up he can pray sitting down – Allah takes pride in it near His angels and says, Indeed I forgive him.

22nd Night of Rajab - It has been reported from the Holy Prophet (peace and blessing be upon his family): Whoever recites on the twenty-second night of the month of Rajab, a 8 (4x2) Raka' prayer, reciting in each unit Sura <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
 once and Sura <ref=1:109>Al-Kafiroon</ref>
 seven times, and after finishing the prayer send blessings on the Prophet and his progeny (salawaat) ten times and asks for forgiveness ten times,
he shall not depart from this world until he sees his place in Paradise. He shall die as a Muslim and shall receive the rewards of seventy Prophets

22nd Rajab Manazeral Akherah- Shaikh Abbas Qummi To recite the following 8 Rak’at namaaz(2 rakat X4) on the 22nd of Rajab is beneficial during death. In each Rakat after <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
 , recite Surah <ref=1:109>Al-Kafiroon</ref>
 7 times. After finishing Namaaz, recite salawaat 10 times & recite Astaghfirullah Rabbi wa atooboIlayh 10 times.

23rd Night of Rajab - It has been reported from the Holy Prophet (peace and blessing be upon his family): Whoever recites on the twenty-third night of the month of Rajab, a 2 Raka' prayer, reciting in each unit Sura <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
 once and Sura <ref=1:93>Al-Dhuha</ref> 
 five times,

Allah grants him a rank in Paradise for each letter he has recited and for each atheist man or woman a rank for him in Paradise and Allah would also grant him the reward of seventy Hajj pilgrimages, the reward of attending one-thousand funeral processions, the reward of visiting one-thousand patients, plus the reward of fulfilling the needs of one-thousand Muslims.

24th Night of Rajab - It has been reported from the Holy Prophet (peace and blessing be upon his family): Whoever recites on the twenty-fourth night of the month of Rajab, a 40 (20x2) Raka' prayer, reciting in each unit Sura <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
 once and <ref=1:2:285-286>Aman-al-rasool</ref> 
 once and Sura <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
 once,

Allah would record the reward of a thousand good deeds, wipe out a thousand of his wrongdoings and raise his rank a thousand degrees. And He would send down a thousand angels from the heavens who raise up their hands and bestow blessings upon him. Allah – the Sublime would grant him health in the world and the Hereafter and t would be as if he has experienced the Night of Destiny.

25th Night of Rajab - It has been reported from the Holy Prophet (peace and blessing be upon his family): Whoever recites on the twenty-fifth night of the month of Rajab, a 20 (10x2) Raka' prayer between Maghrib and Isha, reciting in each unit Sura <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
 once and <ref=1:2:285-286>Aman-al-rasool</ref> 
 once and Sura <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
 once,
Allah would protect himself, his wife, his religion, his wealth, his life and his afterlife and he would be forgiven before he moves from his place.

26th Night of Rajab - It has been reported from the Holy Prophet (peace and blessing be upon his family): Whoever recites on the twenty-sixth night of the month of Rajab, a 12 (6x2) Raka' prayer, reciting in each unit Sura <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
 once and Sura <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
 fourty times (in some narrations it has been mentioned four times),

would be hugged by the angels and whoever is hugged by the angels would be saved from being stopped at the Bridge, the Reckoning and the Balance. Allah would appoint seventy angels to ask for Allah's forgiveness for him, record rewards for him, and express Allah's greatness for him and they say, "O' my Allah! Please forgive this worshipping servant!" until the morning.
<ref=3:1019>27th Night of Rajab</ref>

28th Night of Rajab - It has been reported from the Holy Prophet (peace and blessing be upon his family): Whoever recites on the twenty-eighth night of the month of Rajab, a 12 (6x2) Raka' prayer, reciting in each unit Sura <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
 once and Sura <ref=1:87>Al-A'ala</ref> 
 and Sura <ref=1:97>Al-Qadr</ref> 
 ten times each and after completion of the prayer, recites salawaat and asks forgiveness from Allah 100 times each,
Allah the Glorified would record the reward of the worshipping of the angels for him.

29th Night of Rajab - It has been reported from the Holy Prophet (peace and blessing be upon his family): Whoever recites on the twenty-ninth night of the month of Rajab, a 12 (6x2) Raka' prayer, reciting in each unit Sura <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
 once and Sura <ref=1:87>Al-A'ala</ref> 
 and Sura <ref=1:97>Al-Qadr</ref> 
 ten times each and after completion of the prayer, recites salawaat and asks forgiveness from Allah 100 times each,
Allah the Glorified would record the reward of the worshipping of the angels for him.

30th Night of Rajab - It has been reported from the Holy Prophet (peace and blessing be upon his family): Whoever recites on the thirtieth night of the month of Rajab, a 10 (5x2) Raka' prayer, reciting in each unit Sura <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
 once and Sura <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
 ten times,

Allah shall grant him seven towns in Paradise and he would be taken out of the grave with his face shining like the full moon. He would cross the Bridge like glaring lightning and would be saved from the Fire.
Ziyarat Rajabiyah

Shaykh al-Tusi has also reported the following words from Abu’l-Qasim ibn Ruh, the Private Representative of Imam al-Mahdi (`a): “In Rajab, you may visit any of the shrines (of the Holy Infallibles) that are possible for you to visit, and say the following prayer therein:

segments.arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ٱلَّذِي اشْهَدَنَا مَشْهَدَ اوْلِيَائِهِ فِي رَجَبٍ
segments.transliteration: alhamdu lillahi alladhi ashhadana mashhada awliya'ihi fi rajabin

translation.en: All praise be to Allah Who has allowed us to visit the shrine of His Saints in Rajab

segments.arabic: وَاوْجَبَ عَلَيْنَا مِنْ حَقِّهِمْ مَا قَدْ وَجَبَ
segments.transliteration: wa awjaba `alayna min haqqihim ma qad wajaba

translation.en: and made obligatory upon us fulfilling our duties towards them.
May Allah send blessings to Muhammad, the divinely selected,
and upon his Successors—the doors to Him.
O Allah, just as You have allowed us to visit their shrines,
please do fulfill for us the promises You have made with them,
include us with them,
and include us with those whom shall not be prevented from drinking from the (Divine) Pool
in the abode of eternity and perpetuity.
Peace be upon you all.
I have turned my face towards you and directed to you
carrying my request and need with me,

segments.transliteration: wa hiya fakaku raqabati mina alnnari

translation.en: which is the release of my neck from Hellfire
and the settlement with you in the Abode of Final Settlement
with your pious adherents (Shi`ah).
Peace be upon you all for that you persevered in patience.
Now how excellent is the final home!

segments.arabic: انَا سَائِلُكُمْ وَآمِلُكُمْ فِيمَا إِلَيْكُمُ ٱلتَّفْوِيضُ
segments.transliteration: ana sa'ilukum wa amilukum fima ilaykumu alttafwidu

translation.en: I hereby beg you and put my hope in you as regards the matters in which you have the right to act
and recompense.
Truly, through you only are the hopeless restored to good condition,
the ailed healed,

segments.transliteration: wa ma tazdadu al-arhamu wa ma taghidu

translation.en: and that which the wombs absorb and that which they grow.
Verily, I have full faith in your Secret
and I am fully submissive to your words;
I thus beg you in the name of Allah
to make me return having my requests responded,
met, accepted,
given success, and settled,
and set aright all my needs and all my affairs towards you.
Peace be upon you from one who bids you farewell
and puts all his needs with you,
praying to Allah for another visit to you,
since his hope is never cut off from you.

segments.arabic: وَانْ يَرْجِعَنِي مِنْ حَضْرَتِكُمْ خَيْرَ مَرْجِعٍ
segments.transliteration: wa an yarji`ani min hadratikum khayra marji`in

translation.en: I also pray Him to make my departure successful
to a productive place,
a fruitful area,

segments.transliteration: wa da`atin wa mahalin ila hini al-ajali

translation.en: comfort, and luxurious up to the befalling of death.

segments.arabic: وَخَيْرِ مَصِيرٍ وَمَحَلٍّ فِي ٱلنَّعِيمِ ٱلازَلِ
segments.transliteration: wa khayri masirin wa mahallin fi alnna`imi al-azali

translation.en: I also pray Him for the best destiny and abode in the Eternal Bliss,
the affluent living,
the perpetual fruit,
the drinking from the pure drink,

segments.arabic: وَعَلٍّ وَنَهَلٍ لا سَامَ مِنْهُ وَلاَ مَلَلَ
segments.transliteration: wa `allin wa nahalin la sa'ama minhu wa la malala

translation.en: and the divine spring whose drink is refreshing and thirst-quenching that is never bored or fed up.

segments.transliteration: wa rahmatu allahi wa barakatuhu wa tahiyyatuhu `alaykum

translation.en: Allah’s mercy, blessings, and salutations be upon you incessantly
until I return to your presence
and win the honor of visiting you again
and the honor of being resurrected with your group.

segments.arabic: وَرَحْمَةُ ٱللَّهِ وَبَرَكَاتُهُ عَلَيْكُمْ وَصَلَوَاتُهُ وَتَحِيَّاتُهُ
segments.transliteration: wa rahmatu allahi wa barakatuhu `alaykum wa salawatuhu wa tahiyyatuhu

translation.en: Allah’s mercy, blessings, benedictions, and salutations be upon you,

segments.transliteration: wa huwa hasbuna wa ni`ma alwakilu

translation.en: for Allah alone is Sufficient for us! Most Excellent is He in Whom we trust!

text.en: Ziyarah of Imam Ali(peace be upon him) on the Night and Day of Maba'th (27th Rajab)
The ziyarah has been mentioned by Shaykh al-Mufid, Sayyid Ibn Tawus, and al-Shahid in the following form:

When you intend to visit the holy shrine of Imam `Ali Amir al-Mu'minin (peace be upon him) on the Divine Mission Day or the night before it, you may stop at the gate of the holy dome, facing the holy tomb, and say the following:

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا وَارِثَ آدَمَ خَلِيفَةِ ٱللَّهِ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayka ya waritha adama khalifati allahi

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O inheritor of Adam the vicegerent of Allah.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا وَارِثَ نُوحٍ صِفْوَةِ ٱللَّهِ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayka ya waritha nuhin sifwati allahi

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O inheritor of Noah the choice of Allah.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا وَارِثَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ خَلِيلِ ٱللَّهِ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayka ya waritha ibrahima khalili allahi

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O inheritor of Abraham the intimate friend of Allah.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا وَارِثَ مُوسَىٰ كَلِيمِ ٱللَّهِ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayka ya waritha musa kalimi allahi

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O inheritor of Moses the spoken by Allah.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا وَارِثَ عِيسَىٰ رُوحِ ٱللَّهِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O inheritor of Jesus the spirit of Allah.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا وَارِثَ مُحَمَّدٍ سَيِّدِ رُسُلِ ٱللَّهِ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayka ya waritha muhammadin sayyidi rusuli allahi

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O inheritor of Muhammad the chief of Allah’s Messengers.
Peace be upon you, O commander of the faithful.
Peace be upon you, O epitome of the pious ones.
Peace be upon you, O chief of the Prophets’ successors.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا وَصِيَّ رَسُولِ رَبِّ ٱلْعَالَمِينَ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayka ya wasiyya rasuli rabbi al`alamina

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O successor of the Messenger of the Lord of the worlds.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا وَارِثَ عِلْمِ ٱلأَوَّلِينَ وَٱلآخِرِينَ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O inheritor of the knowledge of the past and the coming generations.
Peace be upon you, O Great News.
Peace be upon you, O Straight Path.
Peace be upon you, O noble, refined one.
Peace be upon you, O pious successor (of the Prophet).
Peace be upon you, O well-pleased, pure one.
Peace be upon you, O shining full moon.
Peace be upon you, O grandest veracious one.
Peace be upon you, O greatest distinguisher (between the right and the wrong).
Peace be upon you, O glowing lantern.
Peace be upon you, O leader to the true guidance.
Peace be upon you, O pattern of piety.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا حُجَّةَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلْكُبْرَىٰ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O grand argument of Allah.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا خَاصَّةَ ٱللَّهِ وَخَالِصَتَهُ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayka ya khassata allahi wa khalisatahu

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O select and elite of Allah,
trustee and choice of Allah,
door to and argument of Allah,

segments.transliteration: wa ma`dina hukmi allahi wa sirrahu

translation.en: source and secret of Allah’s judgment,
guardian and keeper of Allah’s knowledge,
and envoy of Allah among His creatures.
I bear witness that you performed the prayers,
paid the zakat,
enjoined the right,
forbade the wrong,
followed the Messenger,
recited the Book as exactly as it must be recited,
conveyed the messages of Allah,
fulfilled your covenant with Allah,
the Words of Allah were accomplished by you,

segments.transliteration: wa jahadta fi allahi haqqa jihadihi

translation.en: strove in Allah’s way in the best manner,

segments.arabic: وَنَصَحْتَ لِلَّهِ وَلِرَسُولِهِ صَلَّىٰ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ
segments.transliteration: wa nasahta lillahi wa lirasulihi salla allahu `alayhi wa alihi

translation.en: advised for the sake of Allah and for the sake of His Messenger—peace be upon him and his Household,
and sacrificed yourself with steadfastness, seeking Allah’s reward,
struggling for the defense of Allah’s religion,

segments.arabic: مُوَقِّياً لِرَسُولِ ٱللَّهِ صَلَّىٰ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ
segments.transliteration: muwaqqiyan lirasuli allahi salla allahu `alayhi wa alihi

translation.en: protecting Allah’s Messenger—peace be upon him and his Household,
seeking for that which is with Allah,
and desiring for that which Allah has promised.
You also sealed your life as martyr,
a witness, and witnessed;
so, may Allah reward you
on behalf of His Messenger, Islam, and the people of Islam
with the most favorite reward, for you are veracious.

segments.arabic: أَشْهَدُ أَنَّكَ كُنْتَ أَوَّلَ ٱلْقَوْمِ إِسْلاَماً
segments.transliteration: ashhadu annaka kunta awwala alqawmi islaman

translation.en: I bear witness that you were the first to embrace Islam,
the most sincere in faith,
the most confident,
and you were the most Allah-fearing,
the most steadfast (against ordeals for the sake of Islam),

segments.arabic: وَأَحْوَطَهُمْ عَلَىٰ رَسُولِ ٱللَّهِ صَلَّىٰ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ
segments.transliteration: wa ahwatahum `ala rasuli allahi salla allahu `alayhi wa alihi

translation.en: the most watchful for Allah’s Messenger, peace be upon him and his Household,
endowed with the most favorable merits,
the foremost of everyone else in everything,
the owner of the most elevated rank,
the owner of the most honorable position,
and the most respected (by Almighty Allah and His Messenger).
Thus, you became stronger when they became weak

segments.arabic: وَلَزِمْتَ مِنْهَاجَ رَسُولِ ٱللَّهِ صَلَّىٰ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ
segments.transliteration: wa lazimta minhaja rasuli allahi salla allahu `alayhi wa alihi

translation.en: and you abode by the course of Allah’s Messenger, peace be upon him and his Household.

segments.transliteration: wa ashhadu annaka kunta khalifatahu haqqan

translation.en: I also bear witness that you were the true vicegerent of him (i.e. the Holy Prophet);
no one could ever compete with you in this position in spite of the existence of hypocrites,
the despise of the unbelievers,
and the malice of the wicked.
You also managed the matters when they failed to do so,
you spoke (the truth) when they stammered,

segments.transliteration: wa madayta binuri allahi idh waqafu

translation.en: and you carried on with the light of Allah when they stopped.
Hence, he who follows you has actually been led to the true guidance.
You were the first to speak,
the firmest in refuting the rivals,
the most eloquent of them,
the most apposite in opinions,
the most courageous,
the most confident of them,
the best of them in deeds,
and the most learned in affairs.
For the believers, you were merciful father,
and they were thus your dependants;
so, you carried the burdens that they were too weak to carry,
preserved that which they forfeited,
conserved that which they neglected,
prepared yourself for facing that which they were too coward to face,
advanced when they were dismayed,
and acted steadfastly when they became anxious.
You were thus incessant torment on the unbelievers,
and you were also rude and furious (on them).

segments.transliteration: wa lilmu'minina ghaythan wa khisban wa `ilman

translation.en: You rained (with mercy) on the believers, and you were fertile and source of knowledge for them.
Your argument was never weak,
your heart never deviated,
your sagacity was never feeble,
and your determination never cowered.
You were as firm as mountain, since storms could never displace you
and misfortunes could never move you.

segments.arabic: كُنْتَ كَمَا قَالَ رَسُولُ ٱللَّهِ صَلَّىٰ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ
segments.transliteration: kunta kama qala rasulu allahi salla allahu `alayhi wa alihi

translation.en: You were as exactly as described by Allah’s Messenger—peace be upon him and his Household—who said about you
that you are strong in your body,
modest in yourself,
great in the view of Allah,
imposing in the earth,
and lofty in the heavens.
None could ever find fault with you,
none could ever speak evil of you,
you never desired for any of the created beings,
and you were never lenient (unfairly) to anyone.

segments.arabic: يُوجَدُ ٱلضَّعِيفُ ٱلذَّلِيلُ عِنْدَكَ قَوِيّاً عَزِيزاً حَتَّىٰ تَأْخُذَ لَهُ بِحَقِّهِ
segments.transliteration: yujadu aldda`ifu aldhdhalilu `indaka qawiyyan `azizan hatta ta'khudha lahu bihaqqihi

translation.en: The weak, humble one was strong and mighty in your view until you give him back his right,

segments.arabic: وَٱلْقَوِيُّ ٱلْعَزِيزُ عِنْدَكَ ضَعِيفاً حَتَّىٰ تَأْخُذَ مِنْهُ ٱلْحَقَّ
segments.transliteration: walqawiyyu al`azizu `indaka da`ifan hatta ta'khudha minhu alhaqqa

translation.en: and the strong, mighty one was weak in your view until you take the others’ rights from him.

segments.arabic: الْقَرِيبُ وَٱلْبَعِيدُ عِنْدَكَ فِي ذٰلِكَ سَوَاءٌ

translation.en: The near and the remote were equal in your view.
Your habit was to be true, honest, and kind,
your words were ruling and determination,
your custom was forbearance and fortitude,
and your view was knowledge and firmness.
At your hands, the religion became even,
the difficult became easy,
the fires were extinguished,
the faith became strong,
Islam became steady,
and the calamity of losing you undermined all people.

segments.transliteration: fa'inna lillahi wa inna ilayhi raji`una

translation.en: Surely, we are Allah's and to Him we shall surely return.
Curse of Allah be upon him who slew you,
curse of Allah be upon him who mutinied against you,
curse of Allah be upon him who forged lies against you,

segments.arabic: وَلَعَنَ ٱللَّهُ مَنْ ظَلَمَكَ وَغَصَبَكَ حَقَّكَ
segments.transliteration: wa la`ana allahu man zalamaka wa ghasabaka haqqaka

translation.en: curse of Allah be upon him who wronged you and usurped your right,

segments.arabic: وَلَعَنَ ٱللَّهُ مَنْ بَلَغَهُ ذٰلِكَ فَرَضِيَ بِهِ
segments.transliteration: wa la`ana allahu man balaghahu dhalika faradiya bihi

translation.en: and curse of Allah be upon him who, when was informed about this, was pleased with it.
Verily, we disavow all those before Allah.
Curse of Allah be upon the group that disagreed with you,
denied your (Divinely commissioned) leadership,
aided each other against you, slew you,
deviated from you, and disappointed you.

segments.arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ٱلَّذِي جَعَلَ ٱلنَّارَ مَثْوَاهُمْ
segments.transliteration: alhamdu lillahi alladhi ja`ala alnnara mathwahum

translation.en: All praise be to Allah, for Hellfire is the abode of those
and evil the place to which they are brought.

segments.arabic: أَشْهَدُ لَكَ يَا وَلِيَّ ٱللَّهِ وَوَلِيَّ رَسُولِهِ صَلَّىٰ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ بِٱلْبَلاَغِ وَٱلأَدَاءِ
segments.transliteration: ashhadu laka ya waliyya allahi wa waliyya rasulihi salla allahu `alayhi wa alihi bilbalaghi wal-ada'i

translation.en: I bear witness for you, O Intimate servant of Allah and loyalist of Allah’s Messenger, peace be upon him and his Household, that you did convey and carry out (your mission).

segments.transliteration: wa ashhadu annaka habibu allahi wa babuhu

translation.en: And I bear witness that you are the beloved of Allah and the door to Him,

segments.arabic: وَأَنَّكَ جَنْبُ ٱللَّهِ وَوَجْهُهُ ٱلَّذِي مِنْهُ يُؤْتَىٰ
segments.transliteration: wa annaka janbu allahi wa wajhuhu alladhi minhu yu'ta

translation.en: you are the Side of Allah and His Face from which one can come to Allah,
you are Allah’s way,
you are Allah’s servant,

segments.arabic: وَأَخُو رَسُولِهِ صَلَّىٰ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ
segments.transliteration: wa akhu rasulihi salla allahu `alayhi wa alihi

translation.en: and you are the brother of His Messenger, peace be upon him and his Household.

segments.arabic: أَتَيْتُكَ زَائِراً لِعَظِيمِ حَالِكَ وَمَنْزِلَتِكَ عِنْدَ ٱللَّهِ وَعِنْدَ رَسُولِهِ
segments.transliteration: ataytuka za'iran li`azimi halika wa manzilatika `inda allahi wa `inda rasulihi

translation.en: I have thus come to visit you because you enjoy a magnificent position and standing with Allah and with His Messenger.
So, I seek nearness to Allah through my visit to you
and I desire that you will intercede for me.
Through your intercession for me, I hope for redemption of myself,
I seek refuge with you against Hellfire,

segments.arabic: هَارِباً مِنْ ذُنُوبِيَ ٱلَّتِي ٱحْتَطَبْتُهَا عَلَىٰ ظَهْرِي
segments.transliteration: hariban min dhunubiya allati ihtatabtuha `ala zahri

translation.en: I am fleeing from my sins that I have overburdened myself with them,
and I resort to you in the hope of my Lord’s mercy.

segments.arabic: أَتَيْتُكَ أَسْتَشْفِعُ بِكَ يَا مَوْلاَيَ إِلَىٰ ٱللَّهِ
segments.transliteration: ataytuka astashfi`u bika ya mawlaya ila allahi

translation.en: I have come to you, O my master, seeking your intercession for me before Allah

segments.arabic: وَأَتَقَرَّبُ بِكَ إِلَيْهِ لِيَقْضِيَ بِكَ حَوَائِجِي
segments.transliteration: wa ataqarrabu bika ilayhi liyaqdiya bika hawa'iji

translation.en: and seeking nearness to Him through you so that He may settle my needs;
so, (please) intercede for me, O Commander of the Faithful.

segments.transliteration: fa'inni `abdu allahi wa mawlaka wa za'iruka

translation.en: Verily, I am the slave of Allah and your servant and visitor,
and you enjoy a distinguished position with Allah,
a great rank,
a big standing,
and an admitted right of intercession.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ
segments.transliteration: allahumma salli `ala muhammadin wa ali muhammadin

translation.en: O Allah, (please do) send blessings upon Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad
and send blessings upon Your servant, Your most faithful trustee,
Your firmest handle,
Your highest Hand,
Your excellent Word,
Your argument on the people,
Your grandest veracious one,
the chief of the Prophets’ successors,
the reliance of the saints,
the support of the elite ones,
the commander of the faithful,
the leader of the pious ones,
the example of the veracious ones,
the pattern of the righteous ones,
the one protected against flaws,
the one saved from defects,
the one cleared from faults,
the one purified from doubts,
the brother of Your Prophet,
the successor of Your Messenger,
the one who replaced him when he spent that night instead of him,
the one who sacrificed his soul for him,
the one who always relieved him in harsh situations,
and the one whom You chose to be the sword of his (i.e. the Prophet) Prophethood,
the miracle of his (Divine) mission,
the lucid indication of his arguments,
the bearer of his pennon,
the protector of his soul,
the guide of his nation,
the power of his might,
the crown of his head,
the door to his victory,
and the key to his triumph,
until he defeated the armies of polytheism by Your aid,
terminated the warriors of atheism by Your permission,

segments.arabic: وَبَذَلَ نَفْسَهُ فِي مَرْضَاتِكَ وَمَرْضَاةِ رَسُولِكَ
segments.transliteration: wa badhala nafsahu fi mardatika wa mardati rasulika

translation.en: exerted all efforts to win the pleasure of You and Your Messenger,
devoted all of his efforts to the obedience to him,
and made of himself armor to protect him from any misfortune,

segments.arabic: حَتَّىٰ فَاضَتْ نَفْسُهُ صَلَّىٰ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ فِي كَفِّهِ
segments.transliteration: hatta fadat nafsuhu salla allahu `alayhi wa alihi fi kaffihi

translation.en: until the soul of the Prophet, peace be upon him and his Household, departed his body while his face was in the hand of `Ali,
who thus felt its coldness,
and rubbed his face therewith.

segments.transliteration: wa a`anat-hu mala'ikatuka `ala ghuslihi wa tajhizihi

translation.en: And Your angels helped him bathe and prepare his body for burial.
He then performed [the ritual] prayer for him, buried his body,
settled his debts,
fulfilled his promises,
made his covenants,
followed his example,
and abode by his will.

segments.arabic: وَحِينَ وَجَدَ أَنْصَاراً نَهَضَ مُسْتَقِلاًّ بِأَعْبَاءِ ٱلْخِلاَفَةِ
segments.transliteration: wa hina wajada ansaran nahada mustaqillan bi'a`ba'i alkhilafati

translation.en: When he could find supporters, he undertook the burdens of the position of leadership (caliphate)
and assumed the loads of Imamate.
He thus hoisted the flag of true guidance among Your servants,

segments.transliteration: wa nashara thawba al-amni fi biladika

translation.en: spread the dress of security in Your lands,
extended justice among Your creatures,
judged according to Your Book among your beings,
executed the laws,
suppressed unbelief,
rectified the deviation,
mollified the criticism,
eradicated lethargy,
blocked the gap,
killed the preachers, the wrongdoers, and the apostates,

segments.arabic: وَلَمْ يَزَلْ عَلَىٰ مِنْهَاجِ رَسُولِ ٱللَّهِ صَلَّىٰ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ وَوَتِيرَتِهِ
segments.transliteration: wa lam yazal `ala minhaji rasuli allahi salla allahu `alayhi wa alihi wa watiratihi

translation.en: kept on following the course and tradition of Allah’s Messenger, peace be upon him and his Household,
kept on following his excellent pattern,
kept on following his superb practice,
he imitated his convention,
held fast to his morale,
and copied his way (of treating with matters).
And he always noticed the Prophet’s deeds as if they were before his eyes;
he thus ordered Your servants to copy these deeds
and invited them to imitate them

segments.arabic: إِلَىٰ أَنْ خُضِبَتْ شَيْبَتُهُ مِنْ دَمِ رَأْسِهِ
segments.transliteration: ila an khudibat shaybatuhu min dami ra'sihi

translation.en: until his white beard was dyed with the blood of his head.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ فَكَمَا لَمْ يُؤْثِرْ فِي طَاعَتِكَ شَكّاً عَلَىٰ يَقِينٍ
segments.transliteration: allahumma fakama lam yu'thir fi ta`atika shakkan `ala yaqinin

translation.en: O Allah, as he never preferred a dubious matter to a certain one for the sake of obeying You
and never associated any one with You even for a winking of an eye,
please send upon him growing and increasing blessings

segments.arabic: يَلْحَقُ بِهَا دَرَجَةَ ٱلنُّبُوَّةِ فِي جَنَّتِكَ
segments.transliteration: yalhaqu biha darajata alnnubuwwati fi jannatika

translation.en: due to which he may join the position of Prophethood in Your Paradise,

segments.transliteration: wa ballighhu minna tahiyyatan wa salaman

translation.en: and (please) convey our greetings and compliments to him

segments.arabic: وَآتِنَا مِنْ لَدُنْكَ فِي مُوَالاَتِهِ فَضْلاً وَإِحْسَاناً
segments.transliteration: wa atina min ladunka fi muwalatihi fadlan wa ihsanan

translation.en: and, on account of our allegiance to his leadership, please grant us from You favor, kindness,
forgiveness, and pleasure.
You are verily the Lord of enormous favor.
[please do that] in the name of Your mercy, O most merciful of all those who show mercy.

text.en: You may then put your right and then left cheeks on the tomb, turn to the kiblah direction, offer the Prayer of Ziyarah, and supplicate for anything you want.
Then, you may recite the (famous) Tasbih al-Zahra' and say the following:

translation.en: O Allah, You have conveyed to me glad tidings
through Your Prophet and Messenger
Muhammad, Your peace be upon him and his Household,

segments.arabic: فَقُلْتَ: «وَبَشِّرِ ٱلَّذِينَ آمَنُوٱ أَنَّ لَهُمْ قَدَمَ صِدْقٍ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِمْ.»
segments.transliteration: faqulta wa bashshir alladhina amanu anna lahum qadama sidqin `inda rabbihim

translation.en: as You said, “And give good news to those who believe that theirs is a footing of firmness with their Lord.”

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ وَإِنِّي مُؤْمِنٌ بِجَمِيعِ أَنْبِيَائِكَ وَرُسُلِكَ صَلَوَاتُكَ عَلَيْهِمْ
segments.transliteration: allahumma wa inni mu'minun bijami`i anbiya'ika wa rusulika salawatuka `alayhim

translation.en: O Allah, I indeed believe in all Your Prophets and Messengers, Your blessings be upon them.
So, (please) do not cause me, after my recognition of them, to be in a situation
due to which You expose me in the presence of the Witnesses;

segments.arabic: بَلْ قِفْنِي مَعَهُمْ وَتَوَفَّنِي عَلَىٰ ٱلتَّصْدِيقِ بِهِمْ
segments.transliteration: bal qifni ma`ahum wa tawaffani `ala alttasdiqi bihim

translation.en: rather, (please) include me with them and make me die while I give them credence.
O Allah, You have also bestowed upon them exclusive honor from You
and You have ordered me to follow them.
O Allah, I am Your slave and visitor,
seeking Your nearness through visiting the brother of Your Messenger.

segments.arabic: وَعَلَىٰ كُلِّ مَأْتِيٍّ وَمَزُورٍ حَقٌّ لِمَنْ أَتَاهُ وَزَارَهُ
segments.transliteration: wa `ala kulli ma'tiyyin wa mazurin haqqun liman atahu wa zarahu

translation.en: Indeed, each visited person is expected to pay attention to his duty towards the one who visits and comes to him.
And You are the Best One to Whom one may come
and You are the most Generous One to be visited;

segments.arabic: فَأَسْأَلُكَ يَا اللَّّهُ يَا رَحْمٰنُ يَا رَحِيمُ
segments.transliteration: fa-as'aluka ya allahu ya rahmanu ya rahimu

translation.en: I therefore beseech You, O Allah, O All-beneficent, O All-merciful,
O All-magnanimous, O All-glorious,
O One, O Besought of all,

segments.arabic: يَا مَنْ لَمْ يَلِدْ وَلَمْ يُولَدْ
segments.transliteration: ya man lam yalid wa lam yulad

translation.en: O He Who never begets nor is He begotten,

segments.transliteration: wa lam yakun lahu kufwan ahadun

translation.en: and the like of Whom is none,

segments.transliteration: wa lam yattakhidh sahibatan wa la waladan

translation.en: O He Who has not betaken wife or child,

segments.arabic: أَنْ تُصَلِّيَ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ
segments.transliteration: an tusalliya `ala muhammadin wa ali muhammadin

translation.en: (I beseech You) to send blessings upon Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad,

segments.arabic: وَأَنْ تَجْعَلَ تُحْفَتَكَ إِيَّايَ مِنْ زِيَارَتِي أَخَا رَسُولِكَ
segments.transliteration: wa an taj`ala tuhfataka iyyaya min ziyarati akha rasulika

translation.en: to choose the gift that You present to me on account of my visiting Your Messenger’s brother
to be release of me from Hellfire,

segments.arabic: وَأَنْ تَجْعَلَنِي مِمَّنْ يُسَارِعُ فِي ٱلْخَيْرَاتِ
segments.transliteration: wa an taj`alani mimman yusari`u fi alkhayrati

translation.en: to include me with those who strive with one another in hastening to good deeds
and those who call on You with love and reverence,
and to make me of those who are humble before You.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ إِنَّكَ مَنَنْتَ عَلَيَّ بِزِيَارَةِ مَوْلاَيَ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ
segments.transliteration: allahumma innaka mananta `alayya biziyarati mawlaya `aliyyi bni abi talibin

translation.en: O Allah, You have indeed conferred upon me with the favor of visiting my master `Ali the son of Abu-Talib
and with the favor of my being loyal to his (Divinely commissioned) leadership and recognition of him;

segments.transliteration: faj`alni mimman yansuruhu wa yantasiru bihi

translation.en: so, (please) make me of those whom You give victory and whom You choose to triumph through them.
And (please) bestow me with the favor of making me support Your religion.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ وَٱجْعَلْنِي مِنْ شِيعَتِهِ وَتَوَفَّنِي عَلَىٰ دِينِهِ
segments.transliteration: allahumma waj`alni min shi`atihi wa tawaffani `ala dinihi

translation.en: O Allah, (please) include me with the adherents of him and cause me to die following his doctrine.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ أَوْجِبْ لِي مِنَ ٱلرَّحْمَةِ وَٱلرِّضْوَانِ
segments.transliteration: allahumma awjib li min alrrahmati walrridwani

translation.en: O Allah, (please) cause me to deserve mercy, pleasure,
forgiveness, favor,
and sustenance that is growing, legally gotten, and pleasant,

segments.arabic: مَا أَنْتَ أَهْلُهُ يَا أَرْحمَ ٱلرَّاحِمِينَ
segments.transliteration: ma anta ahluhu ya arhma alrrahimina

translation.en: (and make it) suitable to Your favoring, O most merciful of all those who show mercy!
All praise be to Allah the Lord of the worlds.

text.en: Two other ziyarah's of Imam Ali (peace be upon him) to be recited on the 27th of Rajab:
<ref=3:295>زیارة الرجبیة</ref>
<ref=3:296>الشكل الرابع من زيارة الإمام علي (ع)</ref>
27th Rajab -A'amaal & Dua'a

The twenty-seventh night of Rajab is the night on which Prophet
Muhammad was given the mission of promulgating the religion.This
highly blessed night is called laylat al-mab’ath,which stands for the
beginning of the Holy Prophet’s Mission of promulgating the true
religion of Almighty Allah.The recommended rites of this night are as
follows:

First: Shaykh al-Tusi, in his book of Misbah al-Mutahajjid,has
quoted Imam Muhammad al-Jawad(a.s.) as saying,“There is a night
in Rajab,which carries for people fortune that is more preferable than
whatever is subjected to sunlight. It is the twenty-seventh night of
Rajab.The next morning was the beginning of the Holy Prophet’s
prophethood.Verily, one who does acts of worship at this night will
have the reward of one who has worshipped Almighty Allah for sixty
years.” When the Imam (a.s.) was asked about the rites to be done at this night

he answered, “After you offer the Isha’ Prayer and take a
sleep, you will wake up at any hour before midnight. You may then
offer a prayer of twelve units in each of which you may recite Surah
<ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
 and any other lenient Surah from al-Mufassal, which
begins with Surah <ref=1:47>Al-Muhammad</ref>
 up to the end of the Holy
Qur’an. You should separate each couple of units with Taslim. After
accomplishing this prayer, you should recite Surahs <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
, <ref=1:113>Al-Falaq</ref>
, <ref=1:114>Al-Nas</ref>
, <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
, <ref=1:109>Al-Kafiroon</ref>
, <ref=1:97>Al-Qadr</ref> 
, and <ref=1:2:255>Ayah al-Kursi</ref>
each seven times. After all this, you may say the following
supplicatory prayer:

segments.arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ٱلَّذِي لَمْ يَتَّخِذْ وَلَداً
segments.transliteration: alhamdu lillahi alladhi lam yattakhidh waladan

translation.en: All praise be to Allah Who has not taken to Himself a son,

segments.arabic: وَلَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ شَرِيكٌ فِي ٱلْمُلْكِ
segments.transliteration: wa lam yakun lahu sharikun fi almulki

translation.en: Who has no partner in the Sovereignty

segments.arabic: وَلَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ وَلِيٌّ مِنَ ٱلذُّلِّ
segments.transliteration: wa lam yakun lahu waliyyun min aldhdhulli

translation.en: nor has He any protecting friend through dependence.
And magnify Him with all magnificence.
O Allah, verily I beseech You in the name of the situations of Your Dignity
on the pillars of Your Throne,
in the name of Your utmost mercy in Your Book,
in the name of Your Name—the Greatest, the Greatest, the Greatest,
in the name of Your Mention—the Highest, the Highest, the Highest,
and in the name of Your Words the perfect,
to bless Muhammad and his Household

segments.arabic: وَأَنْ تَفْعَلَ بِي مَا أَنْتَ أَهْلُهُ
segments.transliteration: wa an taf`ala bi ma anta ahluhu

translation.en: and do to me that which befits You.

text.en: You may then submit your desires.
It is also recommended to bathe oneself at this night

It is worth
reminding that a prayer that is advisably offered at this night has been
mentioned with the rites of the fifteenth night of Rajab.

Second: The best act that is ever done at this night is to visit the
holy tomb of Imam Ali ibn Abi-Talib(a.s.). As will be mentioned in
the section of Ziyarat, three formulas of Ziyarah will be mentioned for
visiting the tomb of <ref=3:1018>Imam Ali Ameer al-Momineen(a.s.)</ref>
 at this night.

Third: In al-Balad al-Amin, al-Kaf’ami has instructed the
following:
Say the following supplicatory prayer on the Divine Mission Night:

translation.en: O Allah: I beseech You in the name of the Grandest Manifestation

segments.arabic: فِي هذِهِ اللَّيْلَةِ مِنَ الشَّهْرِ الْمُعَظَّمِ،
segments.transliteration: fi hadhihi allaylati mina alshshahri almu`azzmi

translation.en: at this night in this glorified month
and in the name of the honored Messenger,

text.en: that You send blessings upon Muhammad and his Household

segments.arabic: وَأَنْ تَغْفِرَ لَنَا مَا أَنْتَ بِهِ مِنَّا أَعْلَمُ،
segments.transliteration: wa an taghfira lana ma anta bihi minna a`lamu

translation.en: and that You forgive us that which You know better than we do.

segments.transliteration: ya man ya`lamu wa la na`lamu

translation.en: O He Who knows while we do not.

segments.arabic: اللّهُمَّ بَارِكْ لَنَا فِي لَيْلَتِنَا هذِهِ
segments.transliteration: allahumma barik lana fi laylatina hadhihi

translation.en: O Allah: (please do) bless us at this night
which You have exalted with the honor of the Divine Mission,
and You have esteemed with Your honoring,
and You have raised to the Honorable Place.
O Allah: We thus beseech You in the name of the honorable mission,
the gentle master,
and the chaste line
that You may bless Muhammad and his Household

segments.arabic: وَأَنْ تَجْعَلَ أَعْمَالَنَا فِي هذِهِ اللَّيْلَةِ
segments.transliteration: wa an taj`ala a`malana fi hadhihi allaylati

translation.en: and that You decide our deeds at this night,
as well as all the other nights, as acceptable,
our sins as forgiven,
our good efforts as praiseworthy,
our evildoings as concealed,
our hearts as pleased with decent wording,

segments.transliteration: wa arzaqana min ladunka biliyusri madruratan

translation.en: and our sustenance that You decide for us as copiously flowing with easiness.

segments.transliteration: allahumma innaka tara wa la tura

translation.en: O Allah: Verily You see [everything] but nothing can see You
while You are in the Highest Prospect.
And, verily, to You are the [final] return and goal.
And, verily, to You are causing to death and granting of life.
And to You are the Last and the First [of all things].

segments.arabic: اللّهُمَّ إنَّا نَعُوذُ بِكَ أَنْ نَذِلَّ وَنَخْزَى،
segments.transliteration: allahumma inna na`udhu bika an nadhilla wa nakhza

translation.en: O Allah: we, surely, seek Your protection against meeting of disgrace and shame
and against committing that which You have ordered us not to do.
O Allah: we, surely, pray You for Paradise, out of Your mercy,
and we seek Your protection against Hellfire.
So, (please do) save us from it, in the name of Your omnipotence.
We, also, pray You for the women of Paradise;
so, please do grant us them, in the name of Your might.

segments.arabic: وَاجْعَلْ أَوْسَعَ أَرْزَاقِنَا عِنْدَ كِبَرِ سِنِّنَا،

translation.en: And (please do) make the most expansive of our sustenance be given to us when we become old aged,
and make the best of our deeds be at the time close to our death.
And (please do) extend [our ages and make us spend them] in acts of obedience to You,
and in all that which draws us near to You,
and in all that which allow us to enjoy a good position with You,
and in all that which make our lives acceptable by You.

segments.arabic: وَأَحْسِنْ فِي جَمِيعِ أَحْوَالِنَا وَأُمُورِنَا مَعْرِفَتَنَا،
segments.transliteration: wa ahsin fi jami`i ahwal-ina wa a’umurina ma`rifatana

translation.en: And (please do) improve our recognition of You under all circumstance and conditions.

segments.arabic: وَلا تَكِلْنَا إلَى أَحَدٍ مِنْ خَلْقِكَ فَيَمُنَّ عَلَيْنَا،
segments.transliteration: wa la takilna ila ahadin min khalqika fayamunn `alayna

translation.en: And do not refer us to any of Your beings lest they shall always remind us of their favors to us.

segments.arabic: وَتَفَضَّلْ عَلَيْنَا بِجَمِيعِ حَوَائِجِنَا لِلدُّنْيَا وَالآخِرَةِ،
segments.transliteration: wa tafazl `alayna bijami`i hawa’ijina lilddnia wal-akhirati

translation.en: And please favor us by settling all our needs, in this world as well as the Next World.
And (please do) do the same thing to our forefathers, our descendants,
and all our brethren-in-faith;
with regard to all that which we have besought You for ourselves
O the most Merciful of all those who show mercy.
O Allah: we, verily, beseech You by Your Grand Name
and Your Eternal Kingdom

segments.arabic: أَنْ تُصَلِّيَ عَلَى مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ
segments.transliteration: an tusalliya `ala muhammadin wa ali muhammadin

translation.en: to send blessings upon Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad

segments.transliteration: wa an taghfira lana aldhdhanba al`azima

translation.en: and to forgive our grave sins,

segments.arabic: إنَّهُ لا يَغْفِرُ الْعَظِيمَ إلاّ الْعَظِيمُ.
segments.transliteration: innhu la yaghfiru al`azima illa al`azimu

translation.en: for none can forgive the grave save the All-great.
O Allah: this is Rajab, the honored month,

segments.arabic: الَّذِي أَكْرَمْتَنَا بِهِ أَوَّلُ أَشْهُرِ الْحُرُمِ،
segments.transliteration: alladhy akramtana bihi awulu ash-huri alhurumi

translation.en: which You have honored us through it, being the first of the Sacred Months,
and You have selected us for this honor from amongst the other nations.

segments.arabic: فَلَكَ الْحَمْدُ يَا ذَا الْجُودِ وَالْكَرَمِ،

translation.en: So, all praise be to You; O the Lord of Magnanimity and Generosity.
I thus beseech You by Your Name,
the grandest, the grandest, the grandest,
the most majestic, the most honorable,
that You have created so that it has settled under Your Shadow
and it thus shall never transfer to anyone other than You—

segments.arabic: أَنْ تُصَلِّيَ عَلَى مُحَمَّدٍ وَأَهْلِ بَيْتِهِ الطَّاهِرِينَ،
segments.transliteration: an tusalliya `ala muhammadin wa ahli baytihi alttahirina

translation.en: I beseech You to send blessings upon Muhammad and his Household, the immaculate,

segments.arabic: وَأَنْ تَجْعَلَنَا مِنَ الْعَامِلِينَ فِيهِ بِطَاعَتِكَ،
segments.transliteration: wa an taj`alana mina al`amilina fihi bita`atika

translation.en: and to include us, during this month, with those who practice acts of obedience to You
and those who hope for Your admission.
O Allah: (please do) lead us to the Right Path,
and choose for us the best resting-place with You,
under a dense shade
and abundant possession.
Surely, You are Sufficient for us! Most Excellent are You, and in You do we trust!
O Allah: (please do) make us return (to You) with success and victory;
neither accursed (by You) nor misleading (the Right Path),
out of Your mercy; O the most Merciful of all those who show mercy.
O Allah: I earnestly beseech You, in the name of the matters that bring about Your forgiveness
and the matters that incumbently achieve Your mercy,
to grant me safety from all sins,
gain of all decencies,
attainment of Paradise,
redemption against Hellfire.
O Allah: In the same way as suppliants have besought You, I am beseeching You.
And in the same way as implorers have begged You, I am begging You.

segments.transliteration: wa talaba ilayka alttalibuna wa talabtu ilayka

translation.en: And in the same way as requesters have asked You, I am asking You.
O Allah: You are (my) trust and hope;

segments.transliteration: wa ilayka muntaha alrrghbati fi alddu`a‘i

translation.en: and You are the ultimate desire in my supplications.
O Allah: send blessings upon Muhammad and his Household
and establish conviction in my heart,
light in my sight,
well-wishing in my chest,
and the mention of You on my tongue day and night,

segments.arabic: وَرِزْقاً وَاسِعاً غَيْرَ مَمْنُونٍ وَلا مَحْظُورٍ فَارْزُقْنِي،
segments.transliteration: wa rizqan wasi`an ghayra mamnunin wa la mahzurin farzuqni

translation.en: and expansive sustenance that is neither failing nor banned. So, please provide me sustenance
and bless that which You decide for me,
and make me self-sufficient,
and make me desire for none save You;
out of Your mercy, O the most Merciful of all those who show mercy.

text.en:
