You may then prostrate yourself and repeat the following litany one
hundred times:

translation.en: All praise be to Allah Who has guided us to the recognition of Him,
has included us exclusively with the protection of Him,
and has granted us the obedience to Him.
Thanks, thanks.

text.en:
When you raise your head (i.e. finish the prostration), you may say
the following:

translation.en: O Allah: I am turning my face toward You asking for the settlement of my needs.
And I depend upon You in submitting my problem.

segments.transliteration: wa tawajjhtu ilayka bi-a’immaty wa sadati

translation.en: And I submit before You my Imams and Masters.
O Allah: benefit us by our love for them,
and make us follow their examples,
and grant us their companionship,
and allow us to enter Paradise with their group;
by Your mercy, O the most Merciful of all those who show mercy.

text.en:
Sayyid Ibn Tawus has mentioned that this supplicatory prayer is
said on the Divine Mission Day (i.e. the twenty-seventh of Rajab).
Dua recited after Every Obligatory Prayer 'Yaman Arjohu'
Dua Ya Man Arjuhu Li Kulli Khair
It has been narrated from Imam Jafar Sadiq (peace be upon him); You may say the following prayer each day in Rajab; in mornings, evenings, and after each of the obligatory prayers:

translation.en: O He from whom I hope for every good
and seek safety from His displeasure in the face of every evil!
O He who gives abundantly for that which is trivial!
O He who grants to whomever requests Him!

segments.arabic: يَا مَنْ يُعْطي مَنْ لَمْ يَسْأَلْهُ وَمَنْ لَمْ يَعْرِفْهُ
segments.transliteration: ya man yu`ti man lam yas'alhu wa man lam ya`rifhu

translation.en: O He Who gives those who do not ask Him and do not recognize Him
out of His kindness and mercifulness!

segments.arabic: أَعْطِنِي بِمَسْأَلتِي إِيَّاكَ جَمِيعَ خَيْرِ ٱلدُّنْيَا
segments.transliteration: a`tini bimas'alati iyyaka jami`a khayri alddunya

translation.en: (Please do) give me, by virtue of my petition to You, all the welfare of the world
and all the welfare of the Hereafter
and save me, by virtue of my petition to You,

segments.transliteration: jami`a sharri alddunya wa sharri al-akhirati

translation.en: all the evil of the world and the evil of the Hereafter
For most certainly, what You give is not deficient

segments.transliteration: wa zidni min fadlika ya karimu

translation.en: and increase for me out of Your grace, O All-munificient One!

text.en: The narrator then added that Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) then grasped his beard with his left hand, while saying this supplicatory prayer, and moved ceaselessly the forefinger of his right hand. He then added:

translation.en: O Majestic and Munificent One!
O All generous Bestower of blessings!
O Dispenser of favours and bounties!
(Please do) forbid (save) my white-hair beard from the Hellfire.

text.en: Duas to be recited everyday in the Month of Rajab
1. Dua Ya Man Yamliku Hawa'ija

1. It is advisable to say the following prayer each day in Rajab. It has been narrated that Imam Zayn al-Abideen (peace be upon him) used to say this prayer on the first day of Rajab while he was imprisoned:

translation.en: O He who possesses the means to fulfill the needs of the supplicants,
and is aware of that which is hidden in the heart of the silent!
For You hear every problem instantly,
and respond immediately.
O Allah, Your promises come true,
Your favors are many,
and Your Mercy is extensive,

segments.arabic: فَأَسْأَلُكَ أَنْ تُصَلِّيَ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ
segments.transliteration: fa-as'aluka an tusalliya `ala muhammadin wa ali muhammadin

translation.en: therefore, I beseech You to send blessings to Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad,

segments.transliteration: wa an taqdiya hawa'iji lilddunya wal-akhirati

translation.en: and to fulfill my desires for the world and the Hereafter.
Most certainly, You have power over all things.

text.en: 2. DUA KHAABAL WAAFIDOON
2. It is recommended to say the following supplicatory prayer that Imam Ja`far al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) used to say everyday in Rajab:

translation.en: Those who approach other than You are disappointed.
Those who try to gain the favour of other than You are losers;
those who make other than You the goal of their journey perish;

segments.arabic: وَأَجْدَبَ ٱلْمُنْتَجِعُونَ إِلاَّ مَنِ ٱنْتَجَعَ فَضْلَكَ
segments.transliteration: wa ajdaba almuntaji`una illa man intaja`a fadlaka

translation.en: and all except those who seek resort in Your grace face desolation
Your door is open for the seekers
and your boons are given to those who seek them,
and Your grace is freely available to those who ask for it,
Access to You is granted to those who hope for it!
Your sustenance is extended for even those who disobey You.
Your forbearance extends to those who antagonize You.
Your habit is to do favor to the wrongdoers
and Your way is to be benevolent to the transgressors.
O Allah, so (lovingly) guide me with guidance enjoyed by the rightly-guided,
and enable me to strive like the diligent,

segments.transliteration: wa la taj`alni mina alghafilina almub`adina

translation.en: do not place me with the negligent who are kept at a distance from You
and forgive me on the Day of Retribution.

text.en: 3. DUA ALLAHUMMA INNI ASALUKA SABRUS SHAKEREEN

3. In his book of al-Misbah, Shaykh al-Tusi mentions that al-Mu`alla ibn Khunays has reported Imam Ja`far al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) as saying, “In Rajab, you may say the following supplicatory prayer; It has been also mentioned by Sayyid Ibn Tawus al-Hasani in his book of Iqbal al-A`mal. According to the narration, this supplicatory prayer is the most comprehensive and it is advisable to repeat it any time.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ إنّي أَسْأَلُكَ صَبْرَ ٱلشَّاكِرِينَ لَكَ
segments.transliteration: allahumma inni as'aluka sabra alshshakirina laka

translation.en: O Allah, I implore You to grant me the patience of those who always thank You,
the efforts of those who fear You,
and the conviction of those who worship You devoutly.
O Allah, You are the Most High, the All-Supreme,
and I am Your servant—the destitute and the needy.
You are the Self-Sufficient, the Praised,
and I am the abject and lowly servant.
O Allah, bless Muhammad and his Household
and favour me with Your sufficiency upon my poverty,
and with Your forbearance upon my ignorance,
and with Your strength on my weakness,
O All-Strong, O Almighty.
O Allah, bless Muhammad and his Household;
the Successors (of prophet hood) and the Pleased;

segments.arabic: وَٱكْفِنِي مَا أَهَمَّنِي مِنْ أَمْرِ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَٱلآخِرَةِ
segments.transliteration: wakfini ma ahammani min amri alddunya wal-akhirati

translation.en: and suffice me from whatever aggrieves me from the affairs pertaining to the world and the Hereafter.
O most Merciful of all those who show mercy!

text.en: 4. DUA ALLAHUMMA YA ZAL MINAN
4. Shaykh al-Tusi has also mentioned that the following prayer is repeated each day in Rajab:

This supplication has been recorded by Sayyed Ibn Taawoos in ‘Iqbaal al-A’maal’ from the book of ‘Maalem al-Deen’ from Muhammad Ibn Abi al-Rawaad al-Rawaasi from Muhammad Ibn Ja’far al-Dehaan from Imam-e-Zamaan (peace be upon him).
This supplication comprises of concepts concerning divine unity.
He has described Allah, Mighty and Glorified be He, in a manner which is impossible for us to describe.
Reference: Iqbaal al-A'maal, p. 644; Al-Mazaar al-Kabeer, p. 179; Misbaah al-Zaaer, p. 56; Behaar al-Anwaar, vol. 98, p. 391; Saheefah al-Mahdi, p. 188.
(This supplicatory prayer is also said in Masjid Sa’sa ah.)

translation.en: O Allah, the Lord of the complete favors,
the utter bounties,
the immense mercy,
and all-encompassing power,
and the immense blessings,
and the great gifts,
and the elegant boons,
and plentiful rewards!
O He Who cannot be described by any analogy,
nor compared with any like,
and nor overcome with the help of any supporter.
O He Who created and then granted sustenance,
and inspired (them) and then endowed them with speech,
and originated (the creation) and then initiated it,
transcended it and then surmounted it,
and determined it in an excellent form,
and then formed it in a perfect manner,
and advanced conclusive proofs,
and blessed abundantly,
and rewarded plenteously,
and conferred gracious gifts!
O He Who is so lofty in might
so he eludes the gaze of the creatures' eyes,
and so close in affection
so as to ascend all notions of thought!
O He Who is the sole sovereign of His dominion

segments.arabic: فَلاَ نِدَّ لَهُ فِي مَلَكُوتِ سُلْطَانِهِ
segments.transliteration: fala nidda lahu fi malakuti sultanihi

translation.en: that no peer can ever be found for Him in the realms of His power
and has been so matchless in bounties and supremacy

segments.arabic: فَلاَ ضِدَّ لَهُ فِي جَبَرُوتِ شَأْنِهِ
segments.transliteration: fala didda lahu fi jabaruti sha'nihi

translation.en: that nothing can oppose Him in the omnipotence of His situation!

segments.arabic: يَا مَنْ حَارَتْ فِي كِبْرِيَاءِ هَيْبَتِهِ دَقَائِقُ لَطَائِفِ ٱلأَوْهَامِ
segments.transliteration: ya man harat fi kibriya‘i haybatihi daqa’iqu lata’ifi al-awhami

translation.en: O He the greatness of whose awe, bewilders the intricate subtleties of the imagination

segments.arabic: وَٱنْحَسَرَتْ دُونَ إِدْرَاكِ عَظَمَتِهِ خَطَائِفُ أَبْصَارِ ٱلأَنَامِ
segments.transliteration: wanhasarat duna idraki `azamatihi khata’ifu absari al-anami

translation.en: and before Whose greatness are wearied the glances of the eyes of the worlds inhabitants!
O He before Whose Awe all faces have humbled themselves,
to His greatness all necks bend,
and His fear makes the hearts quake!

segments.arabic: أَسْأَلُكَ بِهٰذِهِ ٱلْمِدْحَةِ ٱلَّتِي لا تَنْبَغِي إِلاَّ لَكَ
segments.transliteration: as'aluka bihadhihi almidhati allati la tanbaghi illa laka

translation.en: I beseech You in the name of this piece of praise which suits none but You,

segments.arabic: وَبِمَا وَأَيْتَ بِهِ عَلَىٰ نَفْسِكَ لِدَاعِيكَ مِنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ
segments.transliteration: wa bima wa'ayta bihi `ala nafsika lida`ika mina almu'minina

translation.en: and by the promise which You have made incumbent upon Yourself for the believers who call upon You,

segments.arabic: وَبِمَا ضَمِنْتَ ٱلإِجَابَةَ فِيهِ عَلَىٰ نَفْسِكَ لِلدَّاعِينَ
segments.transliteration: wa bima daminta al-ijabata fihi `ala nafsika lildda`ina

translation.en: and by the assurance You have given to answer the appeals of those who supplicate to You.
O the sharpest of listeners!
and the keenest of observers!
and the swiftest of reckoners!
O Powerful and All-strong!

segments.arabic: صَلِّ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ خَاتَمِ ٱلنَّبِيِّينَ وَعَلَىٰ أَهْلِ بَيْتِهِ
segments.transliteration: salli `ala muhammadin khatami alnnabiyyina wa `ala ahli baytihi

translation.en: (Please do) bless Muhammad, the seal of the Prophets, and his Household,

segments.arabic: وَٱقْسِمْ لِي فِي شَهْرِنَا هٰذَا خَيْرَ مَا قَسَمْتَ
segments.transliteration: waqsim li fi shahrina hadha khayra ma qasamta

translation.en: and allot for me in this month of ours the best of what You have allotted to anyone,

segments.arabic: وَٱحْتِمْ لِي فِي قَضَائِكَ خَيْرَ مَا حَتَمْتَ
segments.transliteration: wahtim li fi qada'ika khayra ma hatamta

translation.en: and ordain for me in Your ordainments the best of what You have ordained for anyone,
seal my life with happiness with which You seal the lives of others,
Give me a prosperous life, so long as You keep me alive,
and grant me to die happy and forgiven,

segments.arabic: وَتَوَلَّ أَنْتَ نَجَاتِي مِنْ مُسَاءَلَةِ ٱلبَرْزَخِ
segments.transliteration: wa tawalla anta najati min musa'alati albarzakhi

translation.en: You assume the charge of my deliverance during the questioning in the purgatory,
and spare me from Munkar and Nakir,
and make Mubashshir and Bashir appear before my eyes,

segments.arabic: وَٱجْعَلْ لِي إِلَىٰ رِضْوَانِكَ وَجِنَانِكَ مَصِيراً
segments.transliteration: waj`al li ila ridwanika wa jinanika masiran

translation.en: let my return be toward Your pleasure and paradise,
and grant me a happy life,
and a big kingdom,

segments.transliteration: wa salli `ala muhammadin wa alihi kathiran

translation.en: and bless Muhammad and his Household very much.

text.en: 5. DUA ALLAHUMMA INNI ASALUKA BI MA’AANI
5. Shaykh al-Tusi has narrated that the following holy Signature (Tawqee) – Signed Letter of Imam Mahdi (atfs) was conveyed to his representative Shaykh Abu-Ja`far Muhammad ibn `Uthman ibn Sa`eed:
On each day in Rajab, you may say the following prayer:

translation.en: In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ إنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ بِمَعَانِي جَمِيعِ مَا يَدْعُوكَ بِهِ وُلاةُ أَمْرِكَ
segments.transliteration: allahumma inni as'aluka bima`ani jami`i ma yad`uka bihi wulatu amrika

translation.en: O Allah, I beseech You by the meanings of all the supplications of those whom You have invested with Your authority,
the trustees of Your secret,
theose who rejoice in Your command,
the describers of Your power,
the proclaimers of Your supremacy.

segments.arabic: أَسْأَلُكَ بِمَا نَطَقَ فِيهِمْ مِنْ مَشِيئَتِكَ
segments.transliteration: as'aluka bima nataqa fihim min mashi'atika

translation.en: I implore You by Your will that has manifested itself through them
and thus You have made them the essences of Your Words,
the pillars of Your Oneness,

segments.arabic: وَآيَاتِكَ وَمَقَامَاتِكَ الَّتِي لا تَعْطِيلَ لَهَا فِي كُلِّ مَكَانٍ
segments.transliteration: wa ayatika wa maqamatika allati la ta`tila laha

translation.en: Your Signs and Stations, which are never inoperative in any place.

segments.transliteration: fi kulli makanin ya`rifuka biha man `arafaka

translation.en: Through them, You are known to those who know You.

segments.arabic: لا فَرْقَ بَيْنَكَ وَبَيْنَهَا إِلاَّ أَنَّهُمْ عِبَادُكَ وَخَلْقُكَ
segments.transliteration: la farqa baynaka wa baynaha illa annahum `ibaduka wa khalquka

translation.en: #NAME?
their unstitching and mending is in Your hand
they enamate from You and return to You.
They are the instruments, witnesses,
planners, defenders
guards and scouts
by whose means You have filled Your heavens and earth

segments.arabic: حَتَّىٰ ظَهَرَ أَنْ لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ
segments.transliteration: hatta zahara an la ilaha illa anta

translation.en: until it became manifest that there is no god save You.
I thus implore You in the name of all this,
and by the recipients of honor and might by your mercy,
and by Your stations and Signs,
to send blessings to Muhammad and his Household

segments.transliteration: wa an tazidani imanan wa tathbitan

translation.en: and increase me in faith and fortitude.
O He Who is Immanent in His Evidence!

segments.transliteration: wa zahiran fi butunihi wa maknunihi

translation.en: O He Who is Evident in His Immanence and in His hiddenness!
O He Who sets apart light from murk!
O He Whom is described without recognition of his real essence!
and He Whom is recognized without having anything like Him!
O Edger of all edged things!
O Witness of all done things!
O Bringer into being all existent things!
O Knower of the number of all countable things!
O Knower of the place of all lost things!
None is worshipped except You.
You are the worthiest of all pride and magnanimity.
O He about Whom, it is infeasible to as ‘how’
and it is infeasible to ask ‘where’!
O He Who is concealed from all sights!

segments.arabic: يَا دَيْمُومُ يَا قَيُّومُ وَعَالِمَ كُلِّ مَعْلُومٍ
segments.transliteration: ya daymumu ya qayyumu wa `alima kulli ma`lumin

translation.en: O Everlasting, O Self-Subsistent, O Knower of all known things!

segments.arabic: صَلِّ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِهِ وَعَلَىٰ عِبَادِكَ ٱلْمُنْتَجَبِينَ
segments.transliteration: salli `ala muhammadin wa alihi wa `ala `ibadika almuntajabina

translation.en: (Please do) send blessings to Muhammad, his Household, Your selected servants,
Your concealed human beings,
Your favorite angels,
and the Angels who draw themselves out in ranks and who throng (round the Divine Throne),

segments.arabic: وَبَارِكْ لَنَا فِي شَهْرِنَا هٰذَا ٱلْمُرَجَّبِ ٱلْمُكَرَّمِ
segments.transliteration: wa barik lana fi shahrina hadha almurajjabi almukarrami

translation.en: and bless us in this month—the honored and dignified—

segments.transliteration: wa ma ba`dahu min al-ashhuri alhurumi

translation.en: as well as the next sacred months,
pour upon us Your graces in this month,
grant us abundant shares of blessings,
admit our requests,
in the name of Your Name, the Greatest, the Greatest, the Loftiest, the Most Honored
that You put on daylight and it thus lit up
and You put on night and it thus darkened,

segments.arabic: وَٱغْفِرْ لَنَا مَا تَعْلَمُ مِنَّا وَمَا لا نَعْلَمُ
segments.transliteration: waghfir lana ma ta`lamu minna wa ma la na`lamu

translation.en: forgive us all that which You know about us and all that which we do not know,
protect us against sins with the best of protection,
save us against the acts that You predetermine,
bestow upon us with Your superb looking,
do not leave us in the hands of anyone other than You,
do not prevent us from Your goodness,

segments.arabic: وَبَارِكْ لَنَا فِيمَا كَتَبْتَهُ لَنَا مِنْ أَعْمَارِنَا
segments.transliteration: wa barik lana fima katabtahu lana min a`marina

translation.en: bless us during the age that You have decided for us,
make righteous for us our inner selves,
grant us Your security,
make us enjoy perfect faith,
and make us catch the month of fasting

segments.transliteration: wa ma ba`dahu mina al-ayyami wal-a`wami

translation.en: and the days and years coming after,
O Lord of Majesty and Honor!

text.en: 6. DUA ALLAHUMMA INNI ASALUKA BIL-MAWLOODAIN

Shaykh al-Tusi (a.r.)has also narrated that a document signed by Imam al-Mahdi (peace be upon him) was received by by Shaikh Abu al-Qasim Husain Ibn Rawh al-Nawbakhti (r.a.), Imam al-Mahdi’(peace be upon him)s representative, comprising that the following prayer is recommended to be said in the month of Rajab:

In this supplication, we swear upon Allah, Mighty and Glorified be He, for the sake of the two blessed births that took place in this month, Imam al-Jawaad (peace be upon him) and Imam al-Haadi (peace be upon him). We also seek forgiveness and pardon from Him and that He metes out unto us a favourable end.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ إنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ بِٱلْمَوْلُودَيْنِ فِي رَجَبٍ
segments.transliteration: allahumma inni as'aluka bilmawludayni fi rajabin

translation.en: O Allah, I beseech You in the name of those who were born in Rajab,
Muhammad ibn `Ali the second
and his son `Ali ibn Muhammad the divinely selected;

segments.transliteration: wa ataqarrabu bihima ilayka khayra alqurabi

translation.en: I seek nearness to You through both of them for they are the best means of nearness to You.
O He from Whom favors are sought
and what He possesses is desired!
I beseech You with the entreaty of him
whom is enchained by his sins
and is bound by his defects;
therefore, he devoted himself to offenses,
and disasters have attacked him heavily.
He is now begging You for accepting his repentance,
good return from You,
redeeming from punishment,
releasing from Hellfire,
and overlooking the sins that are hanged to him.

segments.transliteration: fa-anta mawlaya a`zamu amalihi wa thiqatihi

translation.en: Certainly, You, O my Master, the greatest of all hopes and desires he has.
O Allah, I beg You in the name of the holy means that You admit
and all of the elevated courses that take to You

segments.arabic: أَنْ تَتَغَمَّدَنِي فِي هٰذَا ٱلشَّهْرِ بِرَحْمَةٍ مِنْكَ وَاسِعَةٍ
segments.transliteration: an tataghammadani fi hadha alshshahri birahmatin minka wasi`atin

translation.en: to encompass me, in this month, with vast mercy of You,
abundant favors,
and satisfaction with what You have decided for me
until I am put in my final hole,
which is my abode to the Hereafter
and the Final Fate.

text.en: SALAAH FOR EVERY NIGHT IN RAJAB

It is recommended to offer a sixty-unit prayer in Rajab. Each night, it is recommended to offer two units of this prayer in the first unit of which Surah <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
 is recited once, Surah <ref=1:109>Al-Kafiroon</ref>
 repeated three times, and Surah <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
 recited one time. After the accomplishment of the two prayer units, it is recommended to raise the hands towards the sky and say the following:

translation.en: There is no god save Allah,
the One and Only Who has no partner.
To Him is the kingdom and to Him is all praise.
He restores to live and cause to die
and He is Ever-living and He never dies.
In His Hands is all goodness,
He has power over all things,
and to Him is the return of all things.

segments.arabic: وَلاَ حَوْلَ وَلاَ قُوَّةَ إِلاَّ بِٱللَّهِ ٱلْعَلِيِّ ٱلْعَظِيمِ
segments.transliteration: wa la hawla wa la quwwata illa billahi al`aliyyi al`azimi

translation.en: There is neither might nor strength save with Allah, the Most High, the All-great.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ ٱلنَّبِيِّ ٱلْأُمِّيِّ وَآلِهِ
segments.transliteration: allahumma salli `ala muhammadin alnnabiyyi al-ummiyyi wa alihi

translation.en: O Allah, (please do) bless Muhammad, the Ummi Prophet, and his Household.

text.en: It is also recommended to pass one’s hand over the face while reciting the last sentence of this supplication.

The Holy Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) is reported to have said that if one offers this prayer and recites this supplication, Almighty Allah will respond to his needs and will award him the reward of sixth times of Hajj and `Umrah.
Lailatul Raghaib' - First Thursday Night of Rajab

The first Thursday night of Rajab is considered significant, especially in making your relation with Allah stronger. Apart from the fast and prayer, scholars also recommend seeking Allah's pleasure by serving His creations. The Holy Prophet (s.a) is reported to have said, that these prayers are Means of forgiveness and on the first night in our grave, the Almighty will send the reward of this prayer in the best, enlightened and eloquent form.
It is recommended to fast on the first Thursday
12 Rakat Salat Between Maghrib & Isha*
*Note : Though the salat is mentioned in Iqbal Amal , it is narrated by Anas bin Malik who was an opponent to the Ahlulbayt(as) & hence authenticity is not verified
12 Rak’at salaat in six sets of 2 units.(with niyat/intention of Rija Matloob see * below )

In every Rak’at after Surah <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
 recite 3 times Surah <ref=1:97>Al-Qadr</ref> 
 & 12 times Surah <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
.
After completing the full prayer recite 70 times: :
Recite 70 times:

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ ٱلنَّبِيِّ ٱلامِّيِّ وَعَلَىٰ آلِهِ
segments.transliteration: Allahumma s’ale a’laa muh’ammadin nabiyyil ummi wa a’laa aaleh.

translation.en: O Allah send blessings on Muhammad, the Ummi Prophet, and on his descendants.

text.en: Then go into sajdah & recite 70 times:

translation.en: Holy and most Holy is the Lord of the Angels and spirits.

text.en: Then sit erect & say 70 times:

segments.arabic: رَبِّ ٱغْفِرْ وَٱرْحَمْ وَتَجَاوَزْ عَمَّا تَعْلَمُ إنَّكَ انْتَ ٱلْعَلِيُّ ٱلاعْظَمُ
segments.transliteration: rabbighfir war-ham watajaawaz a’mma ta’lamo innaka antal a’liyyul a’zam

translation.en: O Lord! Forgive, have mercy and be indulgent about that which Thou knows well, verily Thou art Sublime, Mighty.

text.en: Then go into sajdah & say 70 times:

translation.en: Holy and most Holy is the Lord of Angels and spirits.

text.en: Then he spent asking his need, God willing.

name: Merits of Rajab and general amaal

text.en: Merits of Rajab

Be it known to you that in addition to the months of Sha’ban and
Ramadan, the month of Rajab enjoys a great honor, as cited in many
narrations that have also conveyed the merits of this month.
Furthermore, the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.a.) is reported as saying,
“Verily, Rajab is the Great Month of Allah. No other month can be as
sacred and virtuous as this month. To fight against the polytheists
during this month is forbidden. Most certainly, Rajab is the month of
Allah, Sha’ban is my month, and Ramadan is the month of my people.
Therefore, if one observes fasting on one day of this month, he will
bindingly win the Grand Pleasure of Almighty Allah, will be taken
away from His ire, and the gates of Hellfire will be closed in his face.

Imam Musa ibn Ja’far al-Kazim (a.s.) is also reported to have said,
“If one observes fasting on one day in Rajab, Hellfire will go away
from him a distance of one year walking. One who fasts three days
will bindingly win Paradise.”

The Imam (a.s.) is also reported as saying, “Rajab is (the name of) a
river in Paradise that is whiter than milk and sweeter than honey.
Hence, if one fasts even a single day in Rajab, Almighty Allah will
allow him to drink from that river.”

Imam Ja’far al-Sadiq(a.s.) has reported the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.a.)
as saying: Rajab is the month of imploring for forgiveness for my
people; so, you should implore forgiveness repeatedly in this month
much more than you do in other months, for Almighty Allah is
forgiving and merciful. This month is described as the advantageous
month, because mercy is poured on my people during it so heavily.
You should thus repeat very much the following imploration prayer:

translation.en: I implore for Allah’s forgiveness and I pray to Him for accepting of my repentance.

text.en:
In a considerable form of narration, Ibn Baabwayh has quoted
Salim as saying: I once visited Imam al-Sadiq(a.s.) in the last days of
Rabaj. When his eyes turned towards me, he asked, “Have you fasted
on any day in this month, Salim?”
“No, I have not,”

answered I.
“You have missed great reward,” said the Imam (a.s.). “Such a
great reward that none can ever grant except Almighty Allah. Verily,
this month has been given precedence to the other months by
Almighty Allah Who has also given its sanctity a great consideration
and will have certainly honored those who observe fasting during it.”
I then said, “O son of Allah’s Messenger, what if I fast on the rest
days of Rajab, will I then be awarded anything of the rewards
dedicated to those fasting in it?”

Imam al-Sadiq(a.s.) answered, “Listen, Salim! If one observes
fasting on the last day of this month, this will secure him against the
agonies of death, the horror of the resurrection, and the grave
chastisement. As for one who observes fasting on the last two days of
this month, this will give him permission to pass the Discriminating
Bridge (sirat) peacefully. As for one who observes fasting on the last
three days of the month, one shall be secured on the Grand Horror
Day against all the horrors and hardships thereon and shall be also
granted release from Hellfire.”

Finally, be it known to you that to observe fasting during the month
of Rajab wholly brings about great favors. However, it has been
narrated that one who is incapable of fasting during the month of
Rajab may repeat the following supplicatory prayer instead one
hundred times each day in Rajab:

translation.en: Glory be to the All-majestic God.

segments.arabic: سُبْحَانَ مَنْ لا يَنْبَغِي ٱلتَّسْبِيحُ إِلاَّ لَهُ
segments.transliteration: subhana man la yanbaghi alttasbihu illa lahu

translation.en: Glory be to Him other than Whom none should be glorified.
Glory be to the All-honorable and All-dignified.

segments.arabic: سُبْحَانَ مَنْ لَبِسَ ٱلْعِزَّ وَهُوَ لَهُ اهْلٌ
segments.transliteration: subhana man labisa al`izza wa huwa lahu ahlun

translation.en: Glory be to Him Who has dressed Himself with grandeur that fits none but Him.

text.en: 4.The Holy Prophet (saws) is reported as saying: Whoever repeats the following imploration for forgiveness 100 times in Rajab and immediately gives alms after it, Almighty Allah will conclude his life with mercy and forgiveness. Also, whoever repeats it 400 times, will be given the reward of one hundred martyrs:

segments.arabic: اسْتَغْفِرُ ٱللَّهَ ٱلَّذِي لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ هُوَ
segments.transliteration: Astaghfirullaha alladhy la ilaha illa huwa

translation.en: I implore the forgiveness of Allah. There is no god save Him,
the One and Only Who has no partner,
and I repent before Him.

text.en: 5.The Holy Prophet (saws) is also reported to have said: Whoever repeats this litany 1000 times in Rajab, Almighty Allah will decide for him 100,000 rewards and award him 100 houses in Paradise:

translation.en: There is no god but Allah.

text.en: 6.A Hadith reads that whoever repeats the following imploration for forgiveness 70 times in the morning and 70 times in the evening on each day in Rajab, will be admitted by Almighty Allah if he dies in Rajab and Hellfire will not touch him on account of the blessings of Rajab:

translation.en: I implore the forgiveness of Allah and I repent to Him.

text.en: After this repeat seventy times, raise the hands towards the sky and say:

translation.en: O Allah, (please do) forgive me and accept my repentance.

text.en: 7.It is recommended to implore Almighty Allah for forgiveness in the following form one thousand times in Rajab so that He will forgive:

translation.en: I implore Allah, the Lord of majesty and honor,

segments.transliteration: min jami i aldhunubi wa ithmi

translation.en: to forgive my all sins and offenses.

text.en:
In his book entitled Iqbal al-A’mal, Sayyid Ibn Tawus
has mentioned great rewards for those who repeat Surah al-Tawheed
ten thousand, one thousand, or one hundred times in Rajab. Likewise,
he has narrated that one who recites this Surah(of al-Tawheed) one
hundred times on Fridays in Rajab will enjoy, on the Resurrection
Day, light that draws him towards Paradise.

Sayyid Ibn Tawus has also narrated that one who fasts
on a day in Rajab and offers a four-unit prayer in the first unit of
which he repeats Ayah al-Kursi one hundred times and in the second,
Surah al-Tawheed two hundred times, will not depart life before he
sees (in sleep) his place in Paradise or someone else sees it on behalf
of him.

Sayyid Ibn Tawus has also reported from the Holy
Prophet (s.a.w.a.) that if one offer a four-unit prayer on Friday in
Rajab between the obligatory Zohr and Asr Prayers in each unit of
which Surah al-Faatehah is recited once, Ayah al-Kursi repeated seven
times, and Surah al-Tawheed five times; and then the following
imploration for forgiveness repeated ten times - if one offers this
prayer,

segments.arabic: أَسْتَغْفِرُ الله الَّذِي لا إِلهَ إِلاّ هُوَ وَأَسْأَلُهُ التَّوْبَةَ

translation.en: I implore for Allah’s forgiveness and I pray to Him for accepting of my repentance.

text.en:
Almighty Allah will decide for him since the day on which he
offered this prayer and up to the day of his death one thousand
rewards a day, a city built with ruby in Paradise for each verse (of the
Holy Qur’an) he recites, a palace built with white pearls in Paradise
for each letter he recites from the Holy Qur’an, Paradisiacal Women,
ultimate pleasure, inclusion with the (true) worshippers, seal his life
with happiness and forgiveness... etc.

It is recommended to fast on three days in the month of
Rajab: Thursday, Friday, and Saturday. It has been narrated that
whoever fasts on these three days in one of the Sacred Months will be
awarded with the reward of nine hundred year worship.

It is recommended to offer a sixty-unit prayer in
Rajab. Each night, it is recommended to offer two units of this prayer
in the first unit of which Surah al-Faatehah is recited once, Surah al-
Kafirun (No. 109) repeated three times, and Surah al-Tawheed recited
one time. After the accomplishment of the two prayer units, it is
recommended to raise the hands towards the sky and say the
following:

translation.en: There is no god but Allah,
the One and Only Who has no partner.
To Him is the kingdom and to Him is all praise.
He restores to live and cause to die
and He is Ever-living and He never dies.
In His Hands is all goodness,
He has power over all things,
and to Him is the return of all things.

segments.arabic: وَلاَ حَوْلَ وَلاَ قُوَّةَ إِلاَّ بِٱللَّهِ ٱلْعَلِيِّ ٱلْعَظِيمِ
segments.transliteration: wa la hawla wa la quwwata illa billahi al`aliyyi al`azimi

translation.en: There is neither might nor strength save with Allah, the Most High, the All-great.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ ٱلنَّبِيِّ ٱلْأُمِّيِّ وَآلِهِ
segments.transliteration: allahumma salli `ala muhammadin alnnabiyyi al-ummiyyi wa alihi

translation.en: O Allah, (please do) bless Muhammad, the Ummi Prophet, and his Household.

text.en:
It is also recommended to pass one’s hand over the face while
uttering the last statement.

The Holy Prophet (s.a.w.a.) is reported to
have said that if one offers this prayer and says this supplicatory
prayer, Almighty Allah will respond to his needs and award him the
reward of sixth times of Hajj and ‘Umrah (ritual pilgrimage).

It has been also narrated that the Holy Prophet
(s.a.w.a.) said, “One who recites Surah al-Tawheed one hundred times
at one night in Rajab will be decided to have fasted for one hundred
years for the sake of Almighty Allah Who will also decide for him one
hundred palaces (in Paradise) in the vicinity of one of the Prophets.”

The Holy Prophet (s.a.w.a.) is also reported as saying
that Almighty Allah will forgive all the sins of those who offer a ten
unit prayer, at one night in Rajab, reciting in each unit Surah al-
Faatehah once and repeating Surah al-Tawheed three times.

Twentieth: In Zad al-Ma’ad, ‘Allamah al-Majlisi has mentioned
that Imam AliAmeer al-Momineen(a.s.) reported the following from
the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.a.): If one repeats the following Quranic
chapters, litanies, and supplicatory prayers three times each day and
each night in the months of Rajab, Sha’ban, and Ramadan Surah al-Faatehah,  Ayah al-Kursi,  Surah al-Kafirun, Surah al-Tawheed, Surah al-Falaq, Surah al-Nas and say:

segments.arabic: سُبْحانَ الله وَالحَمْدُ للهِ وَلا إِلهَ إِلاّ الله وَالله أَكْبَرُ ، وَلا حَوْلَ وَلا قُوَّةَ إِلاّ بِالله العَلِيّ العَظِيمِ
segments.transliteration: vii) Say'Subhanallahi walhumdulillahi wa la illa ha illa allahu wallahu akbar wa la hawla wala quwata illa billahi al'aliyul azimi

translation.en: All glory be to Allah; all praise be to Allah;there is no god save Allah; Allah is the Greatest; and there is no might and no strength save with Allah, the All-high the All-great.

text.en: and say three times:

segments.arabic: اللّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلى مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ
segments.transliteration: viii)Allhumma salli al muhammadin wa aali muhammadin

translation.en: O Allah, bless Muhammad and his family

text.en: and say three times:

translation.en: Allah please forgive the believing men & women

text.en:
and say four hundred
times:

translation.en: I seek God's forgiveness and I repent to him

text.en:
he will
have all his sins forgiven even if they are as many as and as much as
raindrops, tree leaves, sea foam... etc.

Allamah al-Majlisi has also mentioned that it is traditional to
repeat the following litany one thousand times each night in Rajab:

translation.en: There is no god but Allah.

text.en:
Finally, it is highly advisable to visit the Holy Shrine of Imam al-
Rida(a.s.) in Rajab, since it enjoys a special rank in this month.
Similarly, to go on ‘Umrah in Rajab also brings about a great reward.
In this respect, it has been narrated that ‘Umrah comes directly after
the ritual Hajj in the amount of rewarding.

Imam Ali ibn al-Husayn
Zayn al-’Abidin(a.s.) is also reported to have gone on ‘Umrah in
Rabaj. In addition to offering prayers near the Holy Ka’bah, the Imam
(a.s.) used to prostrate himself before Almighty Allah too much, day
and night, and used to say the following words while prostrating:

segments.arabic: عَظُمَ الذَّنْبُ مِنْ عَبْدِكَ فَلْيَحْسُنِ العَفْوُ مِنْ عِنْدِكَ.

text.en:
27th day: This day is one of the great feast days. On this day, the Holy
Prophet (s.a.w.a.) was appointed for conveying the Divine Mission
when Archangel Gabriel first came to him with the Divine Revelation.
Several rites are advisably practiced on this day. Some of them are as
follows:
First: It is recommended to bathe oneself on this day.

Second: It is recommended to observe fasting. In fact, this day is
one of the four days that are dedicated to fasting in the whole year.
The reward of observing fasting on this day is equal to seventy year
fasting.

Third: It is recommended to repeat very frequently the invocation
of blessings upon Prophet Muhammad and his Household.

segments.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ
segments.transliteration: Allahumma salli `ala muhammadin wa ali muhammadin

translation.en: O Allah, (please) bless Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad.

text.en: <ref=3:1232>Ziyarat of Holy Prophet (Saws)</ref> and <ref=3:1018>Particular Ziyarat of Imam Ali(as) on 27th Rajab</ref>

Fifth: In his book entitled Misbah al-Mutahajjid, Shaykh al-Tusi
mentions that al-Rayyan ibn al-Salt is reported as saying that Imam
Muhammad al-Jawad(a.s.) observed fasting on the fifteenth and the
twenty-seventh of Rajab while he was in Baghdad. Following him, all
of his followers observed fasting

He then ordered us to offer the
prayer that consists of twelve units at each of which Surah al-Faatehah
and another Surah should be recited. At the accomplishment of this
prayer, it is recommended to recite Surahs <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
, <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
,
<ref=1:113>Al-Falaq</ref>
, and <ref=1:114>Al-Nas</ref>
 each for times.

Then it is recommended to say the
following litany four times:

segments.arabic: لا إلهَ إلاَّ اللّهُ.   وَاللّهُ أَكْبَرُ.   وَسُبْحَانَ اللّهِ،  وَالْحَمْدُ لِلّهِ،    وَلا حَوْلَ وَلا قُوَّةَ إلاَّ بِاللّهِ الْعَلِيِّ الْعَظِيمِ.
segments.transliteration: Laa Ilaaha Illallaahu Wallahu Akbar Wa Subh'aanallaahi Wal H'amdu Lillaahi Wa Laa H'awla Wa Laa Quwwata Illa Billahil A'liyyil A'z'eem

translation.en: There Is No God Save Allah. Allah Is The Greatest. Glory Be To Allah. Praise Be To Allah. There Is No Might And No Power Save In Allah, The Highest, The Greatest.

text.en: and four times:

segments.arabic: اللّهُ اللّهُ رَبِّي لا أُشْرِكُ بِهِ شَيْئاً.
segments.transliteration: Allaahu Allaahu Rabbee La Ushrika Bihee Shay-Aa

translation.en: Allah! Allah! My Lord I Do Not Associate Anything With Him

text.en: and four times:

translation.en: I Do Not Associate Anything With Him

text.en:
Sixth: Shaykh al-Tusi has also reported Abu’l-Qasim Husayn ibn
Ruh - may Allah have mercy upon him - as saying: On this day, you
may offer a prayer consisting of twelve units at each of which you
may recite Surah al-Faatehah and any other Surah. After each couple
of units, you may say the following:

segments.arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلّهِ الَّذِي لَمْ يَتَّخِذْ وَلَداً،
segments.transliteration: alhamdu lillahi alladhy lam yattakhidh waladan

translation.en: All praise is due to Allah, Who has not taken a son

segments.arabic: وَلَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ شَرِيكٌ فِي الْمُلْكِ،
segments.transliteration: wa lam yakun lahu sharikun fi almulki

translation.en: and Who has not a partner in the kingdom,

segments.arabic: وَلَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ وَلِيٌّ مِنَ الذُّلِّ،
segments.transliteration: wa lam yakun lahu waliyy mina aldhdhulli

translation.en: and Who has not a helper to save Him from disgrace;
and proclaim His greatness with thorough proclamation.
O my Supporter throughout my lifetime!
O my Companion in hardships!
O the provider of me with all graces that I experience!
O my Succor in my desires!
O the source of success of my needs!
O my guardian while I am absent!
O He Who is sufficient for me when I am alone!
O my entertainment in my loneliness!
You alone conceal my faults!
So, all praise be to You.
You alone overlook my slips.
So, all praise be to You.
You alone revive my deterioration.
So, all praise be to You.
(Please do) send blessings upon Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad
and conceal my defects,
and secure my apprehensions,
and oversee my slips,
and pardon my offenses,
and overlook my evildoings
and include me with the People of Paradise;
the promise of truth which they were promised.

text.en:
When you accomplish the prayer, you should recite Surahs <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
, <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
, <ref=1:113>Al-Falaq</ref>
, <ref=1:114>Al-Nas</ref>
, <ref=1:109>Al-Kafiroon</ref>
, <ref=1:97>Al-Qadr</ref> 
, and <ref=1:2:255>Ayah al-Kursi</ref>
 seven times. You should then repeat the following seven
times:

translation.en: There is no god save Allah;
Allah is the Most Great;
Glory be to Allah;

segments.transliteration: wa lahawla wa la quwwata illa billahi

translation.en: and there is neither might nor power save with Allah.

text.en: You should then say the following seven times:

segments.arabic: اللّهُ اللّهُ رَبِّي لا أُشْرِكُ بِهِ شَيْئاً.
segments.transliteration: allahu allahu rabby la ‘ushriku bihi shay’an

translation.en: Allah, Allah is my Lord. I associate none with Him.

text.en: After that, you may submit your needs.

Seventh: As is mentioned in Iqbal al-A’mal and some manuscripts
of Misbah al-Mutahajjid, it is recommended to say the following
supplicatory prayer on this day:

translation.en: O He Who has ordered us to forgive and overlook
and has taken upon Himself to forgive and overlook!
O He Who did forgive and overlook:
(please do) forgive me and overlook (my evildoings) O the All-generous.
O Allah: surely, all requests have been rejected,
and all means and ways have been inaccessible,
and all hopes have been eradicated,
and all expectations have been cut off from everybody save You
alone, there is no partner with You.

segments.arabic: اللّهُمَّ إنِّي أَجِدُ سُبُلَ الْمَطَالِبِ إلَيْكَ مُشْرَعَةً،
segments.transliteration: allahumma inni ajidu subula almatalibi ilayka mushra`atan

translation.en: O Allah: I find the ways to requesting from You wide open,
and springs of hoping for You quenching,

segments.transliteration: wa abwaba aldd`a‘i liman da`aka mufatthatan

translation.en: and doors of supplication unlocked for him who implores You,
and Your aid available for those who seek it.

segments.transliteration: wa a`lamu annka lida`ika bimawdi`i ijabatin

translation.en: I also know that You are in the position of response for him who beseeches You,
and in the position of relief for him who resorts to You,

segments.arabic: وَأَنَّ فِي اللَّهْفِ إلَى جُودِكَ وَالضَّمَانِ بِعِدَتِكَ
segments.transliteration: wa ann fi allahfi ila judika wal-zamani bi`idatika

translation.en: and that hastening to Your magnanimity and the reliance upon Your promise
compensate from the stinginess of the misery ones
and suffice from that which is grasped by the mean ones.
And [I know for sure] that You never screen Yourself against Your beings
but it is their evildoings that prevent them from journeying toward You.

segments.arabic: وَقَدْ عَلِمْتُ أَنَّ أَفْضَلَ زَادِ الرَّاحِلِ إلَيْكَ عَزْمُ إرَادَةٍ يَخْتَارُكَ بِهَا
segments.transliteration: wa qad `alimtu ann afdala zadi alrrahili ilayka `azmu iradatin

translation.en: I have also known for sure that the best provisions of his who intends to travel to You is a true willpower by which He chooses You among others.

segments.transliteration: yakhtaruka biha wa qad najaka bi`azmi aliradati qalbi

translation.en: Hence, my heart has called at You with full willpower;

segments.arabic: وَأَسْأَلُك بِكُلِّ دَعْوَةٍ دَعَاكَ بِهَا رَاجٍ بَلَّغْتَهُ أَمَلَهُ،
segments.transliteration: wa as-aluka bikull da`watin da`aka biha rajin ballghtahu amalahu

translation.en: and I pray You in the name of all supplications said by a hopeful and thus You have achieved his hope,
or said by a seeker of aid and thus You have admitted his request,
or said by an aggrieved, depressed one and thus You have relieved him,
or said by a guilty sinner and thus You have forgiven him,
or said by a healthy one and thus You have perfected Your favors on him,
or said by a poor one and thus You have conferred upon him with Your wealth,

segments.arabic: وَلِتِلْكَ الدَّعْوَةِ عَلَيْكَ حَقٌّ وَعِنْدَكَ مَنْزِلَةٌ
segments.transliteration: wa litilka aldd`wati `alayka haqq wa `indaka manzilatun

translation.en: and that these supplications occupy a considerable position with You and enjoy a standing with You—

segments.arabic: إلاَّ صَلَّيْتَ عَلَى مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ
segments.transliteration: illa sallayta `ala muhammadin wa ali muhammadin

translation.en: I pray You to send blessings upon Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad

segments.transliteration: wa qadayta hawa’ijy hawa’ija alddunya wal-akhirati

translation.en: and to settle all my needs for this world and the Next World.
This is Rajab, the honored and dignified month,

segments.arabic: الَّذِي أَكْرَمْتَنَا بِهِ أَوَّلُ أَشْهُرِ الْحُرُمِ
segments.transliteration: alladhy akramtana bihi awulu ash-huri alhurumi

translation.en: which You have honored us through it, being the first of the Sacred Months,
and You have selected us for this honor among the other nations.
O the Lord of Magnanimity and Generosity.

segments.arabic: فَنَسْأَلُكَ بِهِ وَبِاسْمِكَ الأَعْظَمِ الأَعْظَمِ الأَعْظَمِ،
segments.transliteration: fanasaluka bihi wa bismika ala`zami ala`zami ala`zami

translation.en: I thus beseech You by Your Name, the grandest, the grandest, the grandest,
the most majestic, the most honorable,
that You have created so that it has settled under Your Shadow
and it thus shall never transfer to anyone other than You—

segments.arabic: أَنْ تُصَلِّيَ عَلَى مُحَمَّدٍ وَأَهْلِ بَيْتِهِ الطَّاهِرِينَ
segments.transliteration: an tusalliya `ala muhammadin wa ahli baytihi alttahirina

translation.en: I beseech You to send blessings upon Muhammad and his Household, the immaculate,

segments.transliteration: wa taj`alana mina al`amilina fihi bita`atika

translation.en: and to include us, during this month, with those who practice acts of obedience to You
and those who hope for Your admission.
O Allah: (please do) lead us to the Right Path,

segments.arabic: وَاجْعَلْ مَقِيلَنَا عِنْدَكَ خَيْرَ مَقِيلٍ فِي ظِلٍّ ظَلِيلٍ،
segments.transliteration: waj`al maqilana `indaka khayra maqilin fi zill zalilin

translation.en: and choose for us the best resting-place with You, under a dense shade
and abundant possession. Surely, You are Sufficient for us! Most Excellent are You, and in You do we trust!
Peace be upon His servants, the chosen,
and His blessings be upon them all.

segments.arabic: اللّهُمَّ وَبَارِكْ لَنَا فِي يَوْمِنَا هذَا الَّذِي فَضَّلْتَهُ،
segments.transliteration: allahumma wa barik lana fi yawmina hadha alladhy fazaltahu

translation.en: O Allah: (please do) bless us on this day that You have honored,
and covered with Your nobility,
and implanted in the great and highest Dwelling.

segments.arabic: صَلِّ عَلَى مَنْ فِيهِ إلَى عِبَادِكَ أَرْسَلْتَهُ،

translation.en: (Please do) send blessings upon him whom You sent (as Your Messenger) to Your servants
and whom You accommodated in the Noble Place.

segments.arabic: اللّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلَيْهِ صَلاةً دَائِمَةً تَكُونُ لَكَ شُكْراً،
segments.transliteration: allahumma salli `alayhi salatan da’imatan takunu laka shukran

translation.en: O Allah: send upon him blessing that is never-ending being in the form of thanks to You
and reward to us.
And make easy for us all our affairs;

segments.arabic: وَاخْتِمْ لَنَا بِالسَّعَادَةِ إلَى مُنْتَهَى آجَالِنَا،
segments.transliteration: wakhtim lana bilss`adati ila muntaha ajalina

translation.en: and make our end result up to the last of our lives pleasant

segments.transliteration: wa qad qabilta aliyasira min a`malina

translation.en: after You accept our little deed
and achieve for us all our hopes out of Your mercy.
Verily, You have power over all things.

segments.arabic: وَصَلَّى اللّهُ عَلَى مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِهِ وَسَلَّمَ.
segments.transliteration: wa salla allahu `ala muhammadin wa alihi wa sallama

translation.en: May Allah bless and exalt Muhammad and his Household.

text.en:
It is worth mentioning that this supplicatory prayer, which is one of
the excellent supplications of Rajab, was said by Imam Musa ibn
Ja’far al-Kazim (a.s.) on the day when the ruling authorities took him
to Baghdad. That day was the twenty-seventh of Rajab.

Eighth: As is mentioned in Iqbal al-A’mal, it is recommended to
say the following supplicatory prayer on this day: O Allah: I beseech You in the name of the Grandest Manifestation

This supplicatory prayer has been previously mentioned, yet
according to the narration of al-Kaf’ami, within the rites of the
twenty-seventh night of Rajab.

Narrations have mentioned that it is recommended to bathe oneself
on this day and to observe fasting on it, which achieves the
forgiveness of all sins, both the past and the future. It is also
recommended to offer <ref=3:1201>Salat Salman</ref>
 previously mentioned within the
rites of the first of Rajab.

translation.en: True are the words of Allah, the All-great.
There is no god save Him,
the Ever-living, the Eternal,
The Lord of Majesty and Honor,
the All-beneficent, the All-merciful,
the Forbearing, the All-generous.
There is nothing like Him,
and He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing,
the All-seeing, the All-aware.

segments.arabic: شَهِدَ ٱللَّهُ انَّهُ لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ هُوَ
segments.transliteration: shahida allahu annahu la ilaha illa huwa

translation.en: Allah bears witness that there is no god but He,
and (so do) the angels and those possessed of knowledge,
maintaining His creation with justice.

segments.arabic: لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ هُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ
segments.transliteration: la ilaha illa huwa al`azizu alhakimu

translation.en: There is no god but He, the Mighty, the Wise.
His noble Messengers have conveyed the mission
and I am of those who bear witness to that.
O Allah, to You belongs praise,
to You belongs glory,
to You belongs might,
to You belongs pride,
to You belongs supreme power,
to You belong bounties,
to You belongs greatness,
to You belongs mercy,
to You belongs reverence,
to You belongs sovereignty,
to You belongs beauty,
to You belongs benevolence,
to You belongs glorification,
to You belongs holiness,
to You belongs the confession of Your being the One and Only God,
to You belongs the confession of Your being the most Great Lord,
to You belongs that which is seen,
to You belongs that which is not seen,

segments.transliteration: wa laka ma fawqa alssamawati al`ula

translation.en: to You belongs that which is far above the high heavens,
to You belongs that which is deep down under the abyss,
to You belong the worlds below the heavens,
to You belong the end and the beginning,

segments.arabic: وَلَكَ مَا تَرْضَىٰ بِهِ مِنَ ٱلثَّنَاءِ وَٱلْحَمْدِ
segments.transliteration: wa laka ma tarda bihi min alththana'i walhamdi

translation.en: and to You is all thanks, all praise,
all gratitude, and all bliss as much as it pleases You.
O Allah, (please do) send blessings to (Archangel) Gabriel,
the trustee on Your revelations,
the firm in conveying Your orders,

segments.transliteration: walmuta`i fi samawatika wa mahalli karamatika

translation.en: the obeyed in Your heavens and the places where Your honoring lies,
the bearer of Your Words,
the supporter of Your Prophets,
and the terminator of Your enemies.
O Allah, (please do) send blessings to (Archangel) Michael,
the angel of Your mercy,
the one created for (showing) Your kindness,
and the one seeking forgiveness and assisting the people of obedience to You.
O Allah, (please do) send blessings to Seraph,
the bearer of Your Throne,
the one in charge of the Trumpet (that declares the Resurrection Day),
the one waiting for Your order,
and the one apprehensive and fearful of You.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلَىٰ حَمَلَةِ ٱلْعَرْشِ ٱلطَّاهِرِينَ

translation.en: O Allah, (please do) send blessings to the pure angels who bear Your Throne,
upon the scribes; the noble,
the virtuous, and the immaculate,
upon Your angels; the virtuous recorders (of the deeds),
upon the angels of the gardens of Paradise,
upon the keepers of Hellfire,
and upon the Angel of Death and his assistants.
O Lord of Majesty and Honor!
O Allah, (please do) send blessings to our father Adam:
the foremost of Your creation
whom You honored by making Your angels prostrate themselves before him,
and whom You allowed to dwell in Your Paradise.
O Allah, (please do) send blessings to our mother Eve:
the purified from filth,
the cleaned from dirt,
the favorite from among the humankind,
and the allowed to visit the places of sanctity.
O Allah, (please do) send blessings to Abel, Shayth,
Idris, Noah,
Hud, Salih,
Abraham, Ishmael,
Isaac, Jacob,
Joseph, the Tribes,
Lot, Shu`ayb,
Job, Moses,
Aaron, Joshua,
Micah, al-Khidr,
Dhu’l-Qarnayn (the two-horned), Jonah,
Elias, Elisa,
Dhu’l-Kifl, Saul,
David, Solomon,
Zachariah, Shayah,
John, Turakh,
Matthias, Jeremiah,
Habakkuk, Daniel,
Ezra, Jesus,
Simon, Georges,
the Apostles, the Followers,

segments.transliteration: wa khalidin wa hanzalata wa luqmana

translation.en: Khalid, Hanzalah, and Luqman.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ
segments.transliteration: allahumma salli `ala Muhammadin wa ali Muhammadin

translation.en: O Allah, (please do) send blessings to Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad,
have mercy upon Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad,

segments.transliteration: wa barik `ala Muhammadin wa ali Muhammadin

translation.en: and send benedictions upon Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad,

segments.arabic: كَمَا صَلَّيْتَ وَرَحِمْتَ وَبَارَكْتَ عَلَىٰ إِبْرَاهِيمَ وَآلِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ
segments.transliteration: kama sallayta wa rahimta wa barakta `ala ibrahima wa ali ibrahima

translation.en: just as You have sent blessings, had mercy, and sent benedictions upon Abraham and the Household of Abraham.
You are verily worthy of all praise, full of all glory.
O Allah, (please do) send blessings to the Successors, the happy,
the witnesses, and the leaders to the true guidance.
O Allah, (please do) send blessings to the Substitutes (whom shall be given the favor of participating in Imam al-Mahdi’s campaigns, the loftiest personalities <
those observing fasting with devotion, those who ardently serve You,
those whom You have chosen, the ascetic servants,
and the people of painstaking and hardworking deeds.

segments.arabic: وَٱخْصُصْ مُحَمَّداً وَاهْلَ بَيْتِهِ بِافْضَلِ صَلَوَاتِكَ
segments.transliteration: wakhsus Muhammadan wa ahla baytihi bi-afdali salawatika

translation.en: (Please do) give Muhammad and his Household exclusively the best of Your blessings
and the most abundant of Your favors.

segments.arabic: وَبَلِّغْ رُوحَهُ وَجَسَدَهُ مِنِّي تَحِيَّةً وَسَلاماً
segments.transliteration: wa balligh ruhahu wa jasadahu minni tahiyyatan wa salaman

translation.en: (Please do) convey to his soul and body my greetings and compliments

segments.transliteration: wa zid-hu fadlan wa sharafan wa karaman

translation.en: and give him more favors, honor, and esteem

segments.arabic: حَتَّىٰ تُبَلِّغَهُ اعْلَىٰ دَرَجَاتِ اهْلِ ٱلشَّرَفِ
segments.transliteration: hatta tuballighahu a`la darajati ahli alshsharafi

translation.en: so that You will make him attain the most elevated ranks of the honored ones
from amongst the Prophets, the Messengers,
and the virtuous, favorable ones.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ وَصَلِّ عَلَىٰ مَنْ سَمَّيْتُ وَمَنْ لَمْ اسَمِّ
segments.transliteration: allahumma wa salli `ala man sammaytu wa man lam usammi

translation.en: O Allah, (please) send blessings to those whose names I have mentioned or I have not
from amongst Your angels, Prophets,
Messengers, and obedient servants.

segments.transliteration: wa awsil salawati ilayhim wa ila arwahihim

translation.en: Convey my invocations of blessings to them and to their souls
and make them my brethren in You
and make them help me pray You.
O Allah, I seek Your intercession for me to You,
I seek Your honor in the name of Your honor,
I seek Your magnanimity in the name of Your magnanimity,
and I seek Your mercy in the name of Your mercy
and in the name of the people of the obedience to You.

segments.arabic: وَاسْالُكَ ٱللَّهُمَّ بِكُلِّ مَا سَالَكَ بِهِ احَدٌ مِنْهُمْ
segments.transliteration: wa as'aluka allahumma bikulli ma sa'alaka bihi ahadun minhum

translation.en: I thus beseech You, O Allah, in the name of all the things by whose names those people have besought You,
including the honorable requests that are never rejected by You

segments.arabic: وَبِمَا دَعَوْكَ بِهِ مِنْ دَعْوَةٍ مُجَابَةٍ غَيْرِ مُخَيِّبَةٍ
segments.transliteration: wa bima da`awka bihi min da`watin mujabatin ghayri mukhayyibatin

translation.en: and all the responded and unfailing prayers that they have asked from You.

segments.arabic: يَا اللَّهُ يَا رَحْمٰنُ يَا رَحِيمُ
segments.transliteration: ya allahu ya rahmanu ya rahimu

translation.en: O Allah! O All-beneficent! O All-merciful!

segments.arabic: يَا حَلِيمُ يَا كَرِيمُ يَا عَظِيمُ

translation.en: O All-forbearing! O All-generous! O All-great!

segments.arabic: يَا جَلِيلُ يَا مُنِيلُ يَا جَمِيلُ
segments.transliteration: ya jalilu ya munilu ya jamilu

translation.en: O All-majestic! O Bestower! O All-munificent!

segments.arabic: يَا كَفِيلُ يَا وَكِيلُ يَا مُقِيلُ
segments.transliteration: ya kafilu ya wakilu ya muqilu

translation.en: O Surety! O Dependable! O Rescuer!
O Protector! O All-aware!
O All-luminous! O Eradicator (of the wrongdoers)!

segments.arabic: يَا مَنِيعُ يَا مُدِيلُ يَا مُحِيلُ
segments.transliteration: ya mani`u ya mudilu ya muhilu

translation.en: O Shielding! O Grantor of victory! O Assuring!

segments.arabic: يَا كَبِيرُ يَا قَدِيرُ يَا بَصِيرُ
segments.transliteration: ya kabiru ya qadiru ya basiru

translation.en: O All-big! O All-powerful! O All-seer!
O most Ready to appreciate (servitude)! O All-benign!
O Source of purity! O All-pure!
O Omnipotent! O Evident!
O Immanent! O Concealer!
O All-encompassing! O All-prevailing!
O All-guarding! O Invincible!
O Nigh! O Loving!
O All-praised! O full of all glory!

segments.arabic: يَا مُبْدِئُ يَا مُعِيدُ يَا شَهِيدُ
segments.transliteration: ya mubdi'u ya mu`idu ya shahidu

translation.en: O Originator! O Reproducer! O Witness!
O All-liberal! O All-bountiful!
O Source of all bounties! O Doer of favors!
O Straitening! O Enlarger!
O Guide! O Sender!
O Director! O Leader (to the truth)!
O Giver! O Forbidder!
O Repealer! O Raiser!
O Eternal! O Guardian!
O Creator! O Endower!
O Accepter of repentance! O Decider!
O Donator! O Source of rest!
O He Who has in possessions the clues of all things!

segments.arabic: يَا نَفَّاعُ يَا رَؤُوفُ يَا عَطُوفُ

translation.en: O Source of profit! O All-kind! O All-sympathetic!
O Securer! O Healer!
O Curer! O Awarder!
O Faithful! O Dominant!
O Almighty! O All-potent!

segments.arabic: يَا مُتَكَبِّرُ يَا سَلامُ يَا مُؤْمِنُ
segments.transliteration: ya mutakabbiru ya salamu ya mu'minu

translation.en: O Superb! O Giver of peace! O Grantor of security!
O Only One! O Besought of all!
O Light! O Manager (of all affairs)!
O Single! O Lone!
O Holy! O Giver of victory!

segments.arabic: يَا مُؤْنِسُ يَا بَاعِثُ يَا وَارِثُ
segments.transliteration: ya mu'nisu ya ba`ithu ya warithu

translation.en: O Source of entertainment! O Resurrector! O Inheritor (of all things)!
O All-knowing! O Judge!
O Originator! O Exalted!

segments.arabic: يَا مُصَوِّرُ يَا مُسَلِّمُ يَا مُتَحَبِّبُ

translation.en: O Maker of visions! O Grantor of safety! O Amiable!
O Subsistent! O Everlasting!
O Knowing! O Wise!
O All-magnanimous! O Maker!
O Caring! O Source of pleasure!
O Fair! O Distinguisher (between the right and the wrong)!

segments.arabic: يَا دَيَّانُ يَا حَنَّانُ يَا مَنَّانُ
segments.transliteration: ya dayyanu ya hannanu ya mannanu

translation.en: O Master! O All-munificent! O Favorer!
O All-hearer! O best Maker!
O Observer! O Helper!
O Extender (of mercy)! O Forgiver!

segments.arabic: يَا قَدِيمُ يَا مُسَهِّلُ يَا مُيَسِّرُ
segments.transliteration: ya qadimu ya musahhilu ya muyassiru

translation.en: O Eternal! O Alleviator! O Reliever!
O He Who causes to die! O Grantor of life!
O Grantor of benefits! O Provider of sustenance!
O All-competent! O Creator of causes!

segments.transliteration: ya mughithu ya mughni ya muqni

translation.en: O Aide! O Grantor of wealth! O Giver to hold!

segments.arabic: يَا خَالِقُ يَا رَاصِدُ يَا وَاحِدُ
segments.transliteration: ya khaliqu ya rasidu ya wahidu

translation.en: O Creator! O Onlooker! O One!
O Present! O Mender!
O Safeguarding! O Firm (in punishment)!

segments.arabic: يَا غِيَاثُ يَا عَائِدُ يَا قَابِضُ

translation.en: O Aid! O Donor! O Straitening!
O He Who has mounted to the most elevated point,
He thus occupies the most exalted site!
O He Who has approached so closely that He has become the nearest of all things
and, at the same time, He has been so far that He is in the furthest point,
and He has known the secret and even what is more hidden!

segments.arabic: يَا مَنْ إِلَيْهِ ٱلتَّدْبِيرُ وَلَهُ ٱلْمَقَادِيرُ
segments.transliteration: ya man ilayhi alttadbiru wa lahu almaqadiru

translation.en: O He to Whom all management is relegated and to Whom all measures are referred!

segments.arabic: وَيَا مَنِ ٱلْعَسِيرُ عَلَيْهِ سَهْلٌ يَسِيرٌ
segments.transliteration: wa ya man al`asiru `alayhi sahlun yasirun

translation.en: O He for Whom the most difficult thing is easy and simple!

segments.arabic: يَا مَنْ هُوَ عَلَىٰ مَا يَشَاءُ قَدِيرٌ
segments.transliteration: ya man huwa `ala ma yasha'u qadirun

translation.en: O He Who has power to do whatever He wills!
O He Who sends forth the wind!
O He Who causes the dawn to break!
O He Who revives the spirits!
O Lord of magnanimity and lenience!
O He Who has the power to restore that which has been missed!
O He Who resurrects the dead bodies!
O He Who draws together the dispersal!

segments.arabic: يَا رَازِقَ مَنْ يَشَاءُ بِغَيْرِ حِسَابٍ
segments.transliteration: ya raziqa man yasha'u bighayri hisabin

translation.en: O He Who provides sustenance to whomever He wants without measure!

segments.arabic: وَيَا فَاعِلَ مَا يَشَاءُ كَيْفَ يَشَاءُ
segments.transliteration: wa ya fa`ila ma yasha'u kayfa yasha'u

translation.en: O He Who has power to do whatever He wills however He wills!
O Lord of Majesty and Honor!
O Ever-living! O Self-subsistent!
O He Who existed when there existed nothing else!
O Ever-living! O Reviver of the dead!

segments.transliteration: ya hayyu la ilaha illa anta

translation.en: O Ever-living! There is no god save You
the excellent Maker of the heavens and the earth.
O my God and my Master,
(please do) send blessings to Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad,
have mercy upon Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad,

segments.transliteration: wa barik `ala Muhammadin wa ali Muhammadin

translation.en: and send benedictions upon Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad

segments.arabic: كَمَا صَلَّيْتَ وَبَارَكْتَ وَرَحِمْتَ عَلَىٰ إِبْرَاهِيمَ وَآلِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ
segments.transliteration: kama sallayta wa barakta wa rahimta `ala ibrahima wa ali ibrahima

translation.en: just as You have sent blessings and benedictions and had mercy upon Abraham and the household of Abraham.
You are verily worthy of all praise and full of all glory.
Have mercy upon my humiliation, neediness,
poverty, loneliness, aloneness,
humbleness before You,
reliance upon You,
and submission to You.
I implore You with the imploring of the humble, the subservient,
the fearful, the scared
the afraid, the miserable,
the modest, the insignificant,
the hungry, the poor,
the seeker of Your refuge, the seeker of Your protection,
the confessing of his sins,
the implorer for You to forgive his sin,
and the humble to his Lord;
I implore You with the imploration of one whom has been disappointed by those whom he trusts,
rejected by his intimate ones,
and whose catastrophe has been enormous.
I implore You the imploration of one burning, sad,
weak, mortified,
depressed, wretched,
and resorting to Your shelter.
O Allah, I also beseech You in the name of Your being the King,

segments.arabic: وَانَّكَ مَا تَشَاءُ مِنْ امْرٍ يَكُونُ
segments.transliteration: wa annaka ma tasha'u min amrin yakunu

translation.en: whatever You will shall definitely take place,
and You have power over all things.

segments.transliteration: wa as'aluk bihurmati hadha alshshahri alharami

translation.en: I beseech You in the name of the holiness of this sacred month,
the Sacred House,
the Sacred Town,
the Rukn (i.e. the corner of the Holy Ka`bah), the Maqam (i.e. the standing-place of Prophet Abraham),
the great rituals,

segments.arabic: وَبِحَقِّ نَبِيِّكَ مُحَمَّدٍ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ ٱلسَّلاَمُ
segments.transliteration: wa bihaqqi nabiyyika Muhammadin `alayhi wa alihi alssalamu

translation.en: and in the name of Your Prophet Muhammad, peace be upon him and his Household.
O He Who gave Shayth to Adam,
and gave Ishmael and Isaac to Abraham!

segments.arabic: وَيَا مَنْ رَدَّ يُوسُفَ عَلَىٰ يَعْقُوبَ
segments.transliteration: wa ya man radda yusufa `ala ya`quba

translation.en: O He Who returned Joseph to Jacob!

segments.arabic: وَيَا مَنْ كَشَفَ بَعْدَ ٱلْبَلاءِ ضُرَّ ايُّوْبَ
segments.transliteration: wa ya man kashafa ba`da albala'i durra ayyuba

translation.en: O He Who relieved Job from his harm after long hardships!
O He Who returned Moses to his mother!
O He Who increased al-Khadir knowledge!

segments.transliteration: wa ya man wahaba lidawuda sulaymana

translation.en: O He Who gave Solomon to David,
gave Jonah to Zachariah,
and gave Jesus to Mary!
O He Who safeguarded the daughter of Shu`ayb!

segments.transliteration: wa ya kafila waladi umm musa

translation.en: O He Who cared for the son of the mother of Moses!

segments.arabic: اسْالُكَ انْ تُصَلِّيَ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ
segments.transliteration: as'aluka an tusalliya `ala Muhammadin wa ali Muhammadin

translation.en: I beseech You to send blessings to Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad,

segments.transliteration: wa an taghfira li dhunubi kullaha

translation.en: forgive my all sins,
save me from Your chastisement,

segments.transliteration: wa tujiba li ridwanaka wa amanaka

translation.en: and confer upon me with Your pleasure and security

segments.transliteration: wa ihsanaka wa ghufranaka wa jinanaka

translation.en: and with Your favoring, forgiveness, and gardens of Your Paradise.

segments.arabic: وَاسْالُكَ انْ تَفُكَّ عَنِّي كُلَّ حَلْقَةٍ
segments.transliteration: wa as'aluka an tafukka `anni kulla halqatin

translation.en: I ask You to redeem me from all circles of misfortune
that are drawn around those who intend harm to me and me,
unlock for me all doors,
make easy for me all hardships,
alleviate for me all difficult matters,
mute all those who speak ill of me,
defend me against all oppressors,

segments.arabic: وَتَكْبِتَ عَنِّي كُلَّ عَدُوٍّ لِي وَحَاسِدٍ
segments.transliteration: wa takbita `anni kulla `aduwwin li wa hasidin

translation.en: suppress all my enemies and envious ones,
protect me against all unjust ones,

segments.arabic: وَتَكْفِيَنِي كُلَّ عَائِقٍ يَحُولُ بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَ حَاجَتِي
segments.transliteration: wa takfiyani kulla `a'iqin yahulu bayni wa bayna hajati

translation.en: and rescue me from all impediments that lie between my needs and me,

segments.arabic: وَيُحَاوِلُ انْ يُفَرِّقَ بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَ طَاعَتِكَ
segments.transliteration: wa yuhawilu an yufarriqa bayni wa bayna ta`atika

translation.en: try to take me away from acts of obedience to You,
and try to frustrate me from serving You.
O He Who has bridled all mutinous jinn,
overcome all tyrannical devils,
mortified the necks of the oppressors,
and deterred all the ruling authorities from harming the enfeebled ones!
I beseech You in the name of Your power over all things

segments.transliteration: wa tas-hilika lima tasha'u kayfa tasha'u

translation.en: and Your alleviating whatever You will however You will,

segments.arabic: انْ تَجْعَلَ قَضَاءَ حَاجَتِي فِيمَا تَشَاءُ
segments.transliteration: an taj`ala qada'a hajati fima tasha'u

translation.en: to grant my requests as You will.

text.en: Then, you may prostrate yourself and try to stick your cheeks to the ground, saying the following:

translation.en: O Allah, I am prostrating myself before You
and I have full faith in You;
so, (please) have mercy upon my humility, neediness,
efforts, meekness,

segments.transliteration: wa maskanati wa faqri ilayka ya rabbi

translation.en: paucity, and my want to You, O my Lord.

text.en: You should try to shed even a single drop of tear, for this is the signal of admission.
Dua of last night of shaaban

segments.arabic: اللّهُمَّ إنَّ هذَا الشَّهْرَ الْمُبَارَكَ الَّذِي أُنْزِلَ فِيهِ الْقُرْآنُ
segments.transliteration: allahumma inna hadha alshshahra almubaraka alladhy ’unzila fihi alqur’anu

translation.en: O Allah: verily, this blessed month, in which the Qur'an was revealed

segments.arabic: وَجُعِلَ هُدَىً لِلنَّاسِ وَبَيِّنَاتٍ مِنَ الْهُدَى وَالْفُرْقَانِ
segments.transliteration: wa ju`ila hudan lilnnasi wa bayynatin mina alhuda wal-furqani

translation.en: and was made guidance for people and clear proofs of the guidance, and the Criterion (of right and wrong),
has commenced
So, (please) keep us out of blemish during it, and keep it sound for us,

segments.arabic: وَتَسَلَّمْهُ مِنَّا فِي يُسْرٍ مِنْكَ وَعَافِيَةٍ،
segments.transliteration: wa tasallamhu minna fi yusrin minka wa `afiyatin

translation.en: accept it from us in a state of prosperity and health granted to us by You,  accept my simple (acts).

segments.arabic: يَا مَنْ أَخَذَ الْقَلِيلَ وَشَكَرَ الْكَثِيرَ
segments.transliteration: ya man akhadha alqalila wa shakara alkathira

translation.en: O’ He who acknowledges and gives answer many times to the very little offered to Him and thanks us by giving us plenty,
(please) accept from me the little (deed).

segments.arabic: اللّهُمَّ إنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ أَن ْتَجْعَلَ لِي إلَى كُلِّ خَيْرٍ سَبِيلاً،
segments.transliteration: allahumma inni asaluka an taj`ala li ila kull khayrin sabilan

translation.en: O Allah: I beseech You to make for me a path to every decency,

segments.arabic: وَمِنْ كُلِّ مَا لا تُحِبُّ مَانِعاً
segments.transliteration: wa min kulli ma la tuhibbu mani`an

translation.en: and to make for me a barrier against everything that You do not like
O the most Merciful of all those who show mercy.

segments.arabic: يَا مَنْ عَفَا عَنِّي وَعَمَّا خَلَوْتُ بِهِ مِنَ السَّيِّئَاتِ،
segments.transliteration: ya man `afa `anni wa `amma khalawtu bihi mina alssayyi’ati

translation.en: O He Who has pardoned me and overlooked the misdeeds that I have committed secretly;

segments.arabic: يَا مَنْ لَمْ يُؤَاخِذْنِي بِارْتِكَابِ الْمَعَاصِي،
segments.transliteration: ya man lam yu´akhidhny biartikabi alma`asi

translation.en: O He Who has not punished me for my committing acts of disobedience to Him:
I seek Your pardon, I seek Your pardon, I seek Your pardon, O the All-generous.
O my God: You advised me, but I have not followed Your advice,
and You rebuked me for violating that which You have deemed forbidden, but I have not stopped,
Now, what is my excuse?

segments.arabic: فَاعْفُ عَنِّي يَا كَرِيمُ، عَفْوَكَ عَفْوَكَ.

translation.en: So, (please) pardon me, O the All-generous; I seek Your pardon, I seek Your pardon!
O Allah: I beseech You for rest at death,
and for pardon at calling to account.

segments.arabic: عَظُمَ الذَّنْبُ مِنْ عَبْدِكَ فَلْيَحْسُنِ التَّجَاوُزُ مِنْ عِنْدِكَ،

translation.en: Great is the sin of Your servant; so, let Your overlooking him be excellent.

segments.arabic: يَا أَهْلَ التَّقْوَى وَيَا أَهْلَ الْمَغْفِرَةِ، عَفْوَكَ عَفْوَكَ.
segments.transliteration: ya ahla alttaqwa wa ya ahla almaghfirati `afwaka `afwaka

translation.en: O He Who is worthy of being feared and is worthy of forgiving; I seek Your pardon, I seek Your pardon.

segments.arabic: اللّهُمَّ إنِّي عَبْدُكَ بْنُ عَبْدِكَ بْنُ أَمَتِكَ

translation.en: Allah: I am Your servant and the son of Your servant and Your she-servant,
and I am weak and in need for Your mercy;

segments.arabic: وَأَنْتَ مُنْزِلُ الْغِنَى وَالْبَرَكَةِ عَلَى الْعِبَادِ،
segments.transliteration: wa anta munzilu alghina wal-barakati `ala al`ibadi

translation.en: and You are the Pourer of wealth and bless on the servants (of You),
and You are omnipotent and full of power;
as You have recorded all their (i.e. Your servants) deeds,
and You have decided the sustenance of each one them,

segments.arabic: وَجَعَلْتَهُمْ مُخْتَلِفَةً أَلْسِنَتُهُمْ وَأَلْوَانُهُمْ خَلْقاً مِنْ بَعْدِ خَلْقٍ،
segments.transliteration: wa ja`altahum mukhtalifatan alsinatuhum wa alwanuhum khalqan min ba`di khalqin

translation.en: and You have made them of different languages and colors; in different stages of creation;
Your servants can never attain Your knowledge,
and Your servants can never recognize the real estimate that is due to You
and all of us are needy for Your mercy;
So, (please) do not turn Your Face away from me,

segments.arabic: وَاجْعَلْنِي مِنْ صَالِحِي خَلْقِكَ فِي الْعَمَلِ وَالأَمَلِ وَالْقَضَاءِ وَالْقَدَرِ.
segments.transliteration: waj`alny min salihy khalqika fi al`amali wal-amali wal-qada‘i wal-qadari

translation.en: and include me with the virtuous creatures of You in deeds, hopes, and Your decisions (for me).
O Allah: (please) keep me alive in the best manner of living,

segments.arabic: وَأَفْنِنِي خَيْرَ الْفَنَاءِ عَلَى مُوَالاةِ أَوْلِيَائِكَ،
segments.transliteration: wa afniny khayra alfana‘i `ala muwalati awliya’ika

translation.en: and decide for me to die in the best manner of death keeping loyal to Your representatives,
and incurring the enmity of Your enemies,
and keeping desiring for You and fearing from You,
and submission, faithfulness, and compliance with You,
and keeping belief in Your Book
and following Your Prophet’s instructions.
O Allah: as for whatever I bear in my heart,
including suspicion or doubt
or denial or despair

segments.arabic: أَوْ فَرَحٍ أَوْ بَذَخٍ أَوْ بَطَرٍ
segments.transliteration: aw farahin aw badhakhin aw batarin

translation.en: or (excessive) joy or lavish expenditure or recklessness

segments.arabic: أَوْ خُيَلاءَ أَوْ رِيَاءٍ أَوْ سُمْعَةٍ
segments.transliteration: aw khuyala‘a aw rya‘in aw sum`atin

translation.en: or pride or arrogance or ostentation
or discord or hypocrisy

segments.arabic: أَوْ كُفْرٍ أَوْ فُسُوقٍ أَوْ عِصْيَانٍ

translation.en: or unbelief or licentiousness or disobedience

segments.arabic: أَوْ عَظَمَةٍ أَوْ شَيْءٍ لا تُحِبُّ،

translation.en: or conceit or anything else that You do not like;

segments.arabic: فَأَسْأَلُكَ يَا رَبِّ أَنْ تُبَدِّلَنِي مَكَانَهُ إيمَاناً بِوَعْدِكَ،
segments.transliteration: fasaluka ya rabbi an tubaddlany makanahu ‘imanan biwa`dika

translation.en: I thus beseech You, O my Lord, to substitute all that with belief in Your promise
and fulfilling of my covenant to You
and satisfaction with what You decide for me
and indifference to the worldly pleasures
and desire for that which You have

segments.transliteration: wa atharatan wa tumaninatan wa tawbatan nasuhan

translation.en: and altruism and tranquility and sincere repentance;
I beseech You for all that, O the Lord of the worlds.

segments.arabic: إلهِي أَنْتَ مِنْ حِلْمِكَ تُعْصَى فَكَأَنَّكَ لَمْ تُرَ،
segments.transliteration: ilahy anta min hilmika tu`sa faka-annaka lam tura

translation.en: O my God: because You are so forbearing, You are disobeyed as if You are not present,

segments.arabic: وَمِنْ كَرَمِكَ وَجُودِكَ تُطَاعُ فَكَأَنَّكَ لَمْ تُعْصَ،
segments.transliteration: wa min karamika wa judika tuta`u faka-annaka lam tu`sa

translation.en: and because You are so generous and magnanimous, You are obeyed as if You have not been disobeyed at all

segments.arabic: وَأَنَا وَمَنْ لَمْ يَعْصِكَ سُكَّانُ أَرْضِكَ
segments.transliteration: wa ana wa man lam ya`sika sukkanu ardika

translation.en: Both those who have not disobeyed You and I are the inhabitants of Your lands;
So, (please) confer magnanimously upon us with Your favors
and always refer to us with Your goodness
O the most Merciful of all those who show mercy.

segments.arabic: وَصَلَّى اللّهُ عَلَى مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِهِ صَلاةً دَائِمَةً
segments.transliteration: wa salla allahu `ala muhammadin wa alihi salatan da’imatan

translation.en: May Allah bless Muhammad and his Household with endless blessings

segments.arabic: لا تُحْصَى وَلا تُعَدُّ وَلا يَقْدِرُ قَدْرَهَا غَيْرُكَ
segments.transliteration: la tuhsa wa la tu`add wa la yaqdiru qadraha ghayruka

translation.en: that are innumerable, uncountable, and none can do it save You,
O the most Merciful of all those who show mercy.

text.en:
This holy month is ascribed to the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.a.) who used
to observe fasting in it and connect it to the obligatory fasting of
Ramadan. He (s.a.w.a.) used to say, “Sha’ban is my month; hence,
Paradise will be incumbently decided for one who observes fasting on
one day of my month.”

Imam Ja’far al-Sadiq (‘a) is reported to have said: Imam Ali ibn al-
Husayn Zayn al-’Abidin (‘a) used to gather all his companions at the
beginning of Sha’ban and say to them, “O my companions, do you
known what this month is? It is Sha’ban.

The Holy Prophet (s.a.w.a.)
used to say, ‘Sha’ban is my month.’ You should thus fast in this
month as a sign of your love for your Prophet and as a sign of seeking
nearness to your Lord. I swear by Him Who grasps my soul in His
Hand, I heard my father, al-Husayn (‘a) saying that he heard Imam
Ali, the Commander of the Faithful, saying

‘As for anyone who
observes fasting in Sha’ban for the love of the Messenger of Allah
(s.a.w.a.) and for seeking nearness to Almighty Allah, Almighty Allah
will love him, draw him near to His honor on the Resurrection Day,
and decide Paradise for him incumbently.’

Shaykh al-Tusi has narrated on the authority of Safwan al-Jammal
that Imam al-Sadiq(a.s.) instructed him to urge all people he knew to
observe fasting in Sha’ban. “May Allah accept me as ransom for you!
There is a reason for this instruction. Is there not?” asked Safwan al-
Jammal. Imam al-Sadiq(a.s.) answered, “Yes, there is.

When his eyes
fell on the new moon of Sha’ban, the Messenger of Allah (a.s.)
ordered a spokesman to declare the following to the people of al-
Madinah: O people of Yathrib! I am the messenger of Allah’s
Messenger to you all (carrying this message). Verily, Sha’ban is my
month; therefore, may Allah have mercy upon him who helps me
undertake my month.”

Imam al-Sadiq(a.s.) then added, “Ameer al-
Momineen (Imam Ali) used to say, ‘I have never missed the fasting in
Sha’ban since I heard the call of the Messenger of Allah in Sha’ban.
Furthermore, I shall never miss it so long as I am alive, God willing.’”
Imam al-Sadiq (a.s.) further said, “To fast in two consecutive
months is repentance before Almighty Allah.”

Isma’il ibn ‘Abd al-Khaliq narrated that when the subject of
observing fasting in Sha’ban was once discussed in the presence of
him, Imam Ja’far al-Sadiq(a.s.) said, “The merits of observing fasting
in Sha’ban are so great that even one who sheds inviolable blood is
expected to be forgiven.”
The general rites are those which are advisedly practiced each day in the month without distinction:

First: It is recommended to say the following imploration for
forgiveness seventy times each day in Sha’ban:

translation.en: I seek forgiveness of Allah, and, repentant, beseech Him.

text.en:
Second: It is recommended to recite the following imploration for
forgiveness seventy times each day in Sha’ban:

segments.arabic: اسْتَغْفِرُ ٱللَّهَ ٱلَّذِي لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ هُوَ ٱلرَحْمٰنُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ ٱلْحَيُّ   ٱلْقَيُّوْمُ  وَاتُوبُ إِلَيْهِ
segments.transliteration: astaghfirullaahallad’ee laa ilaaha illa huwar rahmano rah’eemul h’ayyul qayyoomu wa atoobu ilayhi
