segments.transliteration: wa la fawqahunna wa la tahtahunna wa la baynahunna ilhun yu`badu ghayruhu

translation.en: Nor above them, nor beneath them, nor there is between them any god that is worshipped save Him.

segments.arabic: لَكَ الحَمْدُ حَمْداً لا يَقْوَى عَلَى إحْصَائِهِ إلاَّ أَنْتَ،
segments.transliteration: laka alhamdu hamdan la yaqwa `ala ihsa’ihi illa anta

translation.en: To You be the praise that none can count save You,

segments.arabic: فَصَلّ عَلَى مُحَمّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمّدٍ صَلاةً لا يَقْوَى عَلَى إحْصَائِهَا إلاَّ أَنْتَ.
segments.transliteration: fasalli `ala muhammadin wa ali muhammadin salatan la yaqwa `ala ihsa’ihailla anta

translation.en: So, please bless Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad with the blessings that none can count save You.

text.en:
Seventh: Al-Kaf’ami, in al-Balad al-Amin and al-Misbah, has
quoted from Kitab al-Ikhtiyar of Sayyid Ibn Baqi that if one says the
following supplicatory prayer everyday in Ramadan, Almighty Allah
will forgive the sins he has committed for forty years:

translation.en: O Allah: the Lord of the month of Ramadhan,
In which You revealed the Qur'an,
And You imposed fasting on Your servants:
(Please) confer upon me with the pilgrimage to Your Holy House,

segments.arabic: فِي هذَا العَامِ وَفِي كُلّ عَامٍ،
segments.transliteration: fi hadha al`ami wa fi kulli `amin

translation.en: In this year and every year,
And forgive my serious sins,
For none can forgive them save You,
O the Lord of majesty and honor.

text.en:
Eighth: It is recommended to repeat the following doxology one
hundred times every day in Ramadan, as is mentioned by al-Fayd al-
Kashani in Khulasat al-Adhkar.

segments.arabic: سُبْحانَ الضَّارِّ النَّافِعِ سُبْحانَ القاضِي بِالحَقِّ سُبْحانَ العَليِّ الاَعْلى ، سُبحانَهُ وَبِحَمْدِهِ سُبحانَهُ وَتَعالى

text.en:
Ninth: In his book of al-Muqni’ah, Shaykh al-Mufid has mentioned
that one of the most advisable rites in Ramadan is to invoke Almighty
Allah’s blessings upon the Holy Prophet at least one hundred times a
day; and the more the better.

subcategory: Ramadan - Aamal For The Days of ramadan

text.en:
First: Watching the New Moon (Istihlal): It is highly recommended
to watch the new moon of Ramadan at the first night of it. Moreover,
some scholars have decided this act as obligatory.

Second: When your eyes fall on the new moon, you must not point
to it; rather, you should turn yourself towards the kiblah direction,
raise both your hands towards the sky, and address the new moon with
the following words:

translation.en: Your and my Lord is Allah; the Lord of the worlds.
O Allah: (please do) cause this crescent to carry with it for us security and faith
And safety and Islam
And expedition to whatever You love and please.

segments.arabic: اللّهُمّ بَارِكْ لَنَا فِي شَهْرِنَا هذَا،
segments.transliteration: allhum barik lana fi shahrina hdha,

translation.en: O Allah: (please do) bless us in this month,
And confer upon us with its goodness and aid,

segments.arabic: وَاصْرِفْ عَنَّا ضُرّهُ وَشَرّهُ وَبَلاءَهُ وَفِتْنَتَهُ.
segments.transliteration: wasrif anaa durhu washarhu wabalaahu wafitnatahu.

translation.en: And save us from its harm, evil, ordeals, and sedition.

text.en:
It has been narrated that when the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.a.) would
see the new moon of Ramadan, he would turn his face to the kiblah
direction and say the following:

translation.en: O Allah: (please do) cause this crescent to carry with it for us security and faith
And safety and Islam
And encompassing good health and repulsion of ailments,
And assistance on praying, observing fasting, and doing acts of worship,
And on reciting the Qur'an.
O Allah: (please do) keep us sound for the month of Ramadan,
And accept our deeds during it,
And save us during it,
Until the month of Ramadan will not elapse

segments.arabic: وَقَدْ عَفَوْتَ عَنَّا وَغَفَرْتَ لَنَا وَرَحِمْتَنَا.
segments.transliteration: waqad afawta anaa waghafarta lana warahimtana.

translation.en: Except that You have pardoned us, forgiven us, and had mercy upon us

text.en:
Imam Ja’far al-Sadiq(a.s.) is also reported to have said: When you
see the new moon of Ramadan, you may say the following:

translation.en: O Allah: the month of Ramadan has commenced,
And You have made obligatory upon us to observe fasting during it,
And You revealed the Qur'an in it,

segments.arabic: هُدَىً لِلنَّاسِ وَبَيّنَاتٍ مِنَ الهُدَى وَالفُرْقَانِ.
segments.transliteration: hudaya lilnaasi wabaynati mina alhuday walfurqani.

translation.en: As guidance for people and clear proofs of true guidance and distinction (between the right and the wrong).
O Allah: (please) help us observe fasting in it,
And accept it from us,
And keep us safe during it,
And help us observe it adequately,

segments.arabic: وَسَلّمْهُ لَنَا فِي يُسْرٍ مِنْكَ وَعَافِيَةٍ،
segments.transliteration: wasalmhu lana fi yusri minka waafiati,

translation.en: And keep it sound for us with easiness and good health from You.
You verily have power over all things.
O the All-beneficent, O the All-merciful

text.en:
Third: It is recommended, at the sight of the new moon of
Ramadan, to say the supplication No. 43 of al-Sahifah al-Kamilah al-
Sajjadiyyah. Sayyid Ibn Tawus narrated that Imam Ali ibn al-Husayn
Zayn al-’Abidin(a.s.), once, gazed at the new moon of Ramadan while
he was walking. He therefore stopped and said the following:

translation.en: O obedient creature,
speedy and untiring,
frequenter of the mansions of determination,
moving about in the sphere of governance!
I have faith in Him Who lights up darknesses through you,
illuminates jet-black shadows by you,
appointed you one of the signs of His kingdom
And one of the marks of His authority,
So, He identified time through You,
And humbled you through increase and decrease,
And rising and setting,
And illumination and eclipse.

segments.arabic: فِي كُلّ ذلِكَ أَنْتَ لَهُ مُطِيعٌ،
segments.transliteration: fi kul dhlika aanta lahu mutiu,

translation.en: In all of this you are obedient to Him,
And prompt toward His will.

segments.arabic: سُبْحَانَهُ مَا أَعْجَبَ مَا دَبّرَ مِنْ أَمْرِكَ!
segments.transliteration: subhanahu ma aajaba ma dabra min aamrika!

translation.en: Glory be to Him! How wonderful is what He has arranged in Your situation!
How subtle what He has made for Your task!

segments.arabic: جَعَلَكَ مِفْتَاحَ شَهْرٍ حَادِثٍ لأَمْرٍ حَادِثٍ،
segments.transliteration: jaalaka miftaha shahri hadithi laamri hadithi,

translation.en: He has made You the key to a new month for a new situation.
So I ask Allah, my Lord and Your Lord,
my Creator and Your Creator,
my Determiner and Your Determiner,
my Form-giver and Your Form-giver,

segments.arabic: أَنْ يُصَلّيَ عَلَى مُحَمّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمّدٍ،
segments.transliteration: aan yusalya alay muhamdi wali muhamdi,

translation.en: that He bless Muhammad and his Household

segments.arabic: وَأَنْ يَجْعَلَكَ هِلالَ بَرَكَةٍ لا تَمْحَقُهَا الأَيَّامُ،
segments.transliteration: waan yajalaka hilala barakati la tamhaquha alaayaamu,

translation.en: and appoint You a crescent of blessings not effaced by days
and of purity not defiled by sins;
a crescent of security from blights
and of safety from evil deeds;
a crescent of auspiciousness containing no misfortune,
of prosperity accompanied by no adversity,
of ease not mixed with difficulty,
of good unstained by evil;
a crescent of security and faith,
favor and good-doing,
safety and submission!

segments.arabic: اللّهُمّ صَلّ عَلَى مُحَمّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمّدٍ
segments.transliteration: allhum sal alay muhamdi wali muhamdi

translation.en: O Allah, bless Muhammad and his Household,

segments.arabic: وَاجْعَلْنَا مِنْ أَرْضَى مَنْ طَلَعَ عَلَيْهِ،
segments.transliteration: wajalna min aarday man talaa alayhi,

translation.en: place us among the most satisfied of those over whom the crescent has risen,
the purest of those who have looked upon it,
the most fortunate of those who have worshiped You under it;
give us the success during [the new month] to repent,
preserve us within it from misdeeds and exposition to punishment,
allot to us within it thanksgiving for Your favor,
clothe us during it in the shields of well-being,

segments.arabic: وَأَتْمِمْ عَلَيْنَا بِاسْتِكْمَالِ طَاعَتِكَ فِيهِ المِنّةَ،
segments.transliteration: waatmim alayna biastikmali taatika fihi alminta,

translation.en: and complete for us Your kindness by perfecting therein obedience to You!
Surely You are All-kind, Praiseworthy.

segments.arabic: وَصَلَّى اللّهُ عَلَى مُحَمّدٍ وَآلِهِ الطّيّبِينَ،
segments.transliteration: wasalay allhu alay muhamdi walihi altybina,

translation.en: And bless Muhammad and his Household, the good, the pure.

segments.arabic: وَاجْعَلْ لَنَا فِيهِ عَوْناً مِنْكَ عَلَى مَا نَدَبْتَنَا إلَيْهِ
segments.transliteration: wajal lana fihi awnaa minka alay ma nadabtana alayhi

translation.en: And grant us aid from You to carry out that which You have ordered us to do in it;
Such as the obedience to You which You have made incumbent upon us,

segments.arabic: وَتَقَبّلْهَا إنّكَ الأَكْرَمُ مِنْ كُلّ كَرِيمٍ،
segments.transliteration: wataqablha anka alaakramu min kul karimi,

translation.en: and accept them from us, for You are the most Generous of all those who show generosity
and the most Merciful of all those who show mercy.
Respond! Respond! O the Lord of the Worlds.

text.en:
Fourth: It is recommended for husbands to have sexual intercourse
with their wives at this night. In fact, it is discommended to have
sexual intercourse with wives at the first night of the Arabic months;
rather, this exception is one of the peculiarities of the holy month of
Ramadan.

Fifth: It is highly advisable to bathe oneself at this night. A Hadith
reads that if one washes himself at the first night of Ramadan, one will
be secured against prurigo up to the next Ramadan.

Sixth: It is recommended to bathe oneself in a flowing river and to
pour on the head thirty handfuls of water so as to be mentally pure up
to the next Ramadan.

Seventh: It is recommended to visit the holy tomb of Imam al-
Husayn (a.s.) so that sins will be forgiven and the like of the reward of
those who undertake Hajj and ‘Umrah in that year will be gained.

Eighth: It is recommended, at this night, to begin with the one
thousand unit prayer that has been previously mentioned

Ninth: It is recommended to offer a two unit prayer reciting in each
unit Surah <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
 and Surah <ref=1:6>al-An’am</ref> 
 and to pray
Almighty Allah for security against apprehensions and ailments.

Tenth: It is recommended to say the aforementioned supplicatory
prayer. This supplicatory prayer has been mentioned within the rites of the
last night of Sha’ban.

Eleventh: It is recommended to say the following supplicatory
prayer while raising the hands after the Maghrib Prayer. The
supplicatory prayer has been reported in al-Iqbal from Imam
Muhammad and Jawad al-Taqi(a.s.):

segments.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ يا مَنْ يَمْلِكُ التَّدْبِيرَ وَهُوَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيٍْ قَدِيرٌ ، يا مَنْ يَعْلَمُ خائِنَةَ الاَعْيُنِ وَما تُخْفِي الصُّدُورُ وَتُجِنُّ الضَّمِيرُ وَهُوَ اللَّطِيفُ الخَبِيرُ ، اللَّهُمَّ اجْعَلْنا مِمَّنْ نَوى فَعَمِلَ وَلا تَجْعَلْنا مِمَّنْ شَقِيَ فَكَسِلَ وَلا مِمَّنْ هُوَ عَلى غَيْرِ عَمَلٍ يَتَّكِلُ ، اللَّهُمَّ صَحِّحْ أَبْدانَنا مِنَ العِلَلِ وَأَعِنَّا عَلى ما أَفْتَرَضْتَ عَلَيْنا مِنَ العَمَلِ حَتَّى يَنْقَضِيَ عَنَّا شَهْرُكَ هذا وَقَدْ أَدَّيْنا مَفْرُوضَكَ فِيْهِ عَلَيْنا ، اللَّهُمَّ أَعِنَّا عَلى صِيامِهِ وَوَفِّقْنا لِقِيامِهِ وَنَشِّطْنا فِيْهِ لِلصَّلاةِ وَلا تَحْجُبْنا مِنَ القَرأَةِ وَسَهِّلْ لَنا فِيْهِ إِيْتاءَ الزَّكاةِ ، اللَّهُمَّ لا تُسَلِّطْ عَلَيْنا وَصَباً وَلا تَعَباً وَلا سَقْماً وَلا عَطَباً ، اللَّهُمَّ ارْزُقْنا الاِفْطارَ مِنْ رِزْقِكَ الحَلالِ ، اللَّهُمَّ سَهِّلْ لَنا فِيْهِ ما قَسَمْتَهُ مِنْ رِزْقِكَ وَيَسِّرْ ما قَدَّرْتَهُ مِنْ أَمْرِكَ وَاجْعَلْهُ حَلالاً طَيِّباً نَقِيّاً مِنْ الاثامِ خالِصاً مِنَ الاصارِ وَالاَجْرامِ اللَّهُمَّ لا تُطْعِمْنا إِلاّ طَيِّباً غَيْرَ خَبِيثٍ وَلا حَرامٍ وَاجْعَلْ رِزْقَكَ لَنا حَلالاً لايَشُوبُهُ دَنَسٌ وَلا أَسْقامٌ. يا مَنْ عِلْمُهُ بِالسِّرِّ كَعِلْمِهِ بِالاِعْلانِ يا مُتَفَضِّلا عَلى عِبادِهِ بِالاِحْسانِ يا مَنْ هُوَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيٍْ قَدِيرٌ وَبِكُلِّ شَيٍْ عَلِيمٌ خَبِيرٌ أَلْهِمْنا ذِكْرَكَ وَجَنِّبْنا عُسْرَكَ وَأَنِلْنا يُسْرَكَ وَاهْدِنا لِلرَّشادِ وَوَفِّقْنا لِلسَّدادِ وَاعْصِمْنا مِنَ البَلايا وَصُنّا مِنَ الاَوْزارِ وَالخَطايا ، يا مَنْ لا يَغْفِرُ عَظِيمَ الذُّنُوبِ غَيْرُهُ وَلا يَكْشِفُ السُّوءَ إِلاّ هُوَ يا أرْحَمَ الرَّاحِمِينَ وَأَكْرَمَ الاَكْرَمِينَ ، صَلِّ عَلى مُحَمَّدٍ وَأَهْلِ بَيْتِهِ الطَّيِّبِينَ وَاجْعَلْ صِيامَنا مَقْبُولا وَبِالبِرِّ وَالتَّقْوى مَوْصُولا وَكذلِكَ فَاجْعَلْ سَعْيَنا مَشْكُوراً وَقِيامَنا مَبْرُوراً وَقُرْآنَنا مَرْفُوعاً [١] وَدُعائَنا مَسْمُوعاً وَاهْدِنا لِلْحُسْنى وَجَنِّبْنا العُسْرى وَيَسِّرْنا لِلْيُسْرى وَأَعْلِ لَنا الدَّرَجاتِ وَضاعِفْ لَنا الحَسَناتِ وَاقْبَلْ مِنِّا الصَّومَ وَالصَّلاةَ وَاسْمَعْ مِنَّا الدَّعَواتِ وَاغْفِرْ لَنا الخَطِيئاتِ وَتَجاوَزْ عَنَّا السَّيِّئاتِ ، وَاجْعَلْنا مِنَ العامِلِينَ الفائِزِينَ وَلا تَجْعَلْنا مِنَ المَغْضُوبِ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلا الضَّآلِّينَ حَتّى يَنْقَضِي شَهْرُ رَمَضانَ عَنَّا وَقَدْ قَبِلْتَ فِيْهِ صِيامَنا وَقِيامَنا وَزَكَّيْتَ فِيْهِ أَعْمالَنا وَغَفْرْتَ فِيْهِ ذُنُوبَنا وَأَجْزَلْتَ فِيْهِ مِنْ كُلِّ خَيْرٍ نَصِيبَنا ، فَإِنَّكَ الاِلهُ المُجِيبُ وَالرَّبُّ القَرِيبُ وَأَنْتَ بِكُلِ شَيٍْ مُحِيطٌ

text.en:
Twelfth: It is recommended to say the following supplicatory
prayer that is reported from Imam Ja’far al-Sadiq(a.s.) as is mentioned
in the book of al-Iqbal:

segments.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ رَبَّ شَهرِ رَمَضانَ ، مُنَزِّلَ القُرْآنَ ، هذا شَهرُ رَمَضانَ الَّذِي أَنْزَلْتَ فِيهِ القُرْآنَ وأَنْزَلْتَ فِيهِ آياتٍ بَيِّناتٍ مِنَ الهُدى والفُرْقانِ. اللَّهُمَّ ارْزُقْنا صِيامَهُ ، وَأَعِنّا عَلى قِيامِهِ. اللَّهُمَّ سَلِّمْهُ لَنا وَسَلِّمْنا فِيهِ ، وَتَسَلَّمْهُ مِنّا في يُسْرٍ مِنْكَ وَمُعافاةٍ ، وَاجْعَلْ فِيما تَقْضِي وَتُقَدِّرُ مِنَ الاَمْرِ المَحْتُومِ وَفِيما تَفْرُقُ مِنَ الاَمْرِ الحَكِيمِ فِي لَيْلَةِ القَدْرِ مِنَ القَضاء الَّذِي لا يُرَدُّ وَلا يُبَدَّلُ أَنْ تَكْتُبَنِي مِنْ حُجَّاجِ بَيْتِكَ الحَرامِ المَبْرُورِ حَجُّهُمُ المَشْكُورِ سَعْيُهُمُ المَغْفُورِ ذُنُوبُهُمُ ، المُكَفَّرِ عَنْهُمْ سَيِّئاتُهُمْ ، وَاجْعَلْ فِيما تَقْضِي وَتُقَدِّرُ أَنْ تُطِيلَ لِي فِي عُمرِي ، وَتُوَسِّعَ عَلَيَّ مِنَ الرِّزْقِ الحَلالِ

text.en:
Thirteenth: It is recommended to say <ref=3:68>Sahifa Sajjadiya-supplication</ref>

of al-Sahifah al-Kamilah al-Sajjadiyyah, which begins with the
following statement

Fourteenth: It is recommended to say the lengthy supplicatory
prayer that is mentioned by Sayyid Ibn Tawus in Iqbal al-A’mal and
beings with the following statement
Fifteenth: It is recommended to say the following:

segments.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّهُ قَدْ دَخَلَ شَهْرُ رَمَضانَ. اللَّهُمَّ رَبَّ شَهْرِ رَمَضانَ الَّذِي أَنْزَلْتَ فِيهِ القُرْآنَ ، وَجَعَلْتَهُ بَيِّناتٍ مِنَ الهُدى وَالفُرْقانِ. اللَّهُمَّ فَبارِكْ لَنا فِي شَهْرِ رَمَضانَ ، وَأَعِنّا عَلى صِيامِهِ وَصَلَواتِهِ وتَقَبَّلْهُ مِنّا.

text.en:
According to a Hadith, the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.a.) used to say this
supplicatory prayerat the beginning of Ramadan.

Sixteenth: The Holy Prophet (s.a.w.a.) is also reported to have said
the following supplicatory prayer at the first night of Ramadan:

segments.arabic: الحَمْدُ للهِ الَّذِي أَكْرَمَنا بِكَ أَيُّها الشَّهْرُ المُبارَكُ. اللَّهُمَّ فَقَوِّنا عَلى صِيامِنا وَقِيامِنا ، وَثَبِّتْ أَقْدامَنا ، وَانْصُرْنا عَلى القَوْمِ الكافِرِينَ. اللَّهُمَّ أَنتَ الواحِدُ فَلا وَلَدَ لَكَ ، وَأَنْتَ الصَّمَدُ فَلا شِبْهَ لَكَ ، وَأَنتَ العَزِيزُ فَلا يُعِزُّكَ شَيٌْ وَأَنْتَ الغَنيُّ وَأَنا الفَقِيرُ ، وَأَنْتَ المَوْلى وَأَنا العَبْدُ ، وَأَنْتَ الغَفُورُ وَأَنا المُذْنِبُ ، وَأَنْتَ الرَّحِيمُ وَأَنا المُخْطِيُ ، وَأَنْتَ الخالِقُ وَأَنا المَخْلُوقُ ، وَأَنْتَ الحَيُ وَأَنا المَيِّتُ ، أَسْأَلُكَ بِرَحْمَتِكَ أَنْ تَغْفِرَ لِي وَتَرْحَمَنِي وَتَجاوَزَ عَنِّي إِنَّكَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيٍْ قَدِيرٌ

text.en:
Seventeenth: As has been previously mentioned in the book, it is
recommended to say the supplicatory prayer known as <ref=3:233>Dua jawshan kabeer</ref>
 at the first night of Ramadan.

Eighteenth: It is recommended to say the supplicatory prayer
known as <ref=3:1042>Dua al-Hajj</ref> which has been previously mentioned in this
book.

Nineteenth: It is highly advisable to recite the Holy Qur’an very
frequently at the first hours of Ramadan. In this respect, it has been
narrated that Imam Ja’far al-Sadiq(a.s.) used to say the following
supplicatory prayer before he would start reciting the Holy Qur’an:

segments.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَشْهَدُ أَنَّ هذا كِتابُكَ المُنْزَلُ مِنْ عِنْدِكَ عَلى رَسُولِكَ مُحمَّدٍ بْنِ عَبْدِ الله صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله وَكَلامُكَ النَّاطِقُ عَلى لِسانِ نَبِيِّكَ جَعَلْتَهُ هادِيا مِنْكَ إِلى خَلْقِكَ ، وَحَبْلاً مُتَّصِلاً فِيما بَيْنَكَ وَبَيْنَ عِبادِكَ. اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي نَشَرْتُ عَهْدَكَ وَكِتابَكَ. اللَّهُمَّ فاجْعَل نَظَرِي فِيْهِ عِبادَةً ، وَقِرأَتِي فِيْهِ فِكْراً ، وَفِكْرِي فِيْهِ اعْتِباراً وَاجْعَلْنِي مِمَّنْ اتَّعَظَ بِبَيانِ مَواعِظِكَ فِيْهِ ، وَاجْتَنَبَ مَعاصِيَكَ ، وَلا تَطْبَعْ عِنْدَ قِرأَتِي عَلى سَمْعِي ، وَلا تَجْعَلْ عَلى بَصَرِي غِشاوَةً وَلا تَجْعَلْ قِرأَتِي قِرأَةً لا تَدَبُّرَ فِيها بَلْ اجْعَلْنِي أَتَدَبَّرُ آياتِهِ وَأَحْكامَهُ ، آخِذا بِشَرايِعِ دِينِكَ ، وَلا تَجْعَلَ نَظَرِي فِيْهِ غَفْلَةً ، وَلا قِرأَتِي هَذَراً ، إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ الرَّؤُوفُ الرَّحِيمُ.

text.en:
When he finished reciting the Holy Qur’an, Imam al-Sadiq(a.s.)
used to say the following supplicatory prayer:

segments.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي قَدْ قَرَأْتُ ما قَضَيْتَ مِنْ كِتابِكَ الَّذِي أَنْزَلْتَهُ عَلى نَبِيِّكَ الصَّادِقِ صَلَّى الله عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ فَلَكَ الحَمْدُ رَبَّنا. اللَّهُمَّ اجْعَلْنِي مِمَّنْ يُحِلُّ حَلالَهُ ، وَيُحَرِّمُ حَرامَهُ ، وَيُؤْمِنُ بِمُحْكَمِهِ وَمُتَشابِهِهِ ، وَاجْعَلْهُ لِي أُنْساً فِي قَبْرِي ، وَأُنْساً فِي حَشْرِي وَاجْعَلْنِي مِمَّنْ تُرَقِّيْهِ بِكُلِّ آيَةٍ قَرَأَها دَرَجَةً فِي أَعْلى عِلِّيِّينَ ، آمِينَ رَبَّ العالَمِينَ

text.en:
First: It is recommended to bathe oneself in flowing water and to
pour on the head thirty handfuls of water, for such a deed brings about
security against all pains and ailments up to the next year.

Second: It is recommended to wash one’s face with a handful of
rose water so as to be saved against humiliation and poverty, and to
pour some of it on the head so as to be saved from pleurisy.

Third: It is recommended to offer the prayer of the first of the
month that consists of two units and to give alms after that.

Fourth: It is recommended to offer a two-unit prayer at the first of
which Surah <ref=1:1>al-Fatehah</ref>
 and Surah <ref=1:48>al-Fath</ref>
 should be recited
and at the second, Surah <ref=1:1>al-Fatehah</ref> and any other Surah. If one
offers this prayer, Almighty Allah will save him from all evils and
will keep him under His custody up to the next year.

Fifth: When the dawn breaks forth, it is recommended to say the
following supplicatory prayer:

segments.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ قَدْ حَضَرَ شَهْرُ رَمَضانَ وَقَدِ افْتَرَضْتَ عَلَيْنا صِيامَهُ ، وَأَنْزَلْتَ فِيْهِ القُرْآنَ هُدىً لِلنَّاسِ وَبَيِّناتٍ مِنَ الهُدى وَالفُرْقانِ. اللَّهُمَّ أَعِنّا عَلى صِيامِهِ ، وَتَقَبَّلْهُ مِنّا ، وَتَسَلَّمْهُ مِنّا ، وَسَلِّمْهُ لَنا فِي يُسْرٍ مِنْكَ وَعافِيَةٍ ، إِنَّكَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيٍْ قَدِيرٌ

text.en:
Sixth: If one has not had the opportunity to say it at night, it is
recommended to say the <ref=3:68>Sahifa sajjadiya-supplication44</ref> of al-Sahifah al-Kamilah
al-Sajjadiyyah, which has been previously cited.

Seventh: In his book of Zad al-Ma’ad, ‘Allamah al-Majlisi has
mentioned that al-Kulayni, al-Tusi, and others, through an authentic
chain of authority, have reported Imam Musa al-Kazim (a.s.) as
saying, “Say the following supplicatory prayer in the month of
Ramadan (on the first day of it, as is understood by scholars), for
whoever prays Almighty Allah with this supplicatory prayer purely
and without associating any false purposes or showing off, will be
saved throughout that year from all seditious and misleading matters
as well as all afflictions that may injure his religion or his body. In
addition, Almighty Allah will save him from the evils of all the
misfortunes that will take place that year.” The supplicatory prayer is
as follows:

segments.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ بِاسْمِكَ الَّذِي دانَ لَهُ كُلُّ شَيٍْ ، وَبِرَحْمَتِكَ الَّتِي وَسِعَتْ كُلَّ شَيٍْ ، وَبِعَظَمَتِكَ الَّتِي تَواضَعَ لَها كُلُّ شَيٍْ ، وَبِعِزَّتِكَ الَّتِي قَهَرْتَ بِها كُلَّ شَيٍْ ، وَبِقُوَّتِكَ الَّتِي خَضَعَ لَها كُلُّ شَيٍْ ، وَبِجَبَرُوتِكَ الَّتِي غَلَبَتْ كُلَّ شَيٍْ ، وَبِعِلْمِكَ الَّذِي أَحاطَ بِكُلِّ شَيٍْ ، يا نُورُ يا قُدُّوسُ ، يا أوَّلَ قَبْلَ كُلِّ شَيٍْ ، وَيا باقِيا [١] بَعْدَ كُلِّ شَيٍْ ، يا الله يا رَحْمنُ صَلِّ عَلى مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ وَاغْفِرْ لِيَ الذُّنُوبَ الَّتِي تُغَيِّرُ النِّعَمَ ، وَاغْفِرْ لِيَ الذُّنُوبَ الَّتِي تُنْزِلُ النِّقَمَ ، وَاغْفِرْ لِي الذُّنُوبَ الَّتِي تَقْطَعُ الرَّجاءَ ، وَاغْفِرْ لِي الذُّنُوبَ الَّتِي تُدِيلُ [٢] الأَعْداءَ ، وَاغْفِرْ لِيَ الذُّنُوبَ الَّتِي تَرُدُّ الدُّعاءَ ، وَاغْفِرْ لِي الذُّنُوبَ الَّتِي يُسْتَحَقُّ بِها نُزُولُ البَلاِء وَاغْفِرْ لِي الذُّنُوبَ الَّتِي تَحْبِسُ غَيْثَ السَّماء ، وَاغْفِرْ لِي الذُّنُوبَ الَّتِي تَكْشِفُ الغِطاء ، وَاغْفِرْ لِي الذُّنُوبَ الَّتِي تُعَجِّلُ الفَناء ، وَاغْفِر لِيَ الذُّنُوبَ الَّتِي تُورِثُ النَّدَمَ ، وَاغْفِرْ لِيَ الذُّنُوبَ الَّتِي تَهْتِكُ العِصَمَ ، وَأَلْبِسْنِي دِرْعَكَ الحَصِينَةَ الَّتِي لا تُرامُ ، وَعافِنِي مِنْ شَرِّ ما اُحاذِرُ بِاللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهارِ فِي مُسْتَقْبَلِ سَنَتِي هذِه. اللَّهُمَّ رَبَّ السَّماواتِ السَّبْعِ ، وَرَبَّ الأَرْضِينَ السَّبْعِ وَمافِيهِنَّ وَمابَيْنَهُنَّ وَرَبَّ العَرْشِ العَظِيمِ ، وَرَبَّ السَّبْعِ المَثانِي وَالقُرْآنِ العَظِيمِ ، وَرَبَّ إِسْرافِيلَ وَمِيكائِيلَ وَجَبْرائِيلَ ، ورَبَّ مُحَمَّدٍ صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله سَيِّدِ المُرْسَلِينَ وَخاتَمِ النَّبِيِّينَ ، أَسْأَلُكَ بِكَ وَبِما سَمَّيْتَ بِهِ نَفْسَكَ يا عَظِيمُ أَنتَ الَّذِي تَمُنُّ بِالعَظِيمِ ، وَتَدْفَعُ كُلَّ

مَحْذُورٍ ، وَتُعْطِي كُلَّ جَزِيلٍ ، وَتُضاعِفُ الحَسَناتِ [١] بِالقَلِيلِ وَبالكَثِيرِ وَتَفْعَلُ ماتَشاءُ يا قَدِيرُ يا الله يا رَحْمنُ صَلِّ عَلى مُحَمَّدٍ وأَهْلِ بَيْتِهِ ، وَأَلْبِسْنِي فِي مُسْتَقْبَلِ سَنَتِي هذِهِ سِتْرَكَ ، وَنَضِّرْ وَجْهِي بِنُورِكَ ، وَأَحِبَّنِي بِمَحَبَّتِكَ ، وَبَلِّغْنِي رِضْوانَكَ ، وَشَرِيفَ كَرامَتكَ ، وَجَسِيمَ عَطِيَّتِكَ ، وَأَعْطِنِي مِنْ خَيْرِ ما عِنْدَكَ وَمِنْ خَيْرِ ما أَنْتَ مُعْطِيهِ أَحَداً مِنْ خَلْقِكَ ، وَأَلْبِسْنِي مَعَ ذلِكَ عافِيَتَكَ يا مَوْضِعَ كُلِّ شَكْوى ، وَيا شاهِدَ كُلِّ نَجْوى ، وَيا عالِمَ كُلِّ خَفِيَّةٍ ، وَيادافِعَ ماتَشاءُ مِنْ بَلِيَّةٍ ، يا كَرِيمَ العَفْوِ يا حَسَنَ التَّجاوُزِ ، تَوَفَّنِي عَلى مِلَّةِ إِبْراهِيمَ وَفِطْرَتِهِ ، وَعَلى دِينِ مُحَمَّدٍ صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله وَسُنَّتِهِ ، وَعَلى خَيْرِ الوَفاةِ فَتَوَفَّنِي مُوالِيا لاَوْلِيائِكَ ، وَمُعادِيا [٢] لاَعْدائِكَ. اللَّهُمَّ وَجَنِّبْنِي فِي هذِهِ السَّنَةِ كُلَّ عَمَلٍ أَوْ قَوْلٍ أَوْ فِعْلٍ يُباعِدُنِي مِنْكَ ، وَاجْلِبْنِي إِلى كُلِّ عَمَلٍ أَوْ قَوْلٍ أَوْ فِعْلٍ يُقَرِّبُنِي مِنْكَ فِي هذِهِ السَّنَةِ يا أرْحَمَ الرَّاحِمِينَ ، وَامْنَعْنِي مِنْ كُلِّ عَمَلٍ أَوْ قَوْلٍ أَوْ فِعْلٍ يَكُونُ مِنِّي أَخافُ ضَرَرَ عاقِبَتِهِ ، وَأَخافُ مَقْتَكَ إِيَّايَ عَلَيْهِ حِذارَ أَنْ تَصْرِفَ وَجْهَكَ الكَرِيمَ عَنِّي فَأَسْتَوْجِبَ بِهِ نَقْصا مِنْ حَظٍّ لِي عِنْدَكَ ، يا رَؤُوفُ يا رَحِيمُ. اللَّهُمَّ اجْعَلْنِي فِي مُسْتَقْبَلِ سَنَتِي هذِهِ فِي حِفْظِكَ وَفِي جِوارِكَ وَفِي كَنَفِكَ ، وَجَلِّلْنِي سِتْرَ عافِيَتِكَ ، وَهَبْ لِي كَرامَتَكَ ، عَزَّ جارُكَ ، وَجَلَّ ثَناؤُكَ ، وَلا إِلهَ غَيْرُكَ. اللَّهُمَّ اجْعَلْنِي تابِعاً لِصالِحِي مَنْ مَضى مِنْ أَوْلِيائِكَ ، وَأَلْحِقْنِي بِهِمْ ، وَاجْعَلْنِي مُسَلِّما لِمَنْ قالَ بِالصِّدْقِ عَلَيْكَ مِنْهُمْ وأَعُوذُ بِكَ اللَّهُمَّ [٣] أَنْ تُحِيطَ بِي خَطِيئَتِي وَظُلْمِي وَإِسْرافِي عَلى نَفْسِي وَاتِّباعِي لِهَوايَ وَاشْتِغالِي بِشَهَواتِي فَيَحُولُ ذلِكَ بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَ رَحْمَتِكَ وَرِضْوانِكَ فَأَكُونُ مَنْسِيّا عِنْدَكَ ، مُتَعَرِّضاً لِسَخَطِكَ وَنِقْمَتِكَ. اللَّهُمَّ وَفِّقْنِي لِكُلِّ عَمَلٍ صالِحٍ تَرْضى بِهِ عَنِّي ، وَقَرِّبْنِي إِلَيْكَ زُلْفى. اللَّهُمَّ كَما كَفَيْتَ نَبِيَّكَ مُحَمَّداً صَلَّى

لله عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ هَوْلَ عَدُوِّهِ ، وَفَرَّجْتَ هَمَّهُ ، وَكَشَفْتَ غَمَّهُ ، وَصَدَّقْتَهُ وَعْدَكَ ، وَأَنْجَزْتَ لَهُ عَهْدَكَ. اللَّهُمَّ فَبِذلِكَ فَاكْفِنِي هَوْلَ هذِهِ السَّنَةِ وَآفاتِها وأَسْقامَها وَفِتْنَتَها وَشُرُورِها وَأَحْزانَها وَضَيقَ المَعاشِ فِيها ، وَبَلِّغْنِي بِرَحْمَتِكَ كَمالَ العافِيَةِ بِتَمامِ دَوامِ النِّعْمَةِ عِنْدِي إِلى مُنْتَهى أَجَلِي ، أَسْأَلُكَ سُؤالَ مَنْ أَساءَ وَظَلَمَ وَاسْتَكانَ وَاعْتَرَفَ ، وَأَسْأَلُكَ أَنْ تَغْفِرَ لِي ما مَضى مِنَ الذُّنُوبِ الَّتِي حَصَرْتَها حَفَظَتُكَ وَأَحْصَتْها مَلائِكَتِكَ عَلَيَّ ، وَأَنْ تَعْصِمَنِي إِلهِي مِنَ الذُّنُوبِ فِيما بَقِيَ مِنْ عُمْرِي إِلى مُنْتَهى أَجَلِي ، يا الله يا رَحْمنُ يا رَحِيمُ  صَلِّ عَلى مُحَمَّدٍ وَأَهْلِ بَيْتِ مُحَمَّدٍ وَآتِنِي كُلَّ ماسَأَلْتُكَ وَرَغِبْتُ إِلَيْكَ فِيْهِ فَإِنَّكَ أَمَرْتَنِي بِالدُّعاءِ وَتَكَفَّلْتَ لِي بِالاِجابَةِ ، يا أرْحَمَ الرَّاحِمِينَ

text.en:
It is worth mentioning that Sayyid Ibn Tawus has mentioned this
supplicatory prayer within the rites of the first night of Ramadan.

On the sixth of Ramadan, AH 201, Imam Ali ibn Musa al-
Rida(a.s.) was appointed as the crown prince. Sayyid Ibn Tawus has
mentioned that it is recommended to offer a thanksgiving two-unit
prayer in which one should recite Surah <ref=1:1>al-Fatehah</ref>
 once and repeat
Surah  <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref> twenty-five times.

Three rites are dedicated to the thirteenth night of Ramadan, which
is the first of the White Nights:
First: It is recommended to bathe oneself at this night.

Second: It is recommended to offer a four-unit prayer and to recite
at each unit Surah <ref=1:1>al-Fatehah</ref>
 once and Surah <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
 twenty-five
times.

Third: It is recommended to offer a two-unit prayer just like the
prayer that is decided to the thirteenth nights of Rajab and Sha’ban. In
this prayer, it is recommended to recite Surahs al-Faatehah, Yasin, al-
Mulk, and al-Tawheed.
THE 14TH NIGHT

t is recommended to offer at this night a prayer consisting of four
units, each two alone. This prayer is similar to the previous one. As
has been previously mentioned, if one says the famous supplicatory
prayer known as <ref=3:1041>Dua Mujeer</ref> on the White Days of Ramadan, all his
sins will be forgiven even if they are as many as rain drops, tree
leaves, and desert pebbles.
It is a blessed night and there are some A'amal
First: It is recommended to bathe oneself at this night.

Second: It is recommended to visit the holy tomb of Imam al-
Husayn (a.s.).

Third: It is recommended to offer a six unit prayer reciting at each
unit Surahs <ref=1:1>al-Fatehah</ref>
, <ref=1:36>Yasin</ref>, <ref=1:67>al-Mulk</ref>
, and <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
.

Fourth: It is recommended to offer a one hundred unit prayer
reciting at each unit Surah <ref=1:1>al-Fatehah</ref>
 once and repeating Surah <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref> ten times. In al-Muqni’ah, Shaykh al-Mufid has reported
Imam AliAmeer al-Momineen(a.s.) as saying, “Whoever offers this
prayer, Almighty Allah will appoint for him ten angels protecting him
from his human and jinn enemies and will also appoint thirty angels to
secure him against Hellfire at the hour of his death.”

Fifth: When he was asked about the rewards of one who attends at
the holy tomb of Imam al-Husayn (a.s.) at the fifteenth night of
Ramadan, Imam Ja’far al-Sadiq(a.s.) said, “Congratulations be to him!
At the fifteenth night of Ramadan, one who attends at the tomb of
Imam al-Husayn (a.s.) and offers a ten unit prayer after the obligatory
Isha’ Prayer other than the Night Prayer (Salat al-Layl), reciting in
each unit Surah <ref=1:1>al-Fatehah</ref>
 once and repeating Surah <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
 ten
times, and then seeks Almighty Allah’s protection against Hellfire - if
one does this, Almighty Allah will include him with those whom He
releases from Hellfire and he will not die before he sees in dream
angels conveying to him the good tidings of entering Paradise and
security against Hellfire.”

THE FIFTEENTH OF RAMADAN
On the fifteenth of Ramadan, AH 2, Imam al-Hasan al-
Mujtaba(a.s.) was born.
In the word of Shaykh al-Mufid, Imam Muhammad al-Taqi(a.s.)
was born on the fifteenth of Ramadan, AH 195. However, it is widely
known that Imam Muhammad al-Taqi was not born at this date.
Anyhow, the fifteenth of Ramadan is a blessed day; to give alms and
to do righteous deeds on this day bring about great merits.

At this blessed night, the two armies of the Muslims, under the
leadership of the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.a.), and the polytheists of
Quraysh faced each other at Badr Spring. On the seventeenth of
Ramadan, the Battle of Badr took place when Almighty Allah gave
victory to the Holy Prophet’s army over the polytheists’. That was the
Greatest conquest of Islam. In view of that, our master scholars have
declared that it is highly recommended to give alms and thank
Almighty Allah on this day. Besides, to bathe oneself and to practice
acts of worship at this night achieve a great reward.

According to numerous narrations, the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.a.)
asked his companions, at the night before the Battle of Badr, to go to
Badr Spring and bring some water. All the companions kept silent and
none of them had the courage to undertake this mission. Imam
AliAmeer al-Momineen(a.s.), therefore, took a skin of water and went
towards that spring for getting some water. It was so murky, bitterly
cold, and windy night. When Imam Ali(a.s.) reached that dark, deep
spring, he could not find a bucket to use for taking water. He therefore
had to step down that spring and fill in the skin with water. On his
way back to the Holy Prophet’s camp, he faced such a strong storm
that made him sit on the ground. When it calmed down, he continued
marching. Then, another storm that was as intense as the first blew so
heavily that he had to sit on the ground. When it calmed down, Imam
Ali continued marching. Again, a third storm that was as intense as the
previous ones blew so heavily that he had to sit on the ground. When
it calmed down, he continued marching until he reached the Holy
Prophet (s.a.w.a.) who, then, asked why he had been late. “I faced
three heavy storms that made me sit to the ground three times. I
therefore had to wait until they would calm down,” explained Imam
Ali(a.s.). “Did you know what these three storms were, Ali?” asked
the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.a.).

As Imam Ali(a.s.) answered in the
negative, the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.a.) explained, “The first storm was
Archangel Gabriel with one thousand angels who, altogether, came to
greet you. The second storm was Archangel Michael with one
thousand angels who, altogether, came to greet you. The third storm
was Archangel Seraph with one thousand angels who, altogether,
came to greet you. All these angels descended to the earth to back us.”

To this event has a scholar referred, saying, “Imam AliAmeer al-
Momineen(a.s.) had three thousand merits at one night only.

name: Salats for The Nights of ramadan

text.en:
The First Night Prayer: Four units with Surah <ref=1:1>al-Fatehah</ref>
 once and
Surah <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
 fifteen times

The Second Night Prayer: Four units with Surah <ref=1:1>al-Fatehah</ref>
 once
and Surah <ref=1:97>al-Qadr</ref>  twenty times.

The Third Night Prayer: Ten units with Surah <ref=1:1>al-Fatehah</ref>
 once and
Surah <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
 fifty times.

The Fourth Night Prayer: Eight units with Surah <ref=1:1>al-Fatehah</ref>
 once
and Surah <ref=1:97>al-Qadr</ref>  twenty times.

The Fifth Night Prayer: Two units with Surah <ref=1:1>al-Fatehah</ref>
 and
<ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
 fifty times. After accomplishment, SWALAWAT
is repeated one hundred times:

segments.arabic: اللهُمَّ صَلّ عَلى مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ.

text.en:
The Sixth Night Prayer: Four units with Surah <ref=1:1>al-Fatehah</ref>
 and
Surah <ref=1:67>al-Mulk</ref>

The Seventh Night Prayer: Four units with Surah <ref=1:1>al-Fatehah</ref>
 once
and Surah <ref=1:97>al-Qadr</ref>  thirteen times.

The Eighth Night Prayer: Two units with Surah <ref=1:1>al-Fatehah</ref>
 and
Surah <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
 ten times. After accomplishment, SUBHANA
ALLAH is repeated one thousand times:

The Ninth Night Prayer: Six units offered between the Maghrib and
Isha’ obligatory Prayers with Surah <ref=1:1>al-Fatehah</ref>
 and <ref=1:2:255>Ayah al-Kursi</ref>
seven times. After accomplishment, SWALAWAT is repeated fifty
times:

segments.arabic: اللّهُمَّ صَلّ عَلى مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ.

text.en:
The Tenth Night Prayer: Twenty units with Surah <ref=1:1>al-Fatehah</ref>
 once
and Surah <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
 thirty times.

The Eleventh Night Prayer: Two units with Surah <ref=1:1>al-Fatehah</ref>
 once
and Surah <ref=1:108>al-Kawthar</ref> 
 (No. 108) twenty times.

The Twelfth Night Prayer: Eight units with Surah <ref=1:1>al-Fatehah</ref>
 once
and Surah <ref=1:97>al-Qadr</ref> thirty times.

The Thirteenth Night Prayer: Four units with Surah <ref=1:1>al-Fatehah</ref>

once and Surah <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
 twenty-five times.

The Fourteenth Night Prayer: Six units with Surah <ref=1:1>al-Fatehah</ref>

once and Surah <ref=1:99>al-Zalzalah</ref> 
 thirty times.

The Fifteenth Night Prayer: Four units; in the first two units, Surah
<ref=1:1>al-Fatehah</ref>
 is recited once and Surah <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
 repeated one
hundred times. In the last two units, Surah <ref=1:1>al-Fatehah</ref>
 is recited once
and Surah <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
 repeated fifty times.

The Sixteenth Night Prayer: Twelve units with Surah <ref=1:1>al-Fatehah</ref>

once and Surah <ref=1:102>al-Takathur</ref> 
 twelve times.

The Seventeenth Night Prayer: Two units with Surah <ref=1:1>al-Fatehah</ref>

once and any selected Surah in the first unit and Surah <ref=1:1>al-Fatehah</ref>
 once and Surah <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
 one hundred times. After
accomplishment, LA ILAHA ILLA ALLAH is repeated one hundred
times:

The Eighteenth Night Prayer: Four units with Surah <ref=1:1>al-Fatehah</ref>

once and Surah <ref=1:108>al-Kawthar</ref> 
 twenty-five times.

The Nineteenth Night Prayer: Fifty units with Surah <ref=1:1>al-Fatehah</ref>

and Surah <ref=1:99>al-Zalzalah</ref>

The Twentieth to Twenty-Fourth Nights Prayer: At these nights, it
is recommended to offer an eight unit prayer with any selected Surahs.

The Twenty-Fifth Night Prayer: Eight units with Surah <ref=1:1>al-Fatehah</ref>

once and Surah <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
 ten times.

The Twenty-Sixth Night Prayer: Eight units with Surah al <ref=1:1>al-Fatehah</ref>
 once and Surah <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
 one hundred times.

The Twenty-Seventh Night Prayer: Four units with Surah <ref=1:1>al-Fatehah</ref>
 once and Surah al-Mulk. If this is impossible, replace Surah
<ref=1:67>al-Mulk</ref>  with repeating Surah <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
 twenty-five times.

The Twenty-Eighth Night Prayer: Six units with Surah <ref=1:1>al-Fatehah</ref>

once and <ref=1:2:255>Ayah al-Kursi</ref>
 one hundred times, Surah <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
 one
hundred times, and Surah <ref=1:108>al-Kawthar</ref> 
 one hundred times. After
accomplishment, SWALAWAT is repeated one hundred times:

According to my own readings in the books of narrations, I found
that the prayer of the twenty-eighth night of Ramadan consists of six
units with Surah <ref=1:1>al-Fatehah</ref>
 once, <ref=1:2:255>Ayah al-Kursi</ref>
 ten times, Surah <ref=1:108>al-Kawthar</ref>  ten times, and Surah <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
 ten times. After
accomplishment the same aforementioned invocation is repeated one
hundred times.

The Twenty-Ninth Night Prayer: Two units with Surah <ref=1:1>al-Fatehah</ref>

and Surah <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
 twenty times.

The Thirtieth Night Prayer: Twelve units with Surah <ref=1:1>al-Fatehah</ref>

and Surah <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>
 twenty times. After accomplishment,
SWALAWAT is repeated one hundred times:
Note that each two units of all of the aforementioned prayers should
be separated by uttering the Taslim statement.

name: General Aamal for 3 Nights of laylatul qadr

text.en:
First: It is recommended to bathe oneself at each of the Qadr
Nights. According to ‘Allamah al-Majlisi, it is preferable to perform
the bathing at sunset so as to be ready for the Isha’ Prayer with a
bathing

It has been also narrated that when he was asked about the best
prayer that one may say at the Qadr Night, the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.a.)
said, “Pray for good health.”

Second: It is recommended to offer a two unit prayer reciting at
each unit Surah <ref=1:1>Al-Fatehah</ref>
 once and repeating Surah <ref=1:112>al-Ekhlas</ref>

seven times. After the accomplishment of the prayer, the following
imploration should be repeated seventy times:

translation.en: I implore the forgiveness of Allah and I repent to Him.

text.en:
According to a Hadith reported from the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.a.),
Almighty Allah will forgive one who offers this prayer and will
forgive his parents before he leaves his place.

Third: It is recommended to open a copy of the Holy Qur’an
between one’s hands and say the following:

segments.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ بِكِتَابِكَ الْمُنْزَلِ وَ مَا فِيهِ
segments.transliteration: Alaahumma inni asaloka be kitaabekal munzale Wa maa feeh,

translation.en: O Allah I beseech Thee in the name of Thy revealed Book and that which is in it,

segments.arabic: وَ فِيهِ اسْمُكَ الْأَكْبَرُ وَ أَسْمَاؤُكَ الْحُسْنَى وَ مَا يُخَافُ وَ يُرْجَى أَنْ تَجْعَلَنِي مِنْ عُتَقَائِكَ مِنَ النَّارِ
segments.transliteration: wa feehismokal akbaro wa asmaakal husnaa, wa maa yokhaafo Wa yurjaa an taj-alani min otaqaaeka minan-Naar

translation.en: and Thy Great Name and Thy (Other) beautiful names are in it, and in it there is that which warns and gives hope to include me among those who have been set free from the Hellfire.

text.en: After that one should pray to God for any needs of his.

Fourth: It is recommended to put a copy of the Holy Qur’an on the
head and say the following:

translation.en: O Allah: for the sake of this (Holy) Qur'an,

segments.transliteration: we be haqqe man arsaltahoo beh

translation.en: and for the sake of that person through whom You sent it,

segments.transliteration: Wa be haqqe kulle momenin madahtahoo feeh

translation.en: and for the sake of those believers who have been praised therein,
and in the name of Your obligation to them,

segments.transliteration: fa-laa ahada a'arafo be haqqeka mink

translation.en: no one is aware of Your obligation more than You

text.en: Recite the below 10 times each:

translation.en: For Your sake, O Allah,

text.en: ten times

translation.en: for the sake of Muhammad,

text.en: ten times

translation.en: for the sake of `Ali,

text.en: ten times

translation.en: for the sake of Fatimah,

text.en: ten times

translation.en: for the sake of Hasan,

text.en: ten times

translation.en: for the sake of Husayn,

text.en: ten times

translation.en: for the sake of `Ali ibn Husayn,

text.en: ten times

translation.en: for the sake of Muhammad ibn `Ali,

text.en: ten times

translation.en: for the sake of Ja`far ibn Muhammad,

text.en: ten times

translation.en: for the sake of Musa ibn Jafar,

text.en: ten times

translation.en: for the sake of `Ali ibn Musa,

text.en: ten times

translation.en: for the sake of Muhammad ibn `Ali,

text.en: ten times

translation.en: for the sake of `Ali ibn Muhammad,

text.en: ten times

translation.en: for the sake of Hasan ibn `Ali,

text.en: ten times

translation.en: for the sake of al-Hujjah.

text.en: One should then pray to Almighty Allah to fulfill one’s desires.

Fifth: It is advisable to visit the holy tomb of Imam al-Husayn (a.s.)
at these nights. A Hadith, in this respect, reads that at the Qadr Nights,
a caller from the heavens will call from the Seventh Sky and,
specifically, from the centre of the Holy Throne that Almighty Allah
shall forgive whoever visits the tomb of al-Husayn (a.s.)

Sixth: It is highly advisable to spend the whole three Qadr Nights
with acts of worship. A Hadith reads that one who spends the whole
Qadr Nights with acts of worship will have all his sins forgiven even
if they are as many as stars, as heavy as mountains, and as weighty as
seawater.

eventh: In order to win its remarkable reward, it is recommended
to offer the one hundred unit prayer. It is preferable to recite at these
units Surah al-Faatehah once and repeat Surah al-Tawheed ten times.
Eighth: Al-Kaf`amiy has narrated that Imam Zayn al-Abideen (a.s) used to recite this dua very frequently at the Qadr  nights while sitting, standing, in rukuu', and prostrating:

segments.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي امْسَيْتُ لَكَ عَبْداً دَاخِراً

translation.en: I have passed the evening as a very humble servant of Yours

segments.arabic: لاَ امْلِكُ لِنَفْسِي نَفْعاً وَلاَ ضَرّاً
segments.transliteration: la amliku linafsi naf`an wa la darran

translation.en: I have no control over the gains and losses for my self
and I cannot drive back any evil from myself.
I testify to this against myself.

segments.arabic: وَاعْتَرِفُ لَكَ بِضَعْفِ قُوَّتِي وَقِلَّةِ حِيلَتِي
segments.transliteration: wa a`tarifu laka bida`fi quwwati wa qillati hilati

translation.en: To You do I confess of my powerlessness and my feebleness.
So, (please do) send blessings to Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad
and fulfill what You have promised me,
as well as all the believing men and women;
that is Your forgiveness at this night,
and accomplish that which You have conferred upon me,
for I am Your slave—the poor, the humble,
the weak, the needy, and the humiliated.

segments.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ لاَ تَجْعَلْنِي نَاسِياً لِذِكْرِكَ فِيمَا اوْلَيْتَنِي
segments.transliteration: allahumma la taj`alni nasiyan lidhikrika fima awlaytani

translation.en: O Allah, (please) do not make me negligent of Your mention as regards that which You have bestowed upon me,

segments.transliteration: wa la ghafilan li'ihsanika fima a`taytani

translation.en: do not make me inadvertent to Your favors to me as regards that which You have given me,

segments.arabic: وَلاَ آيِساً مِنْ إِجَابَتِكَ وَإِنْ اَبْطَاتْ عَنِّي
segments.transliteration: wa la ayisan min ijabatika wa in abta'at `anni

translation.en: do not make me despair of Your responding to me, even if such response is postponed,
whether I be in prosperity, adversity,
hardship, comfort,
wellbeing, ordeal,
misery, or bliss.
Verily, You are the Hearer of prayers.

text.en:
Al-Kaf’ami has narrated that Imam Zayn al-’Abidin(a.s.) used to
say this supplicatory prayer very frequently at the Qadr Nights while
sitting, standing, genuflecting, and prostrating.

‘Allamah al-Majlisi has mentioned that the most favorable rite at
the Qadr Nights is to implore for Almighty Allah’s forgiveness, to
mention Him, to pray for the settlement of one’s worldly and religious
affairs as well as for one’s parents, relatives, and brethren-in-faith; the
dead and the alive, and to invoke blessings upon the Holy Prophet and
his Household.
According to some narrations, it is recommended to say the famous
supplicatory prayer known as Dua al-Jawshan al-Kabir, which has
been previously cited in this book, at the three Qadr Nights.

It has been also narrated that when he was asked about the best
prayer that one may say at the Qadr Night, the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.a.)
said, “Pray for good health.”

subcategory: Ramadan - Amal For Laylat ul Qadr

name: 19th Night of ramadan

text.en:
The nineteenth night of Ramadan is the first of the Qadr Nights. Of
course, the Qadr Night (laylat al-qadr) is unmatched in merits, since to
practice acts of worship at this night is equal to the acts of one
thousand nights. At this night, the affairs of the whole year are
decided and the angels and the Greatest Spirit are descended by the
permission of Almighty Allah so as to visit the Imam of the Age (i.e.
Imam al-Mahdi), have the honor of being present before him, and
provide the destinies of all the beings before him.

First: It is recommended to repeat the following imploration one
hundred times at the nineteenth night of Ramadan:

Second: It is recommended to repeat the following imprecatory
prayer one hundred times at the nineteenth night of Ramadan:

translation.en: O Allah: (please do) withdraw your mercy from the killers of the Commander of the Believers (Ameer al-Mu'mineen Imam Ali)

text.en:
Third: It is recommended to say the aforementioned supplicatory
prayer:

segments.arabic: يَا ذَا الّذِي كَانَ قَبْلَ كُلّ شَيْءٍ،
segments.transliteration: ya dhalladhy kana qabla kulli shay‘in

translation.en: O He Who has been always there before all things,
And He then created all things,

segments.transliteration: thumm yabqa wa yafna kullu shay‘in

translation.en: And He then stays while all things vanish.

segments.arabic: يَا ذَا الّذِي لَيْسَ كَمِثْلِهِ شَيْءٌ،

translation.en: O He Whose like in not found at all

segments.arabic: وَيَا ذَا الّذِي لَيْسَ فِي السّمَاوَاتِ العُلَى،
segments.transliteration: wa ya dhalladhy laysa fi alssamawati al`ula

translation.en: O He; neither in the highest heavens,
Nor in the lowest layers of the earth

segments.arabic: وَلا فَوْقَهُنّ وَلا تَحْتَهُنّ وَلا بَيْنَهُنّ إلهٌ يُعْبَدُ غَيْرُهُ،
segments.transliteration: wa la fawqahunna wa la tahtahunna wa la baynahunna ilhun yu`badu ghayruhu

translation.en: Nor above them, nor beneath them, nor there is between them any god that is worshipped save Him.

segments.arabic: لَكَ الحَمْدُ حَمْداً لا يَقْوَى عَلَى إحْصَائِهِ إلاَّ أَنْتَ،
segments.transliteration: laka alhamdu hamdan la yaqwa `ala ihsa’ihi illa anta

translation.en: To You be the praise that none can count save You,

segments.arabic: فَصَلّ عَلَى مُحَمّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمّدٍ صَلاةً لا يَقْوَى عَلَى إحْصَائِهَا إلاَّ أَنْتَ.
segments.transliteration: fasalli `ala muhammadin wa ali muhammadin salatan la yaqwa `ala ihsa’ihailla anta

translation.en: So, please bless Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad with the blessings that none can count save You.

text.en: Fourth: It is recommended to say the following supplicatory prayer:

segments.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اجْعَلْ فِيما تَقْضِي وَتُقَدِّرُ مِنَ الاَمْرِ المَحْتُومِ وَفِيما تَفْرُقُ مِنَ الاَمْرِ الحَكِيمِ فِي لَيْلَةِ القَدْرِ وَفِي القَضاء الَّذِي لا يُرَدُّ وَلا يُبَدَّلُ أَنْ تَكْتُبَنِي مِنْ حُجَّاجِ بَيْتِكَ الحَرامِ المَبْرُورِ حَجُّهُمُ المَشْكُورِ سَعْيُهُمُ ، المَغْفُورِ ذُنُوبُهُمُ ، المُكَفَّرِ عَنْهُم سَيِّئاتُهُمْ ، وَاجْعَلْ فِيْما تَقْضِي وَتُقَدِّرُ أَنْ تُطِيلَ عُمْرِي ، وَتُوَسِّعَ عَلَيَّ فِي رِزْقِي ، وَتَفْعَلَ بِي كَذا وَكَذا.

text.en: One may mention his desires after that.

subcategory: Ramadan - Amal For Laylat ul Qadr

name: 21st Night of ramadan

text.en:
The twenty-first night of Ramadan is more meritorious that the
nineteenth. It is worthwhile to practice the previously mentioned
general rites of the Qadr Nights, such as bathing, spending the whole
nights with acts of worship, visiting the tomb of Imam al-Husayn
(a.s.), offering the two unit prayer at which Surah al-Faatehah is
recited once and Surah al-Tawheed repeated seven times, putting a
copy of the Holy Qur’an on the head and saying the supplication
dedicated to this practice, saying the Dua al-Jawshan al-Kabir
supplicatory prayer, and so on. According to narrations, it is strongly
advisable to do the bathing and to spend the whole night with
painstaking acts of worship at this night as well as the twenty-third,
since the real Qadr Night is one of these two nights.
When the Holy Infallibles (a.s.) were asked to specify the Qadr
Night, they did not specify; rather they used to say, “It is too easy to
act at two nights in order to win one’s desire.” They also used to say,
“What will harm you if you do righteously at two nights?”
Dictating to his students, Shaykh al-Saduq said in one of his
sessions, “It is preferable to spend these two nights with studying the
religious knowledge.” Starting with this night, it is recommended to say the supplicatory
prayers that are dedicated to the last ten nights of Ramadan.

In al-Kafi, Shaykh al-Kulayni has reported Imam Ja’far al-
Sadiq(a.s.) as saying: At the last ten nights of Ramadan, you may say
the following supplicatory prayer:

segments.arabic: أَعُوذُ بِجَلالِ وَجْهِكَ الكَرِيمِ أَنْ يَنْقَضِيَ عَنِّي شَهْرُ رَمَضانَ أَوْ يَطْلُعَ الفَجْرُ مِنْ لَيْلَتِي هذِهِ وَلَكَ قِبَلِي ذَنْبٌ أَوْ تَبِعَةٌ تُعَذِّبُنِي عَلَيْهِ

text.en:
As a footnote in al-Balad al-Amin, al-Kaf’ami has mentioned
that Imam Ja’far al-Sadiq(a.s.) used say the following supplicatory
prayer at each of the last ten night of Ramadan after the obligatory and
supererogatory prayers:

segments.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَدِّ عَنّا حَقَّ ما مَضى مِنْ شَهْرِ رَمَضانَ وَاغْفِرْ لَنا تَقْصِيرَنا فِيْهِ ، وَتَسَلَّمْهُ مِنّا مَقْبُولا ، وَلا تُؤاخِذْنا بِإِسْرافِنا عَلى أَنْفُسِنا ، وَاجْعَلْنا مِنَ المَرْحُومِينَ وَلا تَجْعَلْنا مِنَ المَحْرُومِينَ.

text.en:
He adds that Almighty Allah will forgive one who says this
supplicatory prayer all the sins that he has committed in the past days
of Ramadan and will protect him against sinning in the rest of it.

In Iqbal al-A’mal, Sayyid Ibn Tawus has narrated on the
authority of Ibn Abi-’Umayr on the authority of Murazim that Imam
Ja’far al-Sadiq(a.s.) used to say the following supplicatory prayer at
each of the last ten nights of Ramadan:

segments.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّكَ قُلْتَ فِي كِتابِكَ المُنْزَلِ : (شَهْرُ رَمَضانَ الَّذِي اُنْزِلَ فِيْهِ القُرْآنَ هُدىً لِلْنَّاسِ وَبَيِّناتٍ مِنَ الهُدى وَالفُرْقانِ) فَعَظَّمْتَ حُرْمَةَ شَهْرِ رَمَضانَ بِما أَنْزَلْتَ فِيْهِ مِنَ القُرْآنِ وَخَصَصْتَهُ بِلَيْلَةِ القَدْرِ وَجَعَلْتَها خَيْراً مِنْ أَلْف شَهْرٍ. اللَّهُمَّ وَهذِهِ أَيامُ شَهْرِ رَمَضانَ قَدْ انْقَضَتْ وَلَيالِيهِ قَدْ تَصَرَّمَتْ ، وَقَدْ صِرْتُ يا إِلهِي مِنْهُ إِلى ماأَنْتَ أَعْلَمُ بِهِ مِنِّي وَأَحْصى لِعَدَدِهِ مِنَ الخَلْقِ أَجْمَعِينَ فأَسْأَلُكَ بما سَأَلَكَ بِهِ مَلائِكَتُكَ المُقَرَّبُونَ ، وَأَنْبِياؤُكَ المُرْسَلُونَ ، وَعِبادُكَ الصّالِحُونَ أَنْ تُصَلِّي عَلى مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ وَأَن تَفُكَّ رَقَبَتِي مِنَ النّارِ ، وَتُدْخِلَنِي الجَنَّةَ بِرَحْمَتِكَ ، وَأَنْ تَتَفَضَّلَ عَلَيَّ بِعَفْوِكَ وَكَرَمِكَ ، وَتَتَقَبَّلَ تَقَرُّبِي ، وَتَسْتَجِيبَ دُعائي ، وَتَمُنَّ عَلَيَّ بِالاَمْنِ يَوْمَ الخَوْفِ مِنْ كُلِّ هَوْلٍ أَعْدَدْتَهُ لِيَوْمِ القِيّامَةِ. إِلْهِي وَأَعُوذُ بِوَجْهِكَ الكَرِيمِ ، وَبِجَلالِكَ العَظِيمِ أَنْ يَنْقَضِيَ أَيّامُ شَهْرِ رَمَضانَ وَليالِيهِ وَلَكَ قِبَلِي تَبِعَةٌ أوْ ذَنْبٌ تُؤأَخِذُنِي بِهِ ، أوْ خَطِيئَةٌ تُرِيدُ أَنْ تَقْتَصَّها مِنِّي لَمْ تَغْفِرْها لِي سَيِّدِي سَيِّدِي سَيِّدِي أَسْأَلُكَ يا لا إِلهَ إِلاّ أَنْتَ إِذْ لا إِلهَ إِلاّ أَنْتَ إِنْ كُنْتَ رَضِيْتَ عَنِّي فِي هذا الشَّهْرِ فَازْدَدْ عَنِّي رِضىً ، وَإِنْ لَمْ تَكُنْ رَضِيتَ عَنِّي فَمَنْ الانَ فَارْضَ عَنِّي يا أَرْحَمَ الرَّاحِمِينَ ، يا الله يا أَحَدُ يا صَمَدُ يا مَنْ لَمْ يَلِدْ وَلَمْ يُولَدْ وَلَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ كُفْوا أَحَدٌ.

text.en:
You may repeat the following supplicatory prayer as many
times as possible during this night:

segments.arabic: يا مُلَيِّنَ الحَدِيدِ لِداوُدَ عليه‌السلام ، يا كاشِفَ الضُّرِّ وَالكُرَبِ العِظامِ عَنْ أَيّوبَ عليه‌السلام ، أَي مُفَرِّجَ هَمِّ يَعْقُوبَ عليه‌السلام ، أَيْ مُنَفِّسَ غَمِّ يُوسُفَ عليه‌السلام ، صَلِّ عَلى مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ كَما أَنْتَ أَهْلُهُ أَنْ تُصَلِّيَ عَلَيْهِمْ أَجْمَعِينَ ، وَافْعَلْ بِي ما أَنْتَ أَهْلُهُ ، وَلا تَفْعَلْ بِي ما أَنا أَهْلُهُ

text.en:
Shaykh al-Kulayni, in al-Kafi, has narrated, with a complete
chain of authority, that the following supplicatory prayer, which is
also narrated in al-Muqni’ah and al-Misbah yet with an interruption in
the chain of authority, should be said at the twenty-first night of
Ramadan:

translation.en: O He Who causes the night to enter into the day
And causes the day to enter into the night,
And brings forth the living from the dead
And brings forth the dead from the living,

segments.arabic: يَا رَازِقَ مَنْ يَشَاءُ بِغَيْرِ حِسَابٍ،
segments.transliteration: ya raziqa man yasha‘u bighayri hisabin

translation.en: And He Who gives sustenance to whomever He please without measure:
O Allah; O the All-beneficent;
O Allah; O the All-merciful;

segments.arabic: يَا اللّهُ يَا اللّهُ يَا اللّهُ
segments.transliteration: ya allahu ya allahu ya allahu

translation.en: O Allah; O Allah; O Allah;
To You are the Most Excellent Names,
And the most elevated examples,
And greatness and bounties.

segments.arabic: أَسْأَلُكَ أَنْ تُصَلّيَ عَلَى مُحَمّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمّدٍ،
segments.transliteration: as’aluka an tusalliya `ala muhammadin wa ali muhammadin

translation.en: I beseech You to bless Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad,

segments.arabic: وَأَنْ تَجْعَلَ اسْمِي فِي هذِهِ اللّيْلَةِ فِي السّعَدَاءِ،
segments.transliteration: wa an taj`ala asmy fi hadhihi allaylati fi alssu`ada‘i

translation.en: And to include my name with the list of the happiest ones,
And to add my soul to the martyrs,
And record my good deeds in the most exalted rank
And to decide my offense to be forgiven,

segments.arabic: وَأَنْ تَهَبَ لِي يَقِيناً تُبَاشِرُ بِهِ قَلْبِي،
segments.transliteration: wa an tahaba li yaqinan tubashiru bihi qalbi

translation.en: And to grant me certitude that fills in my heart
And faith that removes dubiosity from me
And to make me feel satisfied with that which You decide for me
And (please) grant us reward in this world
And reward in the Hereafter,
And save us from the torment of the burning Fire,

segments.arabic: وَارْزُقْنِي فِيهَا ذِكْرَكَ وَشُكْرَكَ وَالرّغْبَةَ إِلَيْكَ،
segments.transliteration: warzuqny fiha dhikraka wa shukraka wal-rraghbata ilayka

translation.en: And (also) confer upon us at this night Your mentioning, thanking You, and desiring for You,

segments.arabic: وَالإِنَابَةَ وَالتّوْفِيقَ لِمَا وَفّقْتَ لَهُ مُحَمّداً وَآلَ مُحَمّدٍ عَلَيْهِ وَعَلَيْهِمُ السّلامُ.
segments.transliteration: wal-inabata wal-ttawfiqa lima waffaqta lahu muhammadan wa ala muhammadin `alayhi wa `alayhimu alssalamu

translation.en: And turning to You, and success to that to which You led Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad, peace be upon him and them.

text.en:
Recite the following dua on the 21st.
Dua for 21 Ramadhan

segments.arabic: اللّهُمّ صَلّ عَلَى مُحَمّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمّدٍ
segments.transliteration: allahumma salli `ala muhammadin wa ali muhammadin

translation.en: O Allah: (please) bless Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad,

segments.arabic: وَاقْسِمْ لِي حِلْماً يَسُدّ عَنّي بَابَ الجَهْلِ،
segments.transliteration: waqsim li hilman yasuddu `anni baba aljahli

translation.en: And decide for me forbearance that blocks the door to ignorance against me,

segments.arabic: وَهُدَىً تَمُنّ بِهِ عَلَيّ مِنْ كُلّ ضَلالَةٍ،
segments.transliteration: wa hudan tamunn bihi `alayya min kulli dalalatin

translation.en: And guidance that You confer upon me against any item of straying off,

segments.arabic: وَغِنَىً تَسُدّ بِهِ عَنّي بَابَ كُلّ فَقْرٍ،
segments.transliteration: wa ghinan tasudd bihi `anni baba kull faqrin

translation.en: And affluence that blocks the door to all poverty against me,

segments.arabic: وَقُوّةً تَرُدّ بِهَا عَنّي كُلّ ضَعْفٍ،
segments.transliteration: wa quwwatan tarudd biha `anni kulla da`fin

translation.en: And power due to which You repel any weakness from me,

segments.arabic: وَعِزّاً تُكْرِمُنِي بِهِ عَنْ كُلّ ذُلّ،

translation.en: And might with which You honor me against all humiliation,

segments.arabic: وَرِفْعَةً تَرْفَعُنِي بِهَا عَنْ كُلّ ضَعَةٍ،
segments.transliteration: wa rif`atan tarfa`uny biha `an kulli da`atin

translation.en: And sublimity through which You exalt me against all humbleness,

segments.arabic: وَأَمْناً تَرُدّ بِهِ عَنّي كُلّ خَوْفٍ،
segments.transliteration: wa amnan tarudd bihi `anni kulla khawfin

translation.en: And security through which You protect me against any fear,

segments.arabic: وَعَافِيَةً تَسْتُرُنِي بِهَا عَنْ كُلّ بَلاءٍ،

translation.en: And well-being by which You cover me against any ailment,

segments.arabic: وَعِلْماً تَفْتَحُ لِي بِهِ كُلّ يَقِينٍ،
segments.transliteration: wa `ilman taftahu li bihi kulla yaqinin

translation.en: And knowledge due to which You open before me all certitude,

segments.arabic: وَيَقِيناً تُذْهِبُ بِهِ عَنّي كُلّ شَكّ،
segments.transliteration: wa yaqinan tudhhibu bihi `anni kulla shakkin

translation.en: And certitude due to which You remove from me any dubiosity,

segments.arabic: وَدُعَاءً تَبْسُطُ لِي بِهِ الإِجَابَةَ فِي هذِهِ اللَيْلَةِ وَفِي هذِهِ السَّاعَةِ،
segments.transliteration: wa du`a‘an tabsutu li bihi al-ijabata fi hadhihi allaylati wa fi hadhihi alssa`ati

translation.en: And prayer through which You expand Your response to me at this very night and at this very hour,
This very hour, this very hour, this very hour, O the All-generous,

segments.arabic: وَخَوْفاً تَنْشُرُ لِي بِهِ كُلّ رَحْمَةٍ،
segments.transliteration: wa khawfan tanshuru li bihi kull rahmatin

translation.en: And apprehension due to which You spread over me all items of mercy,

segments.arabic: وَعِصْمَةً تَحُولُ بِهَا بَيْنِي وَبيْنَ الذّنُوبِ
segments.transliteration: wa `ismatan tahulu biha bayny wa bina aldhdhunubi

translation.en: And shelter due to which You intervene between my sins and me,

segments.arabic: حَتَّى أُفْلِحَ بِهَا عِنْدَ المَعْصومِينَ عِنْدَكَ

translation.en: So that I shall succeed in the view of the Infallible ones with You,
Out of Your mercy, O the most Merciful of all those who show mercy.

text.en: Holy Prophet(saws) Dua for 21st Night of Ramadan
Sayyed ibn Tawus narrates in his book Iqbal Amaal the following supplication for the 21st Night of the Month of Ramadhan from the Holy Prophet:

translation.en: In the Name of Allah, the Most Beneficent, the Most Merciful

segments.arabic: أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لاَ إلَهَ إِلاَّ اللهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ
segments.transliteration: ash-hadu an laa ilaaha illallahu wah’dahu laa shareeka lahu

translation.en: I bear witness that there is no God but God. He is One and there are no partners for Him

segments.transliteration: wa ash-hadu anna muhammadan a’bduhu wa rasooluhu

translation.en: I bear witness that Muhammad is His servant and Prophet

segments.arabic: وَأَشْهَدُ أَنَّ الْجَنَّةَ حَقٌّ وَالنَّارَ حَقٌّ

translation.en: I bear witness that Paradise is true and Hell is true

segments.arabic: وَأَنَّ السَّاعَةَ آتِيَةٌ لاَ رَيْبَ فِيهَا
segments.transliteration: wa annas-sa’ata aatiyatun laa rayba feeha

translation.en: And I bear witness that the Hour is sure to come and there is no doubt about it

segments.arabic: وَأَنَّ اللهُ يَبْعَثُ مَنْ فِي الْقُبُورِ
segments.transliteration: wa annallahu yab’athu man fil quboor

translation.en: and that God shall resurrect the dead from the graves

segments.arabic: وَأَشْهَدُ أَنَّ الرَّبَّ رَبِّي لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ وَلاَ وَلَدَ لَهُ وَلاَ وَالِدَ لَهُ
segments.transliteration: wa ash-hadu annar-rabba rabbi laa shareeka lahu wa la walada lahu wa la waalida lahu

translation.en: I bear witness that the Lord is my Lord. There are no partners for Him. He has not been born and nor shall He have a child.

segments.arabic: وَأَشْهَدُ أَنَّهُ الْفَعَّالُ لِمَا يُرِيدُ وَالْقَادِرُ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ
segments.transliteration: wa ash-hadu annahul fa’aalu lima yureedu wal qaadiru ‘alaa kulli shayin

translation.en: I bear witness that He does whatever He Wills. He has Power over everything

segments.arabic: وَالصَّانِعُ لِمَا يُريدُ وَالْقَاهِرُ مَنْ يَشَاءُ وَالرَّافِعُ مَنْ يَشَاءُ
segments.transliteration: was-‘sani’u lima yureedu wal qahiru man yasha-u war-rafi’u man yasha-u

translation.en: He can create whatever He wills. He is the Subduer who can do what He wills! The Exalter of whom He pleases!

segments.arabic: مَالِكُ الْمُلْكِ وَرَازِقُ الْعِبَادِ الْغَفُورُ الرَّحِيمُ الْعَلِيمُ الْحَلِيمُ

translation.en: Lord of Power (and Rule)! Provider of Sustenance for the people! All-Forgiving, Merciful! The Knowledgeable! The Forbearing!

segments.arabic: أَشْهَدُ أَشْهَدُ أَشْهَدُ أَشْهَدُ أَشْهَدُ أَشْهَدُ أَنَّكَ سَيْدِي كَذلِكَ وَفَوْقَ ذلِكَ لاَ يَبْلُغُ الْوَاصِفُونَ كُنْهَ عَظَمَتِكَ
segments.transliteration: ash-hadu ash-hadu ash-hadu ash-hadu ash-hadu ash-hadu annaka la yablighu sayyidi kadhalika wa fawqa dhalikal wasifoona kunha ‘adhamatik

translation.en: I bear witness, I bear witness, I bear witness, I bear witness, I bear witness, I bear witness that You-my Master-are so and much more and You are He whose depth of Grandeur those who extol cannot reach
O God, Please send your blessings on Muhammad and His Household

segments.arabic: وَاهْدِنِي وَلاَ تُضِلَّنِي بَعْدَ إِذْ هَدَيْتَنِي
segments.transliteration: wahdini wa la tudhillani b’ada idh hadaytani

translation.en: Please guide me and do not let me be deviated after having guided me.
For surely, You are the Guide.

text.en: Dua 4 after Fajr Salah on 21st Ramadan "la ilaha illa anta muqalliba alqulub"
This supplication has been mentioned by Sayyed ibn Tawus in his book of Iqbal al-Amaal from Imam Ja’far Sadiq who recited in Sijdah (Prostration):

segments.arabic: لَا إِلٰهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ مُقَلِّبَ الْقُلوبِ وَالْأَبْصارِ،
segments.transliteration: la ilaha illa anta muqalliba alqulubi wa alabsari,

translation.en: There is no god save You, the Transformer of hearts and sights

segments.arabic: لَا إِلٰهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ خالِقَ الْخَلقِ
segments.transliteration: la ilaha illa anta khaliqa alkhalqi

translation.en: There is no god save You, the Creator of the creatures,
While You do not need them.

segments.transliteration: la ilaha illa anta mubdiia alkhalqi

translation.en: There is no god save You, the Originator of the creation.

segments.transliteration: wa la yanqusu min mulkika shay‘un,

translation.en: And nothing of creation decreases any of Your Kingdom.

segments.arabic: لَا إِلٰهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ بَاعِثَ مَنْ فِي الْقُبورِ،
segments.transliteration: la ilaha illa anta ba`itha man fy alquburi,

translation.en: There is no god save You, the Resurrector of those who are in graves.

segments.arabic: لَا إِلٰهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ مُدَبِّرَ الْأُمورِ،
segments.transliteration: la ilaha illa anta mudabbira alaumuri,

translation.en: There is no god save You, the Manager of all affairs.

segments.arabic: لَا إِلٰهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ دَيَّانَ وَجَبَّارَ الْجَبابِرَةِ.
segments.transliteration: la ilaha illa anta dayyana wa jabbara aljababirati.

translation.en: There is no god save You, the Master and the most Omnipotent of all those who claim omnipotence.

segments.arabic: لَا إِلٰهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ مُجْرِيَ الْمَاءِ في الصَّخْرَةِ الصَّمَّاءِ
segments.transliteration: la ilaha illa anta mujriya alma‘i fy alssakhrati alssamma‘i,

translation.en: There is no god save You. It is You Who causes water to flow in hard rocks.

segments.arabic: لَا إِلٰهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ مُجْرِيَ الْمَاءِ في النَّباتِ،
segments.transliteration: la ilaha illa anta mujriya alma‘i fy alnnabati,

translation.en: There is no god save You. It is You Who causes water to flow in plants.

segments.arabic: لَا إِلٰهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ مُكَوِّنَ طَعْمِ الثِّمارِ،
segments.transliteration: la ilaha illa anta mukuuina ta`mi alththimari,

translation.en: There is no god save You, the Maker of the flavors of fruits.

segments.arabic: لَا إِلٰهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ مُحْصِيَ عَدَدِ الْقَطْرِ وَما تَحْمِلُهُ السَّحابُ،
segments.transliteration: la ilaha illa anta muhsiya `adadi alqatri wa ma tahmiluhu alssahabu,

translation.en: There is no god save You. It is You Who keeps count of the number of the drops of rain and what is loaded by the clouds.

segments.arabic: لَا إِلٰهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ مُحْصِيَ عَدَدِ ما تَجْري بِهِ الرِّياحُ في الْهَواءِ،
segments.transliteration: la ilaha illa anta muhsiya `adadi ma tajry bihi alrriiahu fy alhawa‘i,

translation.en: There is no god save You. It is You Who keeps count of the number of what the wind carry in the air.

segments.arabic: لَا إِلٰهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ مُحْصِيَ مَا فِي الْبِحَارِ مِنْ رَطْبٍ وَيابِسٍ،
segments.transliteration: la ilaha illa anta muhsiya ma fiy albihari min ratbin wa yabisin,

translation.en: There is no god save You. It is You Who keeps count of what is there in oceans—the wet and the dry.

segments.arabic: لَا إِلٰهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ مُحْصِيَ ما يَدُبُّ في ظُلُمَاتِ الْبِحَارِ وَفِي أَطْباقِ الثَّرى.
segments.transliteration: la ilaha illa anta muhsiya ma yadubbu fy zulumati albihari wa fy atbaqi alththari.

translation.en: There is no god save You. It is You Who keeps count of whatever creeps in the darknesses of oceans and in the layers of the earth.

segments.arabic: أَسْأَلُكَ بِاسْمِكَ الَّذي سَمَّيْتَ بِهِ نَفْسَكَ،
segments.transliteration: as-aluka biasmika alladhy sammayta bihi nafsaka,

translation.en: I beseech You by Your Name that You have used for Yourself

segments.arabic: أَوِ اسْتَأْثَرْتَ بِهِ عَلٰى عِلْمِ الْغَيْبِ عِنْدَكَ،

translation.en: Or You have stored for Yourself in the hidden knowledge of You.

segments.arabic: وَأَسْأَلُكَ بِكُلِّ اسْمٍ سَمَّاكَ بِهِ أَحَدٌ مِنْ خَلْقِكَ،
segments.transliteration: wa as-aluka bikulli asmin sammaka bihi ahadun min khalqika,

translation.en: And I beseech You by every name that is ascribed to You by any of Your created beings:

segments.arabic: مِنْ نَبِيٍّ أَوْ صِدّيقٍ أَوْ شَهيدٍ أَوْ أَحَدٍ مِنْ مَلَائِكَتِكَ،
segments.transliteration: min nabiiiin aw siddiqin aw shahidin aw ahadin min malaiikatika,

translation.en: Such as the Prophets, the veracious ones, the martyrs, or any of Your angels.

segments.arabic: وَأَسْأَلُكَ بِاسْمِكَ الَّذي إِذا دُعِيْتَ بِهِ أَجَبْتَ،
segments.transliteration: wa as-aluka biasmika alladhy idha du`ita bihi ajabta,

translation.en: And I beseech You by Your Name that You answer anyone who beseeches You by it,
And You respond to anyone who asks You by it.

segments.arabic: وَأَسْأَلُكَ بِحَقِّكَ عَلى مُحَمَّدٍ وَأَهْلِ بَيْتِهِ صَلَواتُكَ عَلَيْهِمْ وَبَرَكاتُكَ،
segments.transliteration: wa as-aluka bihaqqika `ala muhammadin wa ahli baytihi salawatuka `alayhim wa barakatuka,

translation.en: And I beseech You in the name of the duty that is incumbent upon Muhammad and his Household—Your blessings and benedictions be upon them—toward You,
And by the duty that You have made incumbent upon Yourself toward them,
And through which You granted them Your favors,

segments.arabic: أَنْ تُصَلِّيَ عَلى مُحَمَّدٍ عَبْدِكَ وَرَسُولِكَ

translation.en: (please) bless Muhammad, Your servant and Your Messenger:
The caller to You by Your permission,
And Your incandescent lantern from among Your servants
In Your lands and Your heavens,
And he whom You made mercy for the peoples,
And light through whom the believers have obtained their light;
He this conveyed to us the good tidings about Your abundant reward
And also warned us against Your painful chastisement.

segments.arabic: أَشْهَدُ أَنَّهُ قَد جَاءَ بِالْحَقِّ مِنْ عِنْدِ الْحَقِّ وَصَدَّقَ الْمُرْسَلينَ،
segments.transliteration: ashhadu annahu qad ja‘a bialhaqqi min `indi alhaqqi wa saddaqa almursalina,

translation.en: I bear witness that he carried to us the truth from the True God, and he verified the Messengers.

segments.arabic: وَأَشْهَدُ أَنَّ الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوهُ ذَائِقُو الْعَذَابِ الْأَلِيمِ.
segments.transliteration: wa ashhadu anna alladhina kadhdhabuhu dhaiiqw al`adhabi alalimi.

translation.en: And I bear witness that those who belied him shall certainly taste the painful chastisement.

segments.arabic: أَسْألُكَ يا اللهُ يا اَللهُ يا اَللهُ،
segments.transliteration: as-aluka ya allahu ya allahu ya allahu,

translation.en: I beseech You, O Allah; O Allah; O Allah;

segments.arabic: يا رَبَّاهُ يا رَبَّاهُ يا رَبَّاهُ،
segments.transliteration: ya rabbahu ya rabbahu ya rabbahu,

translation.en: O our God; O our God; O our God;

segments.arabic: يا سَيِّدي يا سَيِّدي يا سَيِّدي،
segments.transliteration: ya sayyidy ya sayyidy ya sayyidi,

translation.en: O my Master; O my Master; O my Master;

segments.arabic: يَا مَوْلَايَ يَا مَوْلَايَ يَا مَوْلَايَ،
segments.transliteration: ya mawlaia ya mawlaia ya mawlaia,

translation.en: O my Lord; O my Lord; O my Lord.

segments.arabic: أَسْألُكَ فَي هَذِهِ الْغَداةِ أَنْ تُصَلِّيَ عَلى مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ
segments.transliteration: as-aluka fy hadhihi alghadaati an tusallia `ala muhammadin wa ali muhammadin

translation.en: I beseech You in this early morning to bless Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad,

segments.arabic: وَأَنْ تَجْعَلَني مِنْ أَوْفَرِ عِبادِكَ وَسَائِلِيْكَ نَصِيْباً،
segments.transliteration: wa an taj`alany min awfari `ibadika wa saiilika nasibaan,

translation.en: And to include me with Your servants and beseechers upon whom You confer the most abundant of Your rewards,

segments.arabic: وَأَنْ تَمُنَّ عَلَيَّ بِفَكَاكِ رَقَبَتي مِنَ النَّارِ،
segments.transliteration: wa an tamunna `alayya bifakaki raqabaty mina alnnari,

translation.en: And to confer upon me with releasing me from Hellfire,
O the most Merciful of all those who show mercy.

segments.arabic: وَأَسْأَلُكَ بِجَمِيْعِ ما سَأَلْتُكَ وَمَا لَمْ أَسْأَلْكَ مِنْ عَظِيْمِ جَلَالِكَ
segments.transliteration: wa as-aluka bijami`i ma saaltuka wa ma lam asalka min `azimi jalalika,

translation.en: And I beseech You to settle for me all that which I asked from You and also all that which I did not ask from You; that which belongs to Your splendid majesty,
That which I would ask from You if I knew it,

segments.arabic: أَنْ تُصَلِّيَ عَلى مُحَمَّدٍ وَأَهْلِ بَيْتِهِ،
segments.transliteration: an tusalliya `ala muhammadin wa ahli baytihi,

translation.en: (and I beseech You to) send blessings upon Muhammad and his Household,

segments.arabic: وَأَنْ تَأْذَنَ لِفَرَجِ مَنْ بِفَرَجِهِ فَرَجُ أَوْلِيائِكَ وَأَصْفِيائِكَ مِنْ خَلْقِكَ،
segments.transliteration: wa an tadhana lifaraji man bifarajihi faraju awliya’ika wasfiiaiika min khalqika,

translation.en: And to permit the Relief of him whose relief achieves the relief of Your intimate servants and Your select ones from among Your created beings,

segments.transliteration: wa bihi tubidu alzzalimina wa tuhlikuhum,

translation.en: And through whom You will terminate and eradicate the oppressors.
(Please) hasten that, O the Lord of the worlds.

segments.arabic: وَأَعْطِنِي سُؤْلِي يَا ذَا ٱلْجَلَالِ وَالْإِكْرامِ
segments.transliteration: wa a`tiny su´ly ya dhaljalali walikrami

translation.en: And (please) grant me my requests, O the Lord of majesty and honor,

segments.arabic: في جَميعِ ما أَسَأَلْتُكَ لِعاجِلِ الدُّنْيا وَآجِلِ الْآخِرَةِ.
segments.transliteration: fy jami`i ma saltuka li`ajili alddunia wa ajili alakhirati.

translation.en: As regarding all that which I have asked You from my needs for this worldly life and the Hereafter.

segments.arabic: يَا مَنْ هُوَ أَقْرَبُ إِلَيَّ مِنْ حَبْلِ الْوَرِيدِ،
segments.transliteration: ya man huwa aqrabu ilayya min habli aluariidi,

translation.en: O He Who is nearer to me than my life-vein:
(Please) overlook my slips,
And revive me through settling my needs.

segments.arabic: يَا خَالِقِي وَيَا رَازِقي وَيَا بَاعِثِي،
segments.transliteration: ya khaliqy wa ya raziqy wa ya ba`ithi,

translation.en: O my Creator; O the Source of my sustenance; O my Resurrector;

segments.transliteration: wa ya muhiy `izamy wa hiya ramimun,

translation.en: O He Who shall give life to my bones when they are rotten:

segments.arabic: صَلِّ عَلى مُحَمَّدٍ وَ آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ

translation.en: (Please do) bless Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad,

segments.arabic: وَ ٱسْتَجِبْ لِي دُعَائِي يَا أَرْحَمَ الرّاحِمينَ.
segments.transliteration: wastajib ly du`aiy ya arhama alrrahimina.

translation.en: And respond to my prayer; O the most Merciful of all those who show mercy.

subcategory: Ramadan - Amal For Laylat ul Qadr

name: 23rd Night of ramadan

text.en:
The twenty-third night of Ramadan, the last of the Qadr Nights, is
more meritorious than the two previous ones; namely, the nineteenth
and the twenty-first. Many narrations confirm that it is the Qadr Night
at which all wise affairs are decided. Apart from the general acts that
are advisably practiced at the three Qadr Nights, there are other acts to
be practiced at the twenty-third night of Ramadan:
Perform Common Amaal of Laylatul Qadr

translation.en: O Lord of the Grand Night

text.en:
First: It is recommended to recite Surahs <ref=1:29>al-’Ankabut</ref>
 and
<ref=1:30>al-Rum</ref>
. Imam al-Sadiq(a.s.) is reported to have said,
“Whoever recites these two Surahs at this night, will be included with
the people of Paradise.”

segments.transliteration: wa ja`ilaha khayran min alfi shahrin

translation.en: Who decided it to be better than one thousand months!

text.en: Second: It is recommended to recite Surah <ref=1:44>al-Dukhan</ref>

translation.en: O Lord of night, day,

text.en:
Third: It is recommended to repeat Surah <ref=1:97>al-Qadr</ref>one thousand
times.

translation.en: mountains, oceans,

text.en:
Fourth: It is highly advisable to repeat the following famous
supplicatory prayer as many times as possible at this night:

translation.en: darkness, light,

segments.arabic: اللّهُمّ كُنْ لِوَلِيّكَ الحُجّةِ ابْنِ الحَسَنِ

translation.en: O Allah, be, for Your representative, the Hujjat (proof), son of Al­Hassan,
the earth, and the heavens!
Your blessings be on him and his forefathers,
O Maker! O Fashioner!

segments.arabic: فِي هذِهِ السَّاعَةِ وَفي كُلّ سَاعَةٍ
segments.transliteration: fee haazehis saa-a'te wa fee kulle saa-a'tin

translation.en: in this hour and in every hour,
O All-tender! O All-favorer!
a guardian, a protector,
O Allah! O All-beneficent!
a leader, a helper,
O Allah! O Self-Subsisting!
a proof, and an eye.
O Allah! O wonderful Originator!
until You make him live on the earth, in obedience (to You),

segments.arabic: يَا اللَّهُ يَا اللَّهُ يَا اللَّهُ
segments.transliteration: ya allahu ya allahu ya allahu

translation.en: O Allah! O Allah! O Allah!
& Cause him to live in it for a long time
To You are the Most Excellent Names,

text.en: Fifth: It is recommended to say the following supplicatory prayer:

translation.en: the most elevated examples,
O Allah, (please do) prolong my lifetime,
greatness, and bounties.
expand my sustenance,

segments.arabic: اسْالُكَ انْ تُصَلِّيَ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ
segments.transliteration: as'aluka an tusalliya `ala muhammadin wa ali muhammadin

translation.en: I beseech You to bless Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad,
make my body healthy,

segments.arabic: وَانْ تَجْعَلَ ٱسْمِي فِي هٰذِهِ ٱللَّيْلَةِ فِي ٱلسُّعَدَاءِ
segments.transliteration: wa an taj`ala ismi fi hadhihi allaylati fi alssu`ada'i

translation.en: include my name with the list of the happiest ones,
make me attain my hopes,
add my soul to the martyrs,

segments.arabic: وَإِنْ كُنْتُ مِنَ ٱلاشْقِيَاءِ فَٱمْحُنِي مِنَ ٱلاشْقِيَاءِ
segments.transliteration: wa in kuntu min al-ashqya'i famhuni min al-ashqya'i

translation.en: and if I am written with the unhappy ones, (please) erase my name from this list
record my good deeds in the most exalted rank,
write it with the happy ones,
decide my offense to be forgiven,
for You have said in Your Book that You revealed

segments.arabic: وَانْ تَهَبَ لِي يَقِيناً تُبَاشِرُ بِهِ قَلْبِي
segments.transliteration: wa an tahaba li yaqinan tubashiru bihi qalbi

translation.en: grant me certitude that fills in my heart

segments.arabic: عَلَىٰ نَبِيِّكَ ٱلْمُرْسَلِ صَلَوَاتُكَ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ:

translation.en: to Your missioned Prophet, Your peace be upon him and his Household:
and faith that removes doubt from me,

segments.arabic: ”يَمْحُو ٱللَّهُ مَا يَشَاءُ وَيُثْبِتُ وَعِنْدَهُ امُّ ٱلْكِتَابِ“
segments.transliteration: yamhu allahu ma yasha'u wa yuthbitu wa `indahu ummu alkitabi

translation.en: “Allah makes to pass away and establishes what He pleases, and with Him is the basis of the Book.”
make me feel satisfied with that which You decide for me,

text.en: Sixth: It is recommended to say the following supplicatory prayer:

translation.en: grant us reward in this world

segments.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ ٱجْعَلْ فِي مَا تَقْضِي وَفِي مَا تُقَدِّرُ
segments.transliteration: allahumma ij`al fima taqdi wa fima tuqaddiru

translation.en: O Allah, among the matters that You decide and make
and reward in the Hereafter,
from among the inevitable affairs
save us from the torment of the burning Fire,

segments.arabic: وَفِي مَا تَفْرُقُ مِنَ ٱلامْرِ ٱلْحَكِيمِ
segments.transliteration: wa fima tafruqu min al-amri alhakimi

translation.en: and from among the wise matters that You make distinct

segments.arabic: وَٱرْزُقْنِي فِيهَا ذِكْرَكَ وَشُكْرَكَ وَٱلرَّغْبَةَ إِلَيْكَ
segments.transliteration: warzuqni fiha dhikraka wa shukraka walrraghbata ilayka

translation.en: and confer upon us at this night Your mentioning, thanking You, desiring for You,
at the Destiny Night

segments.arabic: وَٱلإِنَابَةَ وَٱلتَّوْفِيقَ لِمَا وَفَّقْتَ لَهُ مُحَمَّداً وَآلَ مُحَمَّدٍ عَلَيْهِ وَعَلَيْهِمُ ٱلسَّلاَمُ
segments.transliteration: wal-inabata walttawfiqa lima waffaqta lahu muhammadan wa ala muhammadin `alayhi wa `alayhimu alssalamu

translation.en: turning to You, and success to that to which You led Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad, peace be upon him and them.

segments.arabic: مِنَ ٱلْقَضَاءِ ٱلَّذِي لاَ يُرَدُّ وَلاَ يُبَدَّلُ
segments.transliteration: min alqada'i alladhi la yuraddu wa la yubaddalu

translation.en: from among the act that is neither rejected nor altered,

segments.arabic: انْ تَكْتُبَنِي مِنْ حُجَّاجِ بَيْتِكَ ٱلْحَرَامِ
segments.transliteration: an taktubani min hujjaji baytika alharami

translation.en: (please) decide me to be with the pilgrims to Your Holy House
at this year;
those whose pilgrimage is admitted,
whose efforts are praised,
whose sins are forgiven,
and whose offenses and pardoned.
Among the matters that You decide and make,
(please) prolong my lifetime
and expand my sustenance.

text.en:
Seventh: It is recommended to say the following supplicatory
prayer that is mentioned in the book of Iqbal al-A’mal:

translation.en: O He Who is intrinsic though He is evident!
O He Who is evident though He is intrinsic!
O He Who is intrinsic that is not missed!
O He Who is evident but He cannot be seen!

segments.arabic: يَا مَوْصُوفاً لاَ يَبْلُغُ بِكَيْنُونَتِهِ مَوْصُوفٌ
segments.transliteration: ya mawsufan la yablughu bikaynunatihi mawsufun

translation.en: O He Who is described, but neither any other described thing
nor can any boundary attain His Being!
O He Who is absent but not lost!
O He Who is present but not witnessed!
When He is sought, He shall be obtained,

segments.arabic: وَلَمْ يَخْلُ مِنْهُ ٱلسَّمَاوَاتُ وَٱلارْضُ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا طَرْفَةَ عَيْنٍ
segments.transliteration: wa lam yakhlu minhu alssamawatu wal-ardu wa ma baynahuma tarfata `aynin

translation.en: and neither the heavens, nor the earth, nor can anything that is between them be empty of Him.
He cannot be realized by asking ‘How is He?’

segments.transliteration: wa la yu'ayyanu bi-aynin wa la bihaythin

translation.en: His place cannot be identified by asking, ‘Where is He?’ or ‘What is His place?’
You are verily the light of all lights
and the Lord of all lords.
You have comprehended all things.
Glory be to Him Whose like is nonexistent,
and He is the All-hearing, the All-seeing.

segments.arabic: سبحَانَ مَنْ هُوَ هٰكَذَا وَلاَ هٰكَذَا غَيْرُهُ
segments.transliteration: subhana man huwa hakadha wa la hakadha ghayruhu

translation.en: Glory be to Him Who is in this state and nothing else can be ever in his state.

text.en: Then, one may pray for settling his needs.

Eighth: It is recommended to do a new bathing, other than the first
one, at the last hours of night. In fact, to bathe oneself, to spend the
whole night with acts of worship, to visit the holy tomb of Imam al-
Husayn (a.s.), and to offer the one hundred unit prayer at this night -
all these deeds have been confirmed by narrations to be done at the
twenty-third night of Ramadan.

In Tahdhib al-Ahkam, Shaykh al-Tusi has narrated that Imam Ja’far
al-Sadiq(a.s.) instructed Abu-Basir to offer at the night that is
